Summary: Incorrect code generated for the Abs block
Description: Code generated for an Abs block might be invalid if both of the following conditions exist: The data type of the block input is a signed integer or signed fixed-point type. The Saturate on integer overflow  block parameter check box is cleared.  The incorrect code might take the form of an if-then-else  statement that performs no actions. For example, if (test_model0_U.in1 < 0) { } else { }  The probability of incorrect code increases if the model contains an S-Function block.
Workaround: Do either the following:Turn off expression folding for the model by clearing the Eliminate superfluous temporary variables (Expression folding) parameter check box on the Optimization pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box.Place a Signal Conversion block at the outport of the offending Abs block and select the Signal Conversion block parameter check box Exclude this block from 'Block reduction' optimization.

Summary: MATLAB? may crash when using mtimes in conjunction with transpose with an N-D array and a nonscalar 2-D array.
Description: The following code produces a crash in MATLAB R2007a: A = ones(3,3,3); % Create an N-D arrayx = ones(3,1); % Create a nonscalar, full 2-D arrayx'*A The final line of code should result in an error because it is not possible to use mtimes  in MATLAB with N-D arrays unless the other argument is a scalar.

Summary: aviread returns identical data for different frame reads
Description: Sometimes subsequent reads of individual frames with aviread  return the same data. For example, the following code can return zero in the last line, indicating that the first two frames are identical, even when the frames are different:    mov1=aviread('myfile.avi',1);   mov2=aviread('myfile.avi',2);   any(any((mov1.cdata==mov2.cdata)~=1))
Workaround: Use the new mmreader function, which replaces aviread. The aviread function will be removed in a future release. For example:  % Create an mmreader object  obj = mmreader('myfile.avi')  % read frame 1  mov1 = read(obj, 1);  % read frame 1202  mov2 = read(obj, 1202);  % compare frames  any(any((mov1 == mov2) ~= 1))The mmreader function is available in MATLAB on various platforms starting with the following releases:R2007b - WindowsR2008a - Mac OSXR2009a - Linux

Summary: MATLAB? may crash when using dmperm.
Description: Calling dmperm  with large sparse matrices may cause MATLAB versions R2007b and previous to crash.

Summary: SEGV with jmi:JNIEnv_::CallVoidMethod Stack Trace
Description: SEGV occurs with the following Stack Trace: [0] jvm.dll:0x6d75baff(247800, 0x03cefe90, 0, 0x00ccb1a8)[1] jmi.dll:public: void __cdecl JNIEnv_::CallVoidMethod(class _jobject *,struct _jmethodID *,...)(247800, 0x03cefe90, 0, 0x03cebce8) + 29 bytes[2] This stack trace does not reveal what action was being performed at the time of the SEGV, and there is no information about which Java class was called. In order to further diagnose this problem, please file a companion problem report, providing further details about what you are doing in MATLAB at the time of the failure. ?

Summary: DM642EVM model hangs during start-up
Description: In some cases, a model targeted for DM642EVM platform may hang during the targetInitialize() or <model>_initialize() stages. Investigating the issue further suggests that some I2C calls such as I2C_write() or I2C_read() issued during board initialization or Video Capture device initialization fail to execute resulting in an infinite loop. If the model is executing a real-time video processing algorithm, the net result is blank screen observed at the display device.
Workaround: Follow these steps to update the demo.Download the attached ZIP archive patch_494956.zip and the patch installer patch_installer.m.To install the patch, Start MATLAB and enter the following command at the prompt: patch_installer('patch_494956.zip', 'install')Installing patch patch_494956.zip fixes the problem by disabling '641x - Configure L2 Memory Settings' option in the DSP/BIOS configuration file. Powe cycle the board after installing the patch.

Summary: R2007b Simulink? Design Verifier is incompatible with some R2007b+ Design Automation Products
Description: If you install any of the following R2007b+ products on top of an R2007b installation that includes Simulink? Design Verifier: Simulink? 7.0.1 Stateflow? 7.0.1 Stateflow? Coder 7.0.1 Simulink Fixed Point 5.5.1 Real-Time Workshop? 7.0.1 Real-Time Workshop Embedded Coder 5.0.1 Simulink? Verification and Validation 2.2.1 Fixed-Point Toolbox 2.1.1  Simulink? Design Verifier may operate incorrectly, causing errors.

Summary: R2007b Simscape is incompatible with some R2007b+ Design Automation Products
Description: If you install any of the following R2007b+ products on top of an R2007b installation that includes Simscape: Simulink? 7.0.1 Stateflow? 7.0.1 Stateflow? Coder 7.0.1 Simulink Fixed Point 5.5.1 Real-Time Workshop? 7.0.1 Real-Time Workshop Embedded Coder 5.0.1 Simulink? Verification and Validation 2.2.1 Fixed-Point Toolbox 2.1.1  Simscape may operate incorrectly, causing errors.

Summary: Generated code for model containing reusable subsystem might not compile
Description: The Real-Time Workshop code generator implements a subsystem as a function with arguments if either of the following is true: The Real-Time Workshop system code  parameter for the subsystem is set to Reusable function . The Real-Time Workshop system code  parameter for the subsystem is set to Auto  and Simulink reuses the subsystem for multiple instances  When a subsystem is implemented as a function with arguments, the generated code is invalid if all of the following are true: One of the inputs to the subsystem is connected to a block output that is defined as a nonaddressable storage class. The storage class of that block output is a signal with a custom storage class that is not addressable, such as BitField  or GetSet . The subsystem input is passed to its function by pointer.  The generated code does not compile because it attempts to take the address of the variable or expression corresponding to the input of the Subsystem block.
Workaround: Do one of the following:Use a Signal Converstion block: Place a Signal Conversion block at the forementioned input to the Subsystem block.Select the Override optimizations and always copy signal check box for the Signal Conversion block.Change the Real-Time Workshop system code parameter for the subsystem to Inline or Function.
Fixed In: R2007b web release, R2008a web release, R2008b

Summary: Double-clicking on a Filter Design Toolbox block in the Signal Processing Blockset opens the subsystem, not the dialog, when placed in a subsystem.
Description: The File Design Toolbox blocks in the Signal Processing Blockset opens the subsystem instead of the mask dialog when placed in a subsystem. This prevents the user from changing the filter design.

Summary: Using 'Save as', the file type shown is incorrect when saving a figure into a directory containing a period in its name
Description: Saving a figure into a directory that contains a period causes the 'save as' type to show up as '*.pathname/name.fig '. For example, using Save as  to save 'untitled2.fig ', into the directory 'abc.def ' brings up the Save as dialog with a type of .def/untitled2.fig .
Workaround: Save into a directory that does not contain a period in its name.

Summary: Function uisetcolor can cause segmentation violations in Linux platforms
Description: Executing uisetcolor could generate segmentation violation stack traces on linux platforms prior to R2007b. These stack traces look like the following: [1] libmwuix.so:ws_getrgb(0x7fffffe2c850, 0x7fffffe2c8a0, 0, 0x100000000000001) + 627 bytes [2] libmwhg.so:hgUisetcolor(0x2aaab305db40, 0x7fffffe2c8a0, 0x2aaab305db40, 0x7fffffe2cc60) + 619 bytes [3] libmwm_dispatcher.so:Mfh_builtin::dispatch_mf(int, mxArray_tag**, int, mxArray_tag**)(0x7fffffe2c8c0, 0x2ac29d28e, 0x2aaab305db40, 0x7fffffe2d5f0) + 87 bytes [4] libmwm_dispatcher.so:Mfh_MATLAB_fn::dispatch_fh(int, mxArray_tag**, int, mxArray_tag**)(2, 170, 170, 6) + 204 bytes [5] libmwm_interpreter.so:inDispatchWithDebug(0, 1, 0x7fffffe2cc60, 0x7fffffe2cba0) + 364 bytes [6] libmwm_interpreter.so:inDispatchFromStack(0, 20, 0, 0x7fffffe2cfbc) + 1397 bytes [7] libmwm_interpreter.so:inDispatchCall(char const*, int, int, int, int*, int*)(0xffffffff00000002, 0x7fffffe2cf20, 0x7fffffe2cf28, 0x1007fffffffffff) + 166 bytes [8] libmwm_interpreter.so:protected_inInterp(inDebugCheck, int, int, opcodes, inPcodeNest_tag*, int*)(0x00ea4fe0 "finished", 0x7fffffe2d358, 0x100000000, 0x2aaaab0ae972) + 4977 bytes [9] libmwm_interpreter.so:inInterPcodeSJ(inDebugCheck, int, int, opcodes, inPcodeNest_tag*, int*)(0x7fffffe2d3b0, 0xffffffffffe2d9b0, 0x7fffffe2d550, 0x1002aaa000009f0) + 272 bytes... ? ?
Workaround: No workaround exists. However, this was fixed in R2007b and later versions of MATLAB.

Summary: Stateflow may generate incorrect code for charts that contain loops with multiple entry and exit points
Description: Using the Stateflow graphical action language, you can create complex flow graphs that contain loops with multiple entry and exit points. Charts that contain these types of flow graphs may generate code that produces wrong answers in the following situations: When you simulate the chart with debugging and animation turned off When you generate code using Real-Time Workshop  The attached file, g436036_ebr.pdf, presents an example of a flow graph that produces this bug.
Workaround: Do not use control logic that contains multiple entry or exit points. For example, define control logic in Embedded MATLAB functions or by using standard constructs such as while loops and for loops.

Summary: Error message during code generation that refers to error in 'CGIR'
Description: When you try to generate code for a model that contains conditionally executed subsystems or Stateflow charts, you might encounter the following error message: Error building Real-Time Workshop target for block diagram 'mymodel'. MATLAB error message: Error using ==> rtwgen Unexpected or internal error encountered in "in CGIR: .\rtwcg_utils\rtwcg_tag.cpp line 938: getNodeRTWCGTag(startNode) == getNodeRTWCGTag(endNode) && getNodeRTWCGTag(startNode) == rtwTag
Workaround: To avoid this problem, clear the Include Comments check box in the Configuration Parameters > Real-Time Workshop > Comments pane.
Fixed In: R2007b web release, R2008a web release, R2008b

Summary: Generating code for a Math Function block with a 16-bit output signal might produce an error
Description: The Real-Time Workshop product might display an incorrect error message if you generate code for a model that contains a Math Function block whose Function  parameter specifies sqrt  and whose output signal specifies a 16-bit fixed-point or integer data type. For example, you encounter this error under all the following conditions: The output signal of the Math Function block specifies a word length of 16 bits, e.g., int16 . On the Hardware Implementation  pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box, the Number of bits  parameter specifies the following values: ? int  long  native word size   Number of bits  16 32 16     In this case, the Real-Time Workshop product displays an error message such as the following: Error using ==> rtwgenReal-Time Workshop can't generate code for this model because block'Math Function' is using the data type 'int32_T' which requires 32 bits,but the maximum supported for code generation is 32.  However, this message is erroneous because the Real-Time Workshop product should be able generate code in this situation.

Summary: Incorrect result: Generated code or simulation results for Bias block might be incorrect
Description: When you generate code or simulate an accelerated model, the generated code or simulation results for the Bias block might be incorrect under all of the following conditions: The input signal to the Bias block is a frame signal. The Signal storage reuse  parameter is selected.  The following is an example of an incorrect  code pattern you might generate for the attached model using the Real-Time Workshop R2007a product: for (i = 0; i < 3; i++) {  /* Bias: '<Root>/Bias1' */  tmp = (rtb_Bias1[inputIndexVar] + temp_P.Bias1_Bias[i]) +  temp_P.Bias1_Bias[i];  inputIndexVar = 3 * i + 2;  tmp += temp_P.Bias1_Bias[i];  rtb_Bias1[inputIndexVar] = tmp;  }
Workaround: Clear the Signal storage reuse check box in the Configuraiton Parameters > Optimization pane.

Summary: uigetfile opens dialog boxes that are different on Windows XP 32-bit and Windows XP 64-bit in MATLAB 7.4 (R2007a)
Description: uigetfile opens a java based dialog box on 64 bit PC's and a native windows dialog box on 32 bit PC's. This is caused by a problem fixed in R2007b.
Workaround: To work around this issue:1. Quit MATLAB2. Rename the following MATLAB-file:$MATLABROOT\toolbox\matlab\uitools\@AbstractFileDialog\AbstractFileDialog.mto AbstractFileDialog.m.old.(Where $MATLAB is the MATLAB root directory on your machine, returned by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command window.)3. Download the attached file and put it in the same directory as in step 2.4. Restart MATLAB.5. After restarting MATLAB, in the MATLAB command window, type:  rehash toolboxcache

Summary: Unnecessary start and terminate functions in generated code
Description: When a subsystem contains a System Outputs block, the Real-Time Workshop code generator might create unnecessary, empty start and terminate functions for the subsystem under the following conditions: The generated code of the subsystem is inlined (by setting the subsystem Real-Time Workshop system code  parameter to Inline , or Auto  and the Real-Time Workshop build process inlines the code). The subsystem is inside another subsystem whose generated code is not inlined.
Workaround: Set the subsystem Real-Time Workshop system code parameter to Function or Reusable function. This eliminates the unnecessary start and terminate functions. Note that the output function is no longer inlined.

Summary: Simulating referenced models that use Stateflow library charts incorrectly requires Stateflow Coder license
Description: When you use Stateflow library charts in referenced models, a Stateflow Coder license is checked out unnecessarily for the Model Reference simulation target. If you do not have a Stateflow Coder license, you receive an error.

Summary: Generated code might crash due to excessive memory usage
Description: When you use a Simulink Math Function block to perform a transpose or hermitian function, the Real-Time Workshop code generator creates an unnecessary local variable that uses a lot of memory, and the generated code might crash when you execute it.

Summary: Pulse Generator block may generate incorrect output in a Normal mode referenced model
Description: A Pulse Generator block in a referenced model may generate incorrect output if the referenced model executes in Normal mode and the Pulse Generator block has a discrete sample time.

Summary: Real-Time Workshop software generates noncompileable code for Saturation blocks
Description: The Real-Time Workshop software might generate noncompileable code for Saturation blocks under all of the following conditions: The output of the Saturation block is connected to one destination. The output signal of the Saturation block is not a test point. The data type of the input signal to the Saturation block is fixed-point.
Workaround: Do any of the following:Connect the output signal of the Saturation block to multiple destinations.Add a test point to the output signal of the Saturation block.Change the data type of the input signal of the Saturation block to a non-fixed-point data type.

Summary: MATLAB may crash during Simulink model compilation if the model intermixes virtual and nonvirtual buses
Description: If a Simulink model intermixes virtual and nonvirtual buses in the following way, MATLAB may exit with a segmentation violation when compiling the model: The model contains a Bus Creator block whose output bus signal is a direct input (no intervening blocks) to a second Bus Creator block. The virtuality of the bus that is input to the second Bus Creator block does not match the virtuality of the bus that is output by the block.
Workaround: Ensure that the virtuality of any bus signal created by one Bus Creator block, and input to a second Bus Creator block, matches the virtuality of the bus signal output by the second Bus Creator block. You can use a Signal Conversion block to change the virtuality of a bus.

Summary: Block I/O buffer of a Merge block might change between local and global when the name of a source block changes
Description: If the name of a source block to a Merge block changes, block I/O buffer allocation might change in the following way. If the alphabetical order of the name of the source block: Precedes the name of the outport signal which serves as input to the Merge block, local buffer allocation occurs. Follows the name of the outport signal which serves as input to the Merge block, global allocation occurs.  The block I/O buffer allocation only occurs when all of the following conditions are met: The Configuration Parameters > Optimization > Enable local block outputs  check box is selected. There are two or more conditionally executed subsystems, for example, the Switch Case Action Subsystem, in the model. Outputs of the conditionally executed subsystems are merged in a different, nonparent subsystem. The conditionally executed subsystems are not in the model root level.

Summary: Incorrect code generation: Code generator incorrectly uses local variable for block output
Description: The Real-Time Workshop software might incorrectly use local variables, instead of global variables, for block output buffers in conditionally executed subsystems, resulting in incorrect or uncompilable generated code, under the following conditions: The Configuration Parameters > Optimization > Enable local block outputs  check box is selected. There are two or more conditionally executed subsystems, for example, the Switch Case Action Subsystem, in the model. Outputs of the conditionally executed subsystems are merged in a different, nonparent subsystem. The conditionally executed subsystems are not in the model root level.  Attached is a sample model that demonstrates the problem.
Workaround: Make the merge signals global using a test point or global storage class.

Summary: Mixed Bus Types: MATLAB can crash when compiling a Simulink model that contains both virtual and non-virtual buses
Description: When all of the following are true, MATLAB may exit with a segmentation violation when compiling a Simulink model. MATLAB will not produce a stack trace on exit. The Simulink model contains both virtual and non-virtual buses. At least one of the buses in the Simulink model is created by a Bus Creator block. If that Bus Creator block is virtual, at least one of its inputs is a nonvirtual bus; or if it is nonvirtual, at least one of its inputs is a virtual bus. The model contains at least one Bus Selector that selects a signal from a Bus Creator that has one of the input configurations mentioned in Step 3.
Workaround: Do not use a Bus Creator block to intermix virtual and non-virtual buses. If the output of a Bus Creator is virtual, all inputs to the block must be virtual buses or non-bus signals. Similarly, if the output of a Bus Creator is non-virtual, all inputs to the block must be non-virtual buses or non-bus signals.

Summary: Overflow error occurs when using a Subtract block to subtract two unsigned values
Description: Suppose that your model contains a Subtract block that subtracts two unsigned integer or fixed-point values. Simulink might display a run-time overflow error if all of the following conditions apply: The Subtract block's Saturate on integer overflow  parameter (on the Signal Attributes  pane of the block dialog box) is disabled. The model's Detect overflow  parameter (under Data Validity > Signals  on the Configuration Parameters dialog box) specifies error .
Workaround: To work around this issue, consider any of the following approaches:Upcast the Subtract block's input signals to a larger data type. For example, suppose that the data type of a Subtract block's input signals specify ufix16_En3 (i.e., a 16-bit unsigned generalized fixed-point number), and the block's Output data type parameter specifies Inherit: Inherit via internal rule. In this case, you can prevent the overflow error by upcasting both inputs to a data type such as ufix17_En3 (i.e., a 17-bit unsigned generalized fixed-point number).Convert both of the Subtract block's input signals to signed integer or fixed-point data types.Set the model's Detect overflow parameter (under Data Validity > Signals on the Configuration Parameters dialog box) to warning.

Summary: Spurious error message for asynchronous function call subsystem containing Stateflow chart connected to Mux or Bus Creator block
Description: Simulink can incorrectly flag an error for an asynchronous function call subsystem if all of the following conditions are true: The asynchronous function call subsystem contains a Stateflow chart that outputs data to Simulink. At least one data output from the Stateflow chart connects to a Mux or Bus Creator block. At least one input of the Mux or Bus Creator block is not from the Stateflow chart.  Simulink displays the following error message: mux-block-name  may not be used to combine an asynchronous function call with data or a synchronous function call.
Workaround: To work around this bug, insert a nonvirtual block between the Stateflow chart outport and the Mux or Bus Creator block.

Summary: Assertion failure in LowerMatrixTypesForBackend.cpp during code generation of models containing Embedded MATLAB functions.
Description: A bug caused by the 'Create reference parameters' optimization causes an assertion during code generation for models containing Embedded MATLAB Function blocks in Simulink or Embedded MATLAB functions in Stateflow. The source file LowerMatrixTypesForBackend.cpp  appears at the top of the stack trace. The bug is triggered by code that contains the pattern x=function (x) , where function   is the name of an Embedded MATLAB function. Note that this pattern may originate from Embedded MATLAB library code.
Workaround: Switch off the 'Create reference parameters' optimization by typing the following command at the MATLAB command prompt: sf('feature', 'Create reference parameters', 0)

Summary: Possible incorrect numerical results due to improper target function library (TFL) replacement of addition operation
Description: During code generation for a model containing a Sum block, the addition operation may be improperly replaced with a TFL-specified function if both of the following conditions are true: The inputs to the Sum block have different scaling values than the output of the Sum block. The addition operator entry registered in the current TFL specifies that TFL replacement request processing should not check for an exact match to the call-site slope and bias values.  Under these conditions, the addition operation is replaced and the difference in the slopes and biases between the inputs and output is lost. When the generated code is executed, the numerical results may not match the simulated results for the same model. Note:  This issue can only occur if a custom target function library is used and it registers a replacement for the addition operation. The target function libraries provided with Real-Time Workshop and Real-Time Workshop Embedded Coder do not exhibit this issue.

Summary: S functions with zero crossings may cause RTW code generation to fail if a variable step solver is used.
Description: The RTW file created during code generation does not contain the names of the signals registering zero crossings for S functions. This can cause code generation to fail if the S function uses zero crossing detection. This will only occur if a variable step solver is selected because zero crossing detection is only enabled with those solvers.

Summary: Switch block might route signals incorrectly if the data type of the control input signal cannot represent the real-world value of zero
Description: The generated code for the Switch block might route its signals incorrectly under all of the following conditions: The Switch block's Criteria for passing first input  parameter specifies u2 ~= 0 . The data type associated with the Switch block's control input (u2) cannot represent the real-world value of zero, as is the case for the data type sfix32_Bp5 .  When simulating the model in Simulink, the control input signal can never be zero; hence, the Switch block's condition is always true and the block always routes its first input signal to its output. But in the code generated by Real-Time Workshop, the value of zero from the u2 ~= 0 expression is cast to the control input signal's data type for comparison, resulting in a non-zero real-world-value; consequently, the control input signal might attain a value that causes the Switch block's condition to be false and passes the third input signal as the block's output.
Workaround: To work around this issue, ensure that the data type of a Switch block's control input signal can represent the real-world value of zero. For example, insert a Data Type Conversion block before the Switch block and cast the control input signal to an appropriate data type. The data type that you specify should be able to represent the real-world value of zero without losing precision, range, or signedness.

Summary: Using slash characters ("/") in the names of Stateflow inputs, outputs, and events may prevent you from loading a model after resaving in it Stateflow 7.0 (R2007b)
Description: When you add inputs, outputs, and events in Stateflow charts, Simulink creates corresponding ports of the same name on the Stateflow block. If the names of these objects contain a forward slash "/" character, the full path name of the port looks ambiguous to Simulink. This ambiguity may cause errors in the way Simulink handles the model. As a result, after saving and closing the model, the model may no longer load. As an example, consider a model "M" containing a chart "C", which has an input named "A/B". The full path name for the input port corresponding to this data is "M/C/A/B", which can be misinterpreted as a model "M" containing subsystem "C" containing subsystem "A" containing port "B".
Workaround: If your model is open:Simulate your model to flag incorrectly named data as parse errors.Fix the errors by renaming the data to eliminate the slash characters.If your model is not open, there is no workaround at this time.

Summary: Inport block of virtual subsystem incorrectly inherits constant (inf) sample time when Inline Parameters is Off
Description: An inport block in a virtual subsystem can get a constant (inf) sample time when it should not if the following conditions hold: The inport specifies a sample time of inherited (-1) The virtual subsystem is driven by an atomic subsystem that has a sample time specified in its dialog Inline parameters (on the Optimization tab of Configuration Parameters) is off for the model  ?
Workaround: To work around this issue either:Set the desired sample time on the inport block in the virtual subsystem, orTurn off inline parameters

Summary: In MacOSX 10.5, SimBiology 2.1.2 (7b release) causes MATLAB to crash.
Description: In MacOSX 10.5, simulation of models using SimBiology 2.1.2 causes MATLAB to crash.
Workaround: No workaround. Version 2.2 of SimBiology fixes the problem.

Summary: Dragging blocks from one window to another on Mac OS X 10.5 ("Leopard") may crash Simulink
Description: Dragging a block from one window to another on Mac OS X 10.5 ("Leopard") can crash Simulink. The crash occurs when the mouse moves over any non-X11 window, including the Mac OS X desktop, during the drag. When the crash occurs, Apple's X11.app also crashes.
Workaround: To fix this issue in an R2007a, R2007a Student Version, or R2007b installation:Log into your Macintosh, using an account with Administrator privileges.Download the appropriate patch for your version from the patches attached to this report.Locate the download.If your web browser did not unzip it for you, double-click in the Finder to unzip the file.Double-click the disk image to mount it.At the top level of the mounted disk image is an application called "Install Patch." Double-click to launch it.You are prompted to select your MATLAB installation. Select the root folder of the install. The patch script attempts to locate this for you and preselects it in the folder chooser if it is successful.Follow the prompts to complete installation of the patch. You can unmount the disk image when you are done.This patch renames the following files (adding a suffix of ".old") in your MATLAB installation, if they are present:matlabroot/bin/mac/libmwuix.dylibmatlabroot/bin/mac/libmwuix.dylib.csfmatlabroot/bin/maci/libmwuix.dylibmatlabroot/bin/maci/libmwuix.dylib.csfIt then replaces them with updated versions.

Summary: The Lookup Table Editor for a masked block returns an error when block parameters are defined by using the Mask Initialization panel
Description: When using the Lookup Table Editor for a masked block, if the block has parameters defined through the Mask Initialization panel, the Lookup Table Editor posts an error message saying: "The LUT Editor requires the parameter 'parameterName' of block 'BlockName' to be defined." This message appears because the LUT Editor could not obtain the parameter value that was defined using the Mask Initialization panel

Summary: Segmentation fault may occur when simulating or generating code for a Stateflow chart that references data store memory
Description: Simulating or generating code for a model may cause a segmentation fault if the following are true: The model contains a Stateflow chart that references a data store. You select Data store name must resolve to Simulink signal object . There is no signal object of the specified name.
Workaround: Define the missing Simulink signal object.

Summary: Lookup Table Editor rounds values specified in block dialog box of lookup table
Description: Suppose your model contains a lookup table block whose breakpoint vectors or table data specify values that have six or greater digits. Note that when you open the Lookup Table Editor to view or edit this data, the editor displays such values rounded to five digits. But if you then select Update Block Data  from either the editor's File  menu or toolbar, the editor replaces those values in your lookup table block using the less precise rounded values. For example, the editor replaces 1234567  with 1.2346e+006 ; similarly, it replaces 0.1234567  with 0.12346 .

Summary: Enabling conditional input execution optimization when a Switch Block's execution branch is contained within an algebraic loop causes a segmentation violation to occur.
Description: A segmentation violation will occur in Simulink duing simulation if a Switch Block's execution branch is included in an Algebraic loop, and Conditional input execution optimization  is selected.
Workaround: To workaround this problem, deselect the Conditional input branch execution checkbox located in the Optimization pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog:From the Simulink toolbar, select SimulationClick on Configuration ParametersFrom the list on the left, select the Optimization paneLocate the Conditional input branch execution checkbox, and clear the checkmarkClick OK to accept the change and close the dialog?

Summary: Incorrect code generation: Generated code fails to compile for a reusable subsystem
Description: The generated code fails to compile when a reusable subsystem has an input signal that is an array of structures. You encounter an error message such as the following: Error codereuse_struct.c: 40?? operands of = have illegal types `myStruct' and `pointer to const myStruct' Error codereuse_struct.c: 41?? operands of = have illegal types `myStruct' and `pointer to const myStruct' Error codereuse_struct.c: 48?? illegal expression Error codereuse_struct.c: 48?? syntax error; found `codereuse_struct_U' expecting `)' Error codereuse_struct.c: 48?? insufficient number of arguments to `codereuse_struct_Subsystem' Error codereuse_struct.c: 48?? syntax error; found `codereuse_struct_U' expecting `;' Warning codereuse_struct.c: 49?? Statement has no effect Error codereuse_struct.c: 49?? syntax error; found `)' expecting `;' Error codereuse_struct.c: 49?? illegal statement termination Error codereuse_struct.c: 49?? skipping `)' Warning codereuse_struct.c: 75?? Statement has no effect
Workaround: If you are using Real-Time Workshop versions R2007a or R2007a+, please read the following:Replace the file matlabroot/rtw/c/tlc/mw/commonpass.tlc as follows:Determine your MATLAB root directory by entering the matlabroot command at the MATLAB Command Line.Quit MATLAB.Rename matlabroot/rtw/c/tlc/mw/commonpass.tlc to matlabroot/rtw/c/tlc/mw/commonpass.tlc.old.Download the attached file attachment_406964_20080508.zip and extract commonpass_g406964_for_7a.tlc from it, placing it in the directory matlabroot/rtw/c/tlc/mw and renaming it to matlabroot/rtw/c/tlc/mw/commonpass.tlcRestart MATLAB.If you are using Real-Time Workshop versions R2007b, please read the following:Replace the file matlabroot/rtw/c/tlc/mw/commonpass.tlc as follows:Determine your MATLAB root directory by entering the matlabroot command at the MATLAB Command Line.Quit MATLAB.Rename matlabroot/rtw/c/tlc/mw/commonpass.tlc to matlabroot/rtw/c/tlc/mw/commonpass.tlc.old.Download the attached file attachment_406964_20080508.zip and extract commonpass_g406964_for_7b.tlc from it, placing it in the directory matlabroot/rtw/c/tlc/mw and renaming it to matlabroot/rtw/c/tlc/mw/commonpass.tlcRestart MATLAB.There are currently no known workarounds for other versions of Real-Time Workshop.

Summary: Stateflow charts that execute at initialization cannot access data store memory in entry actions or default transitions
Description: When you configure a chart to execute at initialization, you cannot access data store memory or input data in state entry actions or default transitions. Attempting these actions causes a segmentation violation during Real-Time Workshop code generation.The reason for the restriction is that charts execute certain state entry actions and default transitions during initialization.
Workaround: Turn off the "Execute at initialization" option for your chart or remove access to data store memory and input data in state entry actions and default transitions.

Summary: Real-Time Workshop can generate code that does not compile correctly if the model?includes function-calls?that are involved in self loops and have wide inputs.
Description: The Real-Time Workshop software generates code that cannot be compiled if all of the following conditons are met in a function-call subsystem: Subsystem has inputs that come from within its own context, or from other function-call contexts that are called from within it (function-call contexts are expained in the Workaround section). Subsystem has wide inputs (vector/matrix, not bus inputs) that have non-constant sample time. Subsystem is set to generate a reusable function.  See the attached model for an example?of this bug.
Workaround: It is not?good modeling practice to use function-call subsystems with inputs that are context-dependent.? Such inputs can change during execution of the funciton-call, and cause the behavior of the function-call to be dependent on how it is connected externally to other blocks. See 'sl_subsys_fcncallerr12.mdl', that ships with Simulink, for a more detailed explanation of this issue. The recommended workaround for this bug is to modify the function-call subsystem to elliminate context-dependent inputs.?You can do this by performing the following steps:Select the Warn if function-call inputs are context dependent checkbox on the subsystem dialog.Ensure that the model level setting for the Context Dependent Inputs option in the Diagnostics pane (Diagnostics > Connectivity > Function-calls) of the block-diagram is NOT set to Disable all.Compile the block-diagram and identify all warnings that are referring to the function-call subsystem in question.Use the Latch inputs by copying inside signal option on all function-call input port blocks that are indicated in the warnings.This should fix the Real-Time Workshop compile error.For example, in the attached model, you can set the Latch inputs by copying inside signal option on the input port block 'In2' of the subsystem to workaround the bug.

Summary: Real-Time Workshop software fails to generate code for models with Stateflow charts
Description: The Real-Time Workshop software might fail to generate code for models with Stateflow charts that execute outputs at initialization. When this occurs, MATLAB displays the following error message at the command line: Error building Real-Time Workshop target for block diagram 'model_name '. MATLAB error message:Error using ==> tlc_newError: File: file_location/file_name Unable to find DeclareInStartFcnScope within the LocalBlockOutput scope.
Workaround: Clear the Execute (enter) Chart At Initialization check box in the Stateflow chart properties dialog.

Summary: Student Version: Index tab in Help Browser does not include topics from MATLAB documentation
Description: When you access the Index tab in the Help browser for the MATLAB Student Version, there are no entries for MATLAB. Index entries correctly appear for all other products.
Workaround: Download the attached zip file to the matlabroot/help/techdoc directory, where matlabroot is the directory in which MATLAB is installed, as determined by running the matlabroot function in the Command WindowUnzip the file, which results in the file helpindex.student.xml being added to your directory.

Summary: The figure property 'SelectionType' does not work properly in compiled applications.
Description: This bug occurs in GUIDE created GUIs that contain a listbox. When using the property 'SelectionType' to detect shift-click, alt-click, and normal-click, these events are occasionally not detected.
Workaround: There is no workaround.

Summary: Code generation fails with messages including "Unable to find CanonicalOutputArgDef within the origInterface scope" and "Arguments to ISFIELD must be records"
Description: Code generation can fail for a model if all of the following conditions are true: The model contains a nonvirtual subsystem, from which output is passed to a Merge block. Additionally, the nonvirtual subsystem contains a reusable subsystem. The reusable subsystem outputs a signal that passes through at least one outport in the containing nonvirtual subsystem. The nonvirtual subsystem outport through which the signal passes specifies an Initial output  value other than [] . In other words, an initial condition is defined between the actual source of the signal and the Merge block.  During code generation, Real-Time Workshop displays errors similar to the following: Error: File: C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2007b\rtw\c\tlc\mw\opaquelib.tlc Line: 362 Column: 36Unable to find CanonicalOutputArgDef within the Interface scopeError: File: C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2007b\rtw\c\tlc\mw\opaquelib.tlc Line: 362 Column: 58The argument for an [] operation must be a repeated scope symbol, a vector, or a matrixError: File: C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2007b\rtw\c\tlc\lib\sysarglib.tlc Line: 59 Column: 21Arguments to ISFIELD must be recordsError: File: C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2007b\rtw\c\tlc\lib\sysarglib.tlc Line: 59 Column: 3Arguments to %addtorecord must be records### Real-Time Workshop build procedure for model: 'model' aborted due to an error.Error using ==> tlc_newError: File: C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2007b\rtw\c\tlc\lib\sysarglib.tlc Line: 67 Column: 21Arguments to ISFIELD must be records
Workaround: To work around this bug, you can perform either of the following actions:Remove the Initial output value from the nonvirtual subsystem outport and place it on the reusable subsystem outport.Remove the Initial output values from all outports and place the initial value on the Merge block.

Summary: Non-virtual buses passing through latched function-call subsystem inputs may produce incorrect results in both simulation and generated code
Description: Function-call subsystems in Simulink models may generate incorrect answers or produce incorrect code if both the following conditions are met: One of the inport blocks in the function call subsystem has 'Latch input by copying inside signal'?set to ON. This inport is connected to a non-virtual bus signal (a bus signal that emanates? from the root inport of a model block, or from a Bus Creator in which the?'Output as nonvirtual bus' option is ON).  Please download the attached model (and companion MATLAB-file) to see a reproduction case for?this bug. Load the model (which should also?run the MATLAB-file as part of the PreLoadFcn)?and simulate it. The results?displayed on?the two scopes should be identical, but are clearly different.
Workaround: Turning off?'Latch input by?copying inside signal'?should eliminate?this issue. You can obtain functionality that is equivalent to the latch by manually inserting a Signal Conversion block after the corresponding Inport block inside the subsystem. The Signal Conversion block should be set to the 'Nonvirtual bus' option. Alternatively, you may convert the non-virtual bus to a virtual bus before feeding it to the function-call subsystem by using the Signal Conversion block and choosing the 'Virtual bus' option.

Summary: External mode: incorrect code and build error can occur for simple model with DWork vector and no accessible signals
Description: The Real-Time Workshop build process can generate an invalid model _dt.h  file for a model, resulting in a build error, when all of the following conditions are true:     External mode is selected for the model     The model has no accessible signals     The model has at least one DWork vector  This error is very rare and occurs only for very simple models.
Workaround: To work around this bug, make sure that models for which external mode is selected have at least one accessible signal.

Summary: Models with NaNs in transition labelPosition property cannot be loaded after resaving in Stateflow 7.0 (R2007b)
Description: In rare cases, you cannot load Stateflow models after saving them in R2007b if the models contain transitions that were saved with NaN s in the labelPosition  property. If you encounter this bug, please email The MathWorks at support@mathworks.com. To proactively detect models that contain this corruption, follow these steps: Download the the attached file look_for_label_position_nans.m   to a writable directory on your machine. Start MATLAB and add this directory to your MATLAB path. In MATLAB, navigate to the directory containing your Simulink model files and run the following function: >> look_for_label_position_nans  The function alerts you to models that contain this corruption by displaying messages in your command window that look like this: Found corrupted labelPosition property set to NaN in xyz.mdl.Found corrupted labelPosition property set to -1.#IO in abc.mdl.
Workaround: To fix corrupt models, follow these steps:Open the model (.mdl file) as a text file in the MATLAB Editor and search for lines containing the pattern labelPosition [-1.#IO or labelPosition [NaN NaN, as in this example: labelPosition ?[-1.#IO -1.#IO 8.182 15.58]Replace the entry -1.#IO or NaN with 0.Navigate to the following directory: $matlabroot/toolbox/stateflow/stateflow/privateIn this path, $matlabroot is the MATLAB installation directory and can be located by executing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt.Rename sfinit.m to sfinit.m.old.Save the attached file (sfinit.m) to this location.Restart MATLAB.

Summary: Sum block might generate a data type propagation error when its input signals specify data type aliases
Description: A Sum block might generate erroneous data type propagation errors under all of the following conditions: The Sum block's Output data type  parameter specifies Inherit: Inherit via internal rule . The Sum block's input signals use Simulink.AliasType  objects to specify data type aliases. For example, suppose that the data type of an input signal is set to myfloat , which is a Simulink.AliasType  object whose BaseType  property specifies single .  In this case, the Sum block might fail to recognize the equivalence between the Simulink.AliasType  object (e.g., myfloat ) and the base data type (e.g., single ) that the block chooses to output. Consequently, Simulink displays a data type propagation error.
Workaround: You can work around this issue using either of the following methods:Specify the Sum block's Output data type parameter as something other than Inherit: Inherit via internal rule. For example, specify the parameter as Inherit: Same as first input; or you can explicitly specify it as the name of your data type alias, e.g., myfloat.Connect a Signal Specification block to the Sum block's outport. Specify the Signal Specification block's Data type parameter as the name of your data type alias, e.g., myfloat.

Summary: The MODEL_acc_data.c file produced for an accelerated simulation may fail to compile, resulting in a failure to simulate
Description: The MODEL_acc_data.c file produced for an accelerated simulation may fail to compile, resulting in a failure to simulate.? The error will refer to an invalid number of initializers; for example it may look similar to the following:MODEL_acc_data.c(975) : error C2078: too many initializers?U:\ARCHIVE\R2007B\PERFECT\MATLAB\BIN\MEX.PL: Error: Compile of MODEL_acc_data.c' failed.

Summary: Matlab R2007a crashes when supplying a v7.3 MAT file to a From File block
Description: A Segmentation Violation?can occur?when a v7.3 MAT file is provided to a From File block.A fragment of the stack trace appears below:?Stack Trace:??[0] libmat.dll:struct mxArray_tag * __cdecl GetNextVariable_7??????(struct MATFile_tag *,char const * *)??????(0x1092c1a0, 0, 0x00cead50, 0x7a38cb31) + 83 bytes??[1] libmat.dll:_matGetNextVariable(0x1092c1a0, 0, 0xd48a3529, 0x1515ac60) + 32 bytes??[2] libmwsimulink.dll:struct slErrMsgQueue * __cdecl FromFileEvalParamsFcn??????(class slBlock *)(0x1515ac60, 0, 0x12641800, 0) + 609 bytes
Workaround: Save the MAT file using?the -v7 flag, for example:???>> clear all???>> load('data.mat') %This is a v7.3 mat-file???>> save('newdata.mat', '-v7')

Summary: Using audioplayer and audiorecorder together on Apple Macintosh OS X causes MATLAB to crash
Description: The problem of MATLAB crashing can be shown with code that uses audiorecorder  and audioplayer  together, as follows: % Set up variablesfc = 440;T = 3;fs = 44100;t = 0:1/fs:T;y = 1*sin(2*pi*fc*t);% Create an audio player and recorderrecorder = audiorecorder(fs, 16, 1);player   = audioplayer(y, fs);% Start playbackplay(player)% Start recordingrecord(recorder, 5);% Pause for 6 secondspause(6)% Stop the recorderstop(recorder) When the code runs on Macintosh OS X, MATLAB hangs and then crashes. Because of a bug in Macintosh OS X, you cannot use the play functionality of audioplayer  simultanously with audiorecorder .
Workaround: To work around this issue, use the playblocking function of audioplayer instead:% Set up variablesfc = 440;T = 3;fs = 44100;t = 0:1/fs:T;y = 1*sin(2*pi*fc*t);% Create an audio player and recorderrecorder = audiorecorder(fs, 16, 1);player   = audioplayer(y, fs);% Play back audio completely before recordingplayblocking(player)% Start recordingrecord(recorder, 5);% Pause for 6 secondspause(6)% Stop the recorderstop(recorder)

Summary: Error occurs when relaunching Model Advisor
Description: The Simulink software produces an error under the following conditions: Launch Model Advisor for your model. Close the Model Advisor window without closing the model. Launch Model Advisor for the model or a subsystem within the model.  The following is an example of the error message: No public field text exists for class handle.
Workaround: You can resolve this issue in two ways:Option 1: Close and reopen the model between Model Advisor sessions.Option 2: Use the following instructions to relpace the matlabroot\toolbox\simulink\simulink\@Simulink\@ModelAdvisor\displayExplorer.p file.Determine your MATLAB root directory by entering the matlabroot command at the MATLAB command prompt.Quit MATLAB.Rename matlabroot\toolbox\simulink\simulink\@Simulink\@ModelAdvisor\displayExplorer.p to matlabroot\toolbox\simulink\simulink\@Simulink\@ModelAdvisor\displayExplorer.p.old.Download the attached file and place it in the directory matlabroot\toolbox\simulink\simulink\@Simulink\@ModelAdvisor.Restart MATLAB.

Summary: Problems downloading sequence files from NCBI GenBank and GenPept databases using GETGENBANK and GETGENPEPT functions
Description: Problems downloading files (specifically with whole genome sequence files) using GETGENBANK and GETGENPEPT functions due to changes to the NCBI Entrez system.
Workaround: Follow these steps as a workaround to these changes:1) Quit MATLAB2) Rename the following MATLAB-files:??? ??? a) $MATLAB\toolbox\bioinfo\bioinfo\private\getncbidata.m to getncbidata.m.old??? ??? b) $MATLAB\toolbox\bioinfo\bioinfo\getgenbank.m to getgenbank.m.old??????? c) $MATLAB\toolbox\bioinfo\bioinfo\getgenpept.m to getgenpept.m.old?????????? (where $MATLAB = the MATLAB root directory on your machine)3) Copy attached files to:??? ??? a) getncbidata.m to $MATLAB\toolbox\bioinfo\bioinfo\private\??? ??? b) getgenbank.m and getgenpept.m to $MATLAB\toolbox\bioinfo\bioinfo\4) Restart MATLAB5) After restarting MATLAB, issue the following command at the MATLAB prompt:????????? rehash toolboxcache

Summary: In Embedded MATLAB, using small negative double values as conditions for if and while statements produces incorrect results during simulation.
Description: In Embedded MATLAB, if  statements and while  loops that use negative double values between -1 and 0 as the condition produce incorrect results during simulation. For example, in the following code, the if  branch never executes: u = -0.06;if u? x = x - 1;else? x = x + 1; % The else branch executes.end
Workaround: Use logical expressions as conditions for if statements and while loops, as in the following example:u = -0.06;if u ~= 0? x = x - 1;else? x = x + 1;end?

Summary: Sporadic crash when using Simulink menus
Description: Using the Simulink menus (in particular, the context menu) can crash MATLAB.? To tell if a MATLAB crash is caused by this problem, examine the the stack trace resulting from the crash. It should contain the following line:Stack Trace: ?? [0] libmwsimulink.dll:bool __cdecl get_selected(struct MWObject_tag * *)
Workaround: Download the attached MATLAB file and copy it into matlabroot/toolbox/shared/dastudio, replacing the version already there.

Summary: Signal Conversion block might generate a data type propagation error when its output signal specifies a data type alias
Description: A Signal Conversion block might generate erroneous data type propagation errors under all of the following conditions: The output signal from the Signal Conversion block uses a Simulink.AliasType  object to specify a data type alias, via either a Signal Specification block or a Simulink.Signal  object. For example, the data type of the output signal is set to myfloat , which is a Simulink.AliasType  object whose BaseType  property specifies single . The input signal to the Signal Conversion block comes from a subsystem, and that signal specifies the same data type as the BaseType  property of the Simulink.AliasType  object that the block's output signal uses.  In this case, the Signal Conversion block might fail to recognize the equivalence between the Simulink.AliasType  object (e.g., myfloat ) and the data type (e.g., single ) of the signal entering the block. Consequently, Simulink displays a data type propagation error.
Workaround: You can work around this issue by inserting a Data Type Conversion block before the Signal Conversion block. Specify the Data Type Conversion block's Output data type parameter as the name of your data type alias, e.g., myfloat.

Summary: Selecting properties to show from the Model Explorer's Customize Contents pane can cause a SegV
Description: Selecting properties to show from the Model Explorer's Customize Contents  pane can cause a SegV if the Model Explorer is set to not show properties.
Workaround: When choosing desired properties to show in the Model Explorer, make sure that the View->Show Properties menu item is checked.

Summary: The Model Explorer may hang momentarily when navigating to a workspace node with sorting enabled
Description: If list sorting is enabled and you select a workspace node with more than 500 objects, the Model Explorer may hang for about 30 seconds.
Workaround: Remove the dastudio.prf file from prefdir before starting MATLAB.

Summary: Bus Selector block: An error can occur on double clicking the Bus Selector block
Description: Double-clicking the Bus Selector block to display its Block Parameters dialog box can cause an error when both of the following are true: The block is connected directly to an input port. The input port's bus object specifies an element with fixed point data type.  The error may look like this: Error occured when trying to create dialogSpecified data type '.....' is not a valid identifier

Summary: S-Function with a terminate method may fail in a model referenced in Normal mode
Description: The terminate method (mdlTerminate ) of an S-Function may work incorrectly when the block is in a model that is referenced in Normal mode. For example, the SimStruct  macro ssGetRootSS(S)  points to the top model during simulation, but points to the S-Function's SimStruct  during mdlTerminate . Some other SimStruct  macros also may not work properly during the call to mdlTerminate , because the macro may be looking at memory that has already been freed and possibly reused. Depending on the current contents of the memory, the effect of this error can range from no effect to a segv .

Summary: Linux platforms:?model build incorrectly uses gcc compiler -ansi flag when target function library is?C99 (ISO)?or GNU99 (GNU)
Description: On Linux platforms, the Real-Time Workshop build process uses the gcc compiler -ansi ?flag to compile the generated code even when the target function library (TFL) is set to C99 (ISO)  or GNU99 (GNU)  on the Interface  pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box. If the model contains certain math function blocks, such as a Trigonometric Function block, the created executable will give wrong results. This bug does not affect other UNIX-based platforms or Windows platforms.
Workaround: To work around this bug, perform the following steps.Note: In the following instructions, matlabroot represents your MATLAB installation root, as returned by the MATLAB command matlabroot.In your MATLAB installation tree, rename the following files, for example, by adding .old to the end of each file name.matlabroot/toolbox/rtw/rtw/rtw_c.mmatlabroot/toolbox/rtw/rtw/rtw_c.pmatlabroot/toolbox/rtw/rtw/@RTW/resolveTflName.pDownload the attached files and place them in the same directory as the files you renamed.Restart MATLAB.

Summary: Exclusive OR operator yields incorrect answers with constant inputs.
Description: Exclusive OR operations can yield incorrect answers if the code generation software can constant-fold the inputs. This problem can occur for: 1) Embedded MATLAB 'xor' function 2) Stateflow action language operator '^' when the "Enable C-bit operations" box is checked in the chart properties dialog

Summary: In Embedded MATLAB, matrix concatenation of fixed-point objects converts inputs using the wrong fimath and numerictype
Description: When preforming matrix concatenation of the form [a, b]  or [a; b]  , where a  or b  are embedded.fi  objects, Embedded MATLAB may use the wrong rounding and overflow modes, thereby producing wrong answers.
Workaround: Before performing matrix concatenation, convert all inputs to the same fimath and numerictype. This can be done using the fi constructor: fi(a,T,F), where T and F are the correct numerictype and fimath, respectively.

Summary: Segmentation violation might occur when bus signals feed root-level Outport blocks
Description: Suppose the top level of your model contains an Outport block to which all of the following conditions apply: The Outport block receives a bus signal as its input. The Outport block's Icon display  parameter specifies either Signal name  or Port number and signal name .  In this case, simulating the model or updating the diagram causes a segmentation violation.
Workaround: To work around this issue, specify Port number as the value of the Icon display parameter for affected Outport blocks in the top level of your model.

Summary: Incorrect code generation: generated code for From Workspace blocks in multiple subsystems is incorrect
Description: The Real-Time Workshop code generator creates one function and reuses it for multiple subsystems with different data values, resulting in incorrect code, when all of the following are true: Two non-virtual and masked?subsystems have the same contents and contain From Workspace blocks The parameter values specified in the two subsystem masks are different The From Workspace blocks in the subsystems use the parameters specified in the masks The subsystems have the Real-Time Workshop system code  parameter set to Reusable function  or Auto .
Workaround: Set the Real-Time Workshop system code parameter for the non-virtual and masked subsystems to Inline or Function.

Summary: Simulink corrupts memory and might crash if Goto or From block icons display long signal names
Description: Suppose your model contains Goto or From blocks to which all of the following conditions apply: The block's Icon Display  parameter specifies Signal name  or Tag and signal name . The name of the signal that the block inputs or outputs contains more than 64 characters.  In this case, Simulink corrupts memory; this might result in a crash or other unpredictable behavior.
Workaround: To work around this issue, specify Tag as the value of the Icon Display parameter for Goto and From blocks in your model. You can use the following script to help achieve this task:sn = find_system(modelname, 'SearchDepth', inf, 'FollowLinks', 'on', 'LookUnderMasks', 'all', 'IconDisplay', 'Signal name');tsn = find_system(modelname, 'SearchDepth', inf, 'FollowLinks', 'on', 'LookUnderMasks', 'all', 'IconDisplay', 'Tag and signal name');all_sn = {sn{:} tsn{:}};The cell array all_sn lists all of a model's Goto and From blocks that might trigger this bug. If you want all of these blocks to display the tag, execute the following commands. However, note that you can make permanent changes to library blocks only in their libraries; the commands that follow modify only the local instance of the Goto or From block.for i = 1:length(all_sn)    set_param(all_sn{i}, 'IconDisplay', 'Tag');endAlternatively, you may choose to change only those Goto and From blocks whose input or output signal names contain more than 64 characters.

Summary: Changing the figure orientation from Portrait to Landscape does not affect print preview or printout
Description: After printing from Print Preview several times, if you change the page orientation from Portrait to Landscape, nothing changes on the print preview or the printout - it defaults to Portrait.? This?happens because the setting cannot be changed after a certain number of figures are generated, even if they were not printed.
Workaround: If this happens, close Print Preview and reopen it. Check the page orientation property (and set it to Landscape if it isn't set) before printing.

Summary: Generated code for model containing empty function call subsystem might not compile
Description: The code generated for a model containing a function call subsystem might not compile if all of the following are true: The function call subsystem does not contain executable blocks (for example, contains only Inport, Outport, Ground, or Terminator blocks) The function call initiator is a noninlined S-Function  ?

Summary: Generating code for Bitwise Operator blocks might crash MATLAB
Description: If you use Real-Time Workshop to build a model containing a Bitwise Operator block whose input signal does not match one of the CPU's native types, MATLAB crashes. In this case, MATLAB either fails to leave a crash dump or produces an empty crash dump file. Likely setups include the following: Models where uncommon types are used in bitwise operations (e.g., uint11  or int17_En5 ). Models for Texas Instruments or Analog Devices SHARC targets with 16-16-16-32 or all-32 configurations, where non-16 or non-32 bit signals are fed into a Bitwise Operator block.
Workaround: To work around this issue, cast the input signals of a Bitwise Operator block to one of the CPU's native types. For example, suppose that char, short, int, and long are all 32 bits on your CPU. Insert a Data Type Conversion block before affected Bitwise Operator blocks in your model and convert the signals to a uint32 type.Download the attached zip file and extract the script detect_g396284.m. Use this script to locate in a model the blocks that are likely suspects for triggering this issue.

Summary: Models containing Embedded MATLAB Function blocks do not run in a directory with parentheses in the name.
Description: If your model is in a directory whose name contains parentheses, you cannot run?Embedded MATLAB Function blocks, Stateflow charts, and Truth Table blocks.
Workaround: Place your model in a directory whose name does not contain parentheses.

Summary: Code generation fails with "Specified file does not exist on CCS path" error
Description: When Link for CCS cannot find a file required to generate code, the code generation process fails in?the file?i_exitHookPoint.m  with the following error message:Error building Real-Time Workshop target for block diagram 'Quadia_R0_2_01'. MATLAB error message:Error using ==> callMakeHook.p>callMakeHook at 47The call to ccslink_ert_make_rtw_hook, during the exit hook generated the following error:??? Error using ==> i_exitHookpoint at 88Error while creating the project:Error using ==> ccs.ccsdsp.fileparamparser at 32Specified file does not exist on CCS path The build process will terminate as a result.  The error message indicates that a file needed for project generation cannot be found but does not provide the file name for further diagnosis.
Workaround: Perform the following steps to download and apply a patch that fixes this problem by adding the name of the missing file to the error message. This patch applies only to R2007a and Link for CCS 3.0. The patch verifies your MATLAB and Link for CCS versions.    Download the attached patch installer patch_installer.m and the ZIP archive patch_395359.zip.?    Start MATLAB and enter the following command at the prompt to install the patch:        patch_installer('patch_395359.zip', 'install')     Follow the instructions displayed at the MATLAB command line to complete the installation.The patch modifies the following file in your MATLAB installation--<matlabroot>\toolbox\ccslink\ccslink\@ccs\@ccsdsp\fileparamparser.p.?After you install the patch, the error message in subsequent builds reports the name?of the file that is causing the code generation failure.?

Summary: Rate Limiter block with unsigned inputs produces incorrect results
Description: Suppose your model contains a Rate Limiter block whose input signal has an unsigned data type. If you execute the build process, Real-Time Workshop generates code that returns wrong answers. Similarly, if you simulate the model using an acceleration mode, Simulink produces wrong answers. However, the results that you obtain from Normal mode simulation are correct.
Workaround: To work around this issue, specify only signed data types for a Rate Limiter block's input signal. For example, to alter the signedness of a Rate Limiter block's input signal, add a Data Type Conversion block that specifies a signed data type with precision doubled. You can insert another Data Type Conversion block after the Rate Limiter block to change its output signal back to an unsigned data type.

Summary: Problems exporting and symbolizing geostructs with NaN-valued attributes
Description: Any numeric-valued attribute of a geostructs can be set to a value of NaN. In a shapefile, null values are encoded as blank fields, and in loading a shapefile into a geostruct, shaperead  correctly translates blank fields into 1-by-1 NaN arrays. However, the functions geoshow, makedbfspec , mapshow , and shapewrite  sometimes error when given such data.
Workaround: To correctly handle NaN-valued attributes in shapefiles, replace the function files makedbfspec.m.m, shapewrite.m, and attributes2properties.m in your installation with the versions in the attachment, as follows:    Exit MATLAB.    Make a backup copy of the following files. Summary: Wrong answer or memory corruption can occur with single-precision matrix math library functions rt_MatDivRC_Sgl and rt_MatDivRR_Sgl
Description: The matrix math library functions rt_MatDivRC_Sgl  and rt_MatDivRR_Sgl  contain a call to the memcpy  function in which an incorrect data type is specified (real_T  instead of real32_T ), which generates an incorrect size calculation for the copy. This can cause memory corruption and lead to wrong numerical results or failure during execution. These functions are defined in the static library function files rt_matdivrc_sgl.c  and rt_matdivrr_sgl.c  provided by Real-Time Workshop in matlabroot /rtw/c/libsrc , and are used by the Simulink Gain and Product blocks when matrix signals are in use.
Workaround: To work around this bug, perform the following steps.Note: In the following instructions, matlabroot represents your MATLAB installation root, as returned by the MATLAB command matlabroot.        In your MATLAB installation tree, rename the following files, for example, by adding .old to the end of each file name.            matlabroot/rtw/c/libsrc/rt_matdivrc_sgl.c        matlabroot/rtw/c/libsrc/rt_matdivrr_sgl.c                Download the attached files and place them in the same directory as the files you renamed.        Restart MATLAB.If you want to use the functions rt_MatDivRC_Sgl and rt_MatDivRR_Sgl with Real-Time Workshop host-based executables, you must regenerate the precompiled version of the Real-Time Workshop library that Real-Time Workshop provides for supported compilers and uses during linking. To invoke a regeneration of the library in Real-Time Workshop provided template makefiles (TMFs), you can specify the OPT_OPTS variable to the make command. For example:make_rtw OPT_OPTS=/ZiThe value for OPT_OPTS is compiler-dependent and must be different than the default optimization setting used by the TMF in order to trigger the regeneration of the library. You can determine the default optimization value for your compiler by looking in the matlabroot/rtw/c/tools directory and viewing the DEFAULT_OPT_OPTS variable in the appropriate makefile among the following:    Microsoft Visual C:? vctools.mak    LCC:? lcctools.mak    Borland:? bctools.mak    Watcom:? watctools.mak    UNIX platforms including Mac:? unixtools.mak

Summary: Accelerator or Rapid Accelerator mode:?possible?incorrect results if single-rate model without continuous sample time?uses variable-step solver
Description: A model running in Accelerator mode or Rapid Accelerator mode may produce incorrect results if all of the following conditions are true: The model uses a variable-step solver. The model does not use a continous sample time. The model is single rate. The Max step size  specified on the Solver  pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box is smaller than the discrete sample time.
Workaround: If your model does not contain a continuous block, you can work around this bug by using a fixed-step solver.

Summary: Embedded MATLAB Function blocks with single, non-tunable parameters rebuild too often and sometimes crash.
Description: Simulink models containing Embedded MATLAB Function blocks with single, non-tunable parameters regenerate code for every simulation and crash sporadically. For example, the following parameter values cause this behavior:single(3)single([3 4 5])
Workaround: Use a double, non-tunable parameter and cast it to single type within your MATLAB code.? For example, consider the following code where X is the single parameter:?Y = X + 1To implement the workaround, change X to a double parameter and rewrite the code as follows: Y = single(X) + 1

Summary: Incorrect display of transient model outliers
Description: If you remove outliers for transient models when used as one-stage models, the points do not display correctly. Outliers remain plotted as normal points in the predicted/observed plot. If you display test numbers you see a warning. An error may occur if you try to remove the last point after removing outliers, and it is possible the Model Browser may lock.
Workaround: To avoid these problems, select outliers from the scatter plot instead of predicted/observed plot.Do not try to display test numbers for transient models.

Summary: Warnings returned when using BIOINFORMATICS Toolbox command GPRREAD on GPR files created with GenePix Pro 6.0.1.25
Description: Warnings similiar to the following one are returned by GPRREAD when attempting to read GPR data created using GenePix Pro 6.0.1.25 : In gprread at 208 Warning: Problem readling line: 19727"XM_112077""A_51_P104768"192101546595 ...210 The warning is repeated many times per file.
Workaround: Download the attached file.Quit MATLABRename the file $MATLAB$\toolbox\bioinfo\microarray\gprread.m to $MATLAB$\toolbox\bioinfo\microarray\gprread.m.old.Where $MATLAB is the directory where MATLAB is installed on your machine.Copy the downloaded file to the directory $MATLAB$\toolbox\bioinfo\microarrayRestart MATLABRun the commandrehash toolboxcacheat the MATLAB prompt.

Summary: The order of model parameter names at the command-line is incorrect
Description: There is a bug in the command-line ordering of model parameter names, returned by the property mbcmodel.modelparameters.Names. The order of these names shown at the command-line is not the same as the order shown in the Stepwise GUI. The associated data values (the properties Values, StepwiseStatus, and StepwiseSelection) match the order shown in the Stepwise window. The order of all these properties including Names should match that shown in the Stepwise window.? ?
Workaround: A patch is available to fix this error. To install this fix:    Rename the file? <matlabroot>/toolbox/mbc/mbc/@mbcmodel/@linearmodelparameters/pGetNames.m to? <matlabroot>/toolbox/mbc/mbc/@mbcmodel/@linearmodelparameters/pGetNames.m.old.    Download the file pGetNames.m that is listed in the Attachments section and save it to <matlabroot>/toolbox/mbc/mbc/@mbcmodel/@linearmodelparameters/pGetNames.m.    Restart MATLAB.

Summary: Incorrect range checking for Simulink data objects whose data types specify slope and bias scaling
Description: Simulink data objects, such as Simulink.Parameter  and Simulink.Signal  objects, perform range checking of their values. For instance, Simulink alerts you when the Value  field of a parameter object lies outside a range that corresponds to its specified Minimum  and Maximum  values and data type. However, for data types that specify slope and bias scaling, Simulink Fixed Point incorrectly calculates the representable range and reports an error.

Summary: Bioinformatics Toolbox function mzxmlread? returns a structure with empty fields for the peak data.
Description: Bioinformatics Toolbox function?mzxmlread  returns a structure with empty fields for the peak data.?A warning messages indicates that?one or more?of the fields in the mzXML structure are missing.This shortcoming occurs with software versions earlier than R2007b, when using mass-spectrometry data generated with the output of some spectrometers that?do not?use??<#text>? nodes in the XML document (e.g. standard Shimadzu CFR mass spectrometry instrument).
Workaround: If you are using a version earlier than R2007b,?update the mzxmlread function?by using the following procedure:1. In the MATLAB Command Window type:which mzxmlread.mThis displays the location of the file mzxmlread.m.2. Rename mzxmlread.m to mzxmlread.m.old?3. Download the updated file attached to this bug report to the directory identified with the which command.4. In the MATLAB Command Window typerehash toolbox

Summary: Linear Simulation Tool error occurs when?adding a square wave signal
Description: Adding a square wave signal in the Linear Simulation Tool causes a dependency?error if the Signal Processing Toolbox software?is not installed.

Summary: External mode: Ctrl+T shortcut mapped to "Start Real-Time Code"?rather than?"Connect To Target"
Description: When external mode is selected in the Simulink model window, the Ctrl+T  keyboard shortcut should be remapped from a toggle for Start  and Stop  (simulation) to a toggle for Connect To Target  and Disconnect From Target . Instead, Ctrl+T  is remapped to Start Real-Time Code , which is greyed out when the target is disconnected. As a result, Ctrl+T  cannot be used as a shortcut for connecting to the target.
Workaround: Use the Simulink Simulation menu item Connect To Target instead of the Ctrl+T shortcut.

Summary: The Selector block emits an incorrect message when the starting index is from an input port?and the derived ending?index?is out of bounds
Description: If the Selector block has a dimension set to Starting index(port) , the block calculates the ending index based on the starting index from the index port and the output size specified in the dialog box parameter. If the ending index references a non-existing element in the input signal at port <U> , the block emits an incorrect message. For example, if the following is true:     Selector block has the first dimension configured to Starting index (port)      Size of this dimension of the input signal is 3     Value of the starting index is 2     Output size is 3  The Selector block emits the following run-time error:The second element, 1, at input port Idx3 in 'untitled/Selector1' is not within the range of permissible values (1 through 2) that allows selection of a valid input data element. This error message is incorrect. The correct message should be: The ending index, 4, for dimension 1 in 'untitled/Selector1' is not within the range of permissible values (1 through 3) that allows selection of a valid input data element.

Summary: Error from cosimulation block during code generation: 'cannot change the model while the simulation is running'
Description: It is illegal to change the model during certain code generation phases.? The cosimulation block violates this rule by redrawing its underlying subsystem during code generation.? The error can occur regardless of where the cosimulation block is instantiated in the Simulink model.? All code generation tools may be affected, such as Stateflow HDL Coder, Simulink HDL Coder, RTW, and Filter Design HDL Coder.? An example error message is:Error in 'Jpeg/HDL Cosimulation': Initialization commandscannot be evaluated.? MATLAB error message:? Error using ==> delete_lineCannot change the model 'Jpeg/HDL Cosimulation' while thesimulation is running.Stateflow HDL code generation failed for chart 'Diff/Pred' (#88).
Workaround: The attached files provide patches for the R2006b+ (also known as R2006bd) and R2007a releases.? To install the patch:    Change directory to matlabroot/toolbox/incisive/incisive    Change the name of lfilinklib.mdl to lfilinklib.mdl.old    Download the attachment lfilinklib_<release>.mdl, where release is the version of MATLAB you are patching    Rename the file to lfilinklib.mdl    Restart MATLABOnce the patch is installed, no errors should occur from the cosimulation block during code generation.

Summary: Crash when using spline lookup tables with more rows than columns
Description: Simscape crashes when you use spline lookup tables with more rows than columns.
Workaround: Choose another interpolation method or transpose the data.
