Summary: Input with leading minus sign causes incorrect numerical output in str2angle
Description: Function str2angle  does not return an error when an invalid input like '-115ˇă15''26"N'  is given. Instead a misleading and numerically incorrect output is returned.The coordinate string above is invalid because the leading negative symbol indicates the coordinate is in the southern hemisphere while the trailing "N" indicates that the coordinate is in the northern hemisphere. In fact, str2angle  does not allow leading minus (or plus) signs at all.
Workaround: To work around this issue, replace the str2angle.m function file in your installation with the version in the attachment, as follows:    Exit MATLAB.    Make a backup copy of the following file:    MATLABROOT\matlab\toolbox\map\map\str2angle.m    MATLABROOT represents the name of your MATLAB installation directory, as returned by the matlabroot function.    For example, rename the existing file, adding "old" to the file extension: str2angle.m.old        Copy the version of str2angle.m from the attachment to the directory specified in the previous step.    Restart MATLAB.    Run the rehash toolbox command at the MATLAB prompt.

Summary: MATLAB may crash when calling a function handle using feval.
Description: MATLAB may crash under certain circumstances if both of the following are true:-??You are calling a function handle using feval ?-? That function handle is a result of a temporary expression.For example, either of these commands may cause MATLAB to crash under certain circumstances:??? feval(funcs{i}, data{i});??? feval(str(i).func, str(i).data); You can avoid this potential crash either by using the normal function calling syntax without feval,  or by first storing the function handle in a variable and then evaluating the variable with feval .
Workaround: To work around this problem, replace the command??? feval(funcs{i}, data{i});either by calling the function handle itself (that is, without the evaluation)??? funcs{i}(data{i});or by using feval as shown here:??? f = funcs{i};??? feval(f, data{i});Similarly, you can replace??? feval(str(i).func, str(i).data);with??? str(i).func(str(i).data);or??? f = str(i).func;??? feval(f, str(i).data);

Summary: Invalid output for sbioparamestim when tspan argument doesn't begin with 0.0
Description: The SimBiology function sbioparamestim  gives incorrect results when passed a tspan  vector that doesn't begin with t=0.0. tspan  describes the time points that label the target data xtarget .
Workaround: Add a dummy datapoint at t=0.0 by adding 0.0 to the beginning of the tspan vector and padding the first row of xtarget with zeros. Note that the dummy datapoint will not adversely affect the parameter estimation; the final value of the residual will be larger, but the values of the estimated parameters will be correct.

Summary: Generated ERT S-function code?does not compile if nonvirtual bus input signal has non-Auto storage class.
Description: Real-Time Workshop generates uncompilable ERT S-function code under the following conditions: The source model has a nonvirtual bus input signal with a storage class other than Auto . An ERT-based target is selected for the model on the Real-Time Workshop  pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box. Create Simulink (S-Function) block  is selected on the Interface  pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box.  The same failure occurs if you use subsystem-based S-function code generation, under the following conditions: The selected subsystem has a nonvirtual bus input signal with a storage class other than Auto . Use Embedded Coder  is selected in the dialog box that appears when you select Real-Time Workshop > Generate S-Function  from the Tools menu or the subsystem right-click menu.
Workaround: To work around this bug in R2007b, perform the following steps.Note: In the following instructions, matlabroot represents your MATLAB installation root folder, as returned by the MATLAB command matlabroot.In your MATLAB installation tree, rename or make a backup copy of the following file:matlabroot/rtw/c/tlc/mw/ertsfcnbody.tlcDownload the attached .zip archive file and extract the file ertsfcnbody.tlc from the archive.Place the extracted file in the same folder as the file that you renamed or copied.Restart MATLAB.For earlier releases, please contact Technical Support.

Summary: Using slupdate in analyze mode on a mex S-Function with .dll extension in Windows?can cause a segmentation violation.
Description: Performing the Model Advisor check?Check model, local libraries, and referenced models for known upgrade issues , or using SLUPDATE  in analyze mode?can cause?Simulink to report a segment violation when a custom S-function ends in a .dll extension. ?
Workaround: Use the proper extension for your S-function (as given by the MEXEXT function) to prevent the segment violation from occurring.?However, note that a coding error in your S-Function is the underlying cause of the problem. Debug your S-function to correct the underlying problem.

Summary: Warnings issued due to unintended incompatibility with 'labelunits' of 'dms' and 'dm' in R2007a
Description: In R2007a calls to set a map axes, ax , to display meridian and parallel labels in degrees-minutes of degrees-minutes-seconds form:setm(ax,'labelunits','dm') andsetm(ax,'labelunits','dms') each generate a large set of warnings.? This incompatible change from earlier releases should not have happened.? Mapping Toolbox warns if the? strings 'dms'  and 'dm'  are used to indicate how angles are?or should be encoded, as in angleunits , for example. (See the R2007a release notes for more information.) However,? the use of these strings to indicate the formatting of meridian and parallel labels is legitimate and should not trigger warnings as they do.
Workaround: Before calling setm, simply turn off the warnings using their IDs:warning('off','map:deg2dms:obsolete')warning('off','map:deg2dm:obsolete')warning('off','map:dms2dm:obsolete')warning('off','map:dms2mat:obsolete')warning('off','map:mat2dms:obsolete')warning('off','map:unitstr:obsoleteDMS')warning('off','map:unitstra:obsoleteDMS')

Summary: Slowdown when checking if referenced model?targets are up to date
Description: When you perform menu-based operations on a model with many referenced models, checking that the referenced models' simulation and Real-Time Workshop targets are up to date may be slow.? Menu-based operations include:    Building the model (Build button on the Configuration Parameters > Real-Time Work ?pane,    or Ctrl-B)     Starting simulation (Start button on the model window, Simulation > Start  command,    or Ctrl-T)  The slowdown occurs even if the targets are all up to date, and becomes more noticeable if the number of referenced models is greater than 100.
Workaround: There are two possible workarounds:    If you are sure that all targets are up to date and will remain so, in the Configuration Parameters > Model Referencing pane:            Set Rebuild options to Never        Set Never rebuild targets diagnostic to None??            Use command-line-based operations rather than GUI-based operations to generate code and simulate the model. For example, use:            rtwbuild (model);        sim (model);

Summary: dspdata objects are not compiled properly by the MATLAB Compiler
Description: If you use dspdata objects such as dspdata.psd, dspdata.msspectrum etc. in a function,? the MATLAB compiler will produce a runtime error.

Summary: The xPC Target API function and COM API method,?GetSignalWidth,?returns incorrect results
Description: xPC Target returns incorrect results when a stand-alone xPC Target client application uses the xPC Target API or xPC Target COM API to call the GetSignalWidth  function or method.

Summary: Simulating a model that contains Integrator blocks with external initial condition ports might crash Simulink
Description: Suppose your model contains either an Integrator or a Discrete-Time Integrator block whose Initial condition source  parameter specifies external . Simulink might crash when simulating this model if all of the following conditions apply to the Integrator or Discrete-Time Integrator block's initial condition port:     A signal from a Constant or an IC block feeds the initial condition port.     The Constant block's Constant value  parameter or the IC block's Initial value  parameter specifies a data type that differs from that which the Integrator or Discrete-Time Integrator block expects. For example, a Constant block's Constant value  parameter specifies int8(10) ; however, its signal feeds the initial condition port of a Discrete-Time Integrator block that expects a double  data type.  The stack trace that Simulink generates in this case begins as follows: [0] libmwsimulink.dll:struct slErrMsg_tag * __cdecl sluGetICFromBlockInput(class slBlock const *,int,int,void * *)(0x2f4447a0, 2, 0x20e52e28, 17) + 409 bytes  [1] libmwsimulink.dll:struct slErrMsg_tag * __cdecl DiscreteIntgDoPostPropagationTasksFcn(class slBlock *)(0, 0x00d17b6c, 0x7a2b36da, 0x2f4447a0) + 2458 bytes  [2] libmwsimulink.dll:struct slErrMsg_tag * __cdecl BlockDoPostPropagationTasks(class slBlock *)(0x2f4447a0, 0, 0x2f4447a0, 0x21153960 "?;¨˘7 ?¨˘7") + 37 bytes  [3] libmwsimulink.dll:struct slErrMsg_tag * __cdecl DoBlockSpecificPostPropagationTasks(class slBlock *)(0x004447a0 "?¨¤??¨¤??¨¤??¨¤??¨¤??¨¤??¨¤??¨¤??¨¤??¨¤??¨¤..", 0x2f38d620, 0x2f443280, 0x210deee0) + 42 bytes  [4] libmwsimulink.dll:struct slErrMsg_tag * __cdecl DoPostPropagationTasks(struct slGraph_tag *,class slModel *)(0x2f38d620, 0x2c367fa0, 0x2f38b620, 0x2f4171e0) + 90 bytes  [5] libmwsimulink.dll:struct slErrMsg_tag * __cdecl DoPostPropagationTasks(struct slGraph_tag *,class slModel *)(0x2f38b620, 0x2c367fa0, 0x301f4020, 0x214e6680) + 77 bytes
Workaround: To work around this issue, ensure that the data type specified by the Constant block's Constant value parameter or the IC block's Initial value parameter is the same as that expected by the Integrator or Discrete-Time Integrator block.

Summary: Out of Memory error occurs when re-using MWCellArrays containing large numeric?arrays?in?a .NET application
Description: In the following code, elements of an MWCellArray are populated with large numeric arrays:?MWCellArray cellArray= new MWCellArray(1,50);for (int i = 1; i <= 50; i++)? {???? cellArray[i]= new MWNumericArray(512, 512, largeArray);? }Before reusing the cellArray the assigned elements are first disposed of as follows:for (int i = 1; i <= 50; i++)? {???? cellArray[i].Dispose();? }However, repeated iterations of these steps results in the following error message:MWMCR::EvaluateFunction error: Out of memory
Workaround: The?problem?is that the Dispose method simply requests that memory for the numeric array be?freed but it does not remove the association of the MWNumericArray with the cell array itself.??While this association remains, the MCR will not free up the memory.? You can remove this association (as in MATLAB) by assigning an empty array to the cell element.? So the code segment used above:for (int i = 1; i <= 50; i++)? {???? cellArray[i].Dispose();? }should be changed to:for (int i = 1; i <= 50; i++)? {???? cellArray[i]= MWNumericArray.Empty;?? }This should fix the problem.

Summary: A crash?can occur? when using rsimgetrtp to create the run time parameter structure for a model containing "to file" blocks
Description: MATLAB may crash when using the following command to create the run time parameter struct  in a model containing to file  blocks:??? rtp = rsimgetrtp('model_with_to_file_block'); The following stack trace is issued:Stack Trace: ??[0] ntdll.dll:0x7c901010(0x00d1dc20, 8, 1, 0) ??[1] libmwsimulink.dll:struct slErrMsg_tag * __cdecl ToFileOutputFcn(class slBlock *,class slSimBlock_tag *)(0x0e90a2a0, 1, 0x00d1dc80 " ??", 0x7a292e17) + 220 bytes ??[2] libmwsimulink.dll:public: virtual struct slErrMsg_tag * __thiscall LegacyDirectRunTimeDispatcher::Output(class slSimBlock_tag *)(0x270fba60, 0x0c0c2780, 0x0e96c320, 0x279e1d00 "¨¤t?&") + 14 bytes?
Workaround: To prevent this error from occurring, remove all to file blocks from your model and use either root level outputs or signal logging?to log signals from the Simulation.

Summary: ADC conversion results contain previous sample time values
Description: When the ADC is used with the?Start of conversion  parameter set to?Software , stored results may show values collected during the previous time the ADC conversion was triggered.
Workaround: 1.?????? Download the attached patch installer patch_installer.m and the ZIP archive patch_388604.zip. 2.?????? This patch is available for MATLAB releases R2006b and R2007a and?may damage any other Matlab releases. 3.?????? Start MATLAB and type ver at the prompt to verify which version of MATLAB you are using. 4.?????? Enter the following command at the MATLAB prompt to install the patch:?patch_installer('patch_388604.zip', 'install')?5.?????? Enter the following command to uninstall the patch?patch_installer('patch_388604.zip', 'uninstall')?

Summary: Why do I receive a "MEX Authentication file read Failed" at runtime with a MATLAB Compiler 4.6 (R2007a)-built application on 64-bit Linux?
Description: When running an application built with?MATLAB Compiler 4.6 (R2007a)?that calls certain functions, such as DATENUM or?GAMMA,?on a 64-bit Linux machine, I receive the following?runtime error:? MEX Authentication file read Failed!??  ??? DATENUM failed. Invalid MEX-file??<matlabroot> /toolbox/matlab/timefun/private/dtstr2dtnummx.mexa64
Workaround: This is a bug in MATLAB Compiler 4.6(R2007a)?on the 64-bit Linux platform. This?results?from an incompatibility between?certain MEX files and?corresponding authentication files which are shipped as part of MATLAB Compiler.To resolve this problem, you?need to supply the correct authentication file for each MEX file that is broken.?The attached?TAR file includes the authentication files for the broken?MEX files.?Please extract the contents of this?TAR file to your?<matlab>/toolbox/compiler/mcr directory.

Summary: UIGETFILE function on a win64 machine sometimes does not open a pop-up menu to select the file in R2007a
Description: If you use the UIGETFILE  function in R2007a on a win64 machine, it does not always open the pop-up menu from which you can select the file.
Workaround: This is a timing issue in java dialogs and you can work around this by flushing the event dispatch thread appropriately. To flush the event dispatch thread, issue drawnow commands before UIGETFILE. You can also turn off the optimizations with a feature('accel','off') to work around this issue.

Summary: Misleading error message when checking in faulty transient models
Description: When you try to check in a transient model to Model-Based Calibration Toolbox and there is a fault with either the Simulink model or the transient model MATLAB file which prevents the model evaluating correctly, you see an error message like the following in the MATLAB Command Window. This occurs because of a bug in the file xregtransient/eval.m.??? Output argument "Y" (and maybe others) not assigned during call to "toolbox\mbc\mbcmodels\@xregtransient\eval.m (i_HiddenEval)".
Workaround: A patch is available that will fix this error. To install this fix:    Rename the file <matlabroot>/toolbox/mbc/mbcmodels/@xregtransient/eval.m to <matlabroot>/toolbox/mbc/mbcmodels/@xregtransient/eval.m.old.    Download the file eval.m that is listed in the Attachments section and save it to <matlabroot>/toolbox/mbc/mbcmodels/@xregtransient/eval.m.    Restart MATLAB.

Summary: Contour computations for large data grids can run out of memory and cause a segmentation fault
Description: When the contour  function runs out of memory, MATLAB can unexpectedly exit rather than signal an error. For example, [X,Y,Z]=sphere(4000);[cout]=contour(Z,-0.9:0.001:0.9);  After several minutes of computation, MATLAB fails with a segmentation violation. ?

Summary: Incorrect code generation: data type conversion code not generated with Data Type Conversion block in model
Description: Real-Time Workshop removes Data Type Conversion blocks during code generation, eliminating data type conversions, if all of the following conditions are met for the Data Type Conversion block:     The Output data type  parameter is a fixed-point type.     The Input and output to have equal  parameter is set to Stored Integer (SI) .     The input and output storage container types are the same.     The bit size of the output is smaller than the bit size of the input.  For example, a signal is not converted from an int8  to a sfix5  if the sfix5  storage container type is an int8 , and the Input and output to have equal  parameter is set to Stored Integer (SI) .

Summary: Functions BLASTNCBI and GETBLAST do not return the correct number of hits specified with the property ALIGNMENTS.
Description: With the current version of the NCBI BLAST server, functions BLASTNCBI and GETBLAST do not return the correct number of hits specified with the property ALIGNMENTS.
Workaround: Quit MATLAB.Rename the file $MATLAB$\toolbox\bioinfo\bioinfo\blastncbi.m to $MATLAB$\toolbox\bioinfo\bioinfo\blastncbi.m.old Rename the file $MATLAB$\toolbox\bioinfo\bioinfo\getblast.m to $MATLAB$\toolbox\bioinfo\bioinfo\getblast.m.old?? where $MATLAB$ is the directory where MATLAB is installed on your machine.Download the attached MATLAB files to the directory $MATLAB$\toolbox\bioinfo\bioinfo\Restart MATLAB.At the MATLAB prompt, run the following command: rehash toolboxcache

Summary: The Spectrum Scope block errors out when data type override is set to Scaled doubles
Description: The FFT block used within the Spectrum Scope block gives the following error when the Data type override  parameter on the Fixed-Point Tool is set to Scaled doubles :Error reported by S-function 'sdspfft2' in 'generic_test1/Spectrum Scope1/Periodogram/Magnitude FFT/FFT': This block does not support 'Scaled doubles' for the 'Data type override' parameter in the Simulink Fixed-Point interface. To access this parameter, click Tools->Fixed-Point Settings."
Workaround: To work around this issue, use the attached files:    32-bit Windows platforms -- sdspfft2.mexw32 and sdspfft2.mexw32.csf    64-bit Linux platforms -- sdspfft2.mexa64 and sdspfft2.mexa64.csfDownload the appropriate files for your platform and place them in the directory matlabroot\toolbox\dspblks\dspmexwhere matlabroot is the path returned to you when you type matlabroot at the MATLAB command line.Type clear mex at the MATLAB command line to pick up the new files if you have a MATLAB session running before you replace the files.

Summary: External mode: models with names?longer than?49 characters cannot connect
Description: When your Simulink model is operating in external mode and you connect to target code, MATLAB performs an eval  of the generated file model _targ_data_map.m  (where model   represents the name of your model). MATLAB requires eval ed MATLAB file names to contain 63 or fewer characters. If your model name contains 50 or more characters, the generated file name model _targ_data_map.m  exceeds 63 characters, and you cannot successfully connect to target code.
Workaround: To work around this bug, rename your model such that the name contains 49 or fewer characters. This will allow you to successfully connect to target code in external mode.

Summary: Reshape block might generate errors related to port widths and signal dimensions
Description: Suppose your model contains a Reshape block whose     Input signal is a matrix of size M-by-N     Output signal is a vector of width W  When you update the diagram or start the simulation, the Reshape block might generate errors related to port widths and signal dimensions. In this case, the Reshape block incorrectly assumes that its input signal is a column vector of size W-by-1. For example, this might happen when the Reshape block is connected to a Selector block that explicitly specifies W as the size of its input port.
Workaround: You can work around this issue by determining the actual dimensions of the signal that the Reshape block receives. Specify these dimensions explicitly for the Reshape block input signal using either of the following methods:    Insert a Signal Specification block before the Reshape block. In the Signal Specification block dialog box, specify a value for the Dimensions parameter.    Name the Reshape block input signal, such that it resolves to a Simulink.Signal object. Specify a value for the Dimensions attribute of the signal object.

Summary: Cannot specify?initial values?for Stateflow Truth Table data
Description: The data dialog for Stateflow Truth Tables does not provide a field for specifying initial values.
Workaround: Specify initial values for Truth Table data in the Contents pane of the Model Explorer.

Summary: Simulink crashes during simulation when certain signals are logged in a submodel.
Description: When a parent model is simulated, Simulink will crash if all of the following are true:1. A signal is marked for logging within a referenced model.2. The data type of the logged signal is a user-defined data type.????Example: a?custom data type registered from a user written S-function.
Workaround: Within a referenced model, do not log any signal that has a user-defined data type.

Summary: dicomread errors when reading files containing private metadata between repeated groups
Description: When I try to open a DICOM file using the dicomread  function I get the following error: >> X = dicomread('standard.dcm')??? Index exceeds matrix dimensions.Error in ==> dicomread>getOverlays at 1071    overlays(:,:,1,p) = reshape(tmp(1:(cols * rows)), [cols, rows])';Error in ==> dicomread>newDicomread at 266  [overlays, X] = getOverlays(X, metadata);Error in ==> dicomread at 82  [X, map, alpha, overlays] = newDicomread(msgname, varargin{:}); However, I can read the same DICOM file using the following command. X = dicomread('standard.dcm', 'raw', 1);
Workaround: It's possible to work around this error by providing extra arguments to dicomread. For example, both of these commands will prevent the error:X = dicomread('standard.dcm', 'frames', 'all');X = dicomread('standard.dcm', 'raw', true);These commands use an older, somewhat slower parser which correctly handles files that contain private metadata in the range normally reserved for repeated groups.

Summary: Simulink leaks memory after receiving a message reporting "Too many errors."
Description: Memory can be consumed but not freed when working with a Simulink model that has several hundred warnings and errors.?This can result in increasingly more memory being consumed after each successive update diagram operation.? Terminating the MATLAB session frees the consumed memory. Simulink reports this memory leak by issuing the following message: Too many errors.
Workaround: Correct the model to reduce the number of warnings and errors below the point where the Too many errors message is issued. This indicates that the memory leak is no longer present.

Summary: Ghostscript directory not included in MCR
Description: If Ghostscript directory ?matlab/sys/gs8x is  not included in the MCR, Ghostscript will not run.
Workaround: Copy the contents of the matlab/sys/gx8s directory into the MCR.

Summary: Code generation might fail for a Lookup Table (2-D) block whose row index specifies a data object
Description: Real-Time Workshop fails to generate code for a Simulink model containing a Lookup Table (2-D) block, for which all of the following conditions apply:     The Row index input values  parameter specifies a noninlined data object, e.g., a Simulink.Parameter  object whose storage class property is any value but Auto .     The Column index input values  parameter specifies an inlined vector of evenly-spaced breakpoints, e.g., [0 1 2] .     The Lookup method  parameter specifies Interpolation-Use End Values .  In this case, the Real-Time Workshop build process aborts after displaying the following error: Error: File: matlabroot\rtw\c\tlc\fixpt\fixptlook2D.tlc Line: 551 Column: 39The + operator only works on numeric arguments
Workaround: You can work around this issue using any of the following techniques:    For affected Lookup Table (2-D) blocks, specify the Column index input values parameter as a noninlined data object, e.g., a Simulink.Parameter object whose storage class property is any value but Auto.    For affected Lookup Table (2-D) blocks, specify the Lookup method parameter as any value other than Interpolation-Use End Values.    For affected Lookup Table (2-D) blocks, specify the Column index input values parameter as a vector whose breakpoints are not evenly spaced.    On the Optimization panel of the Configuration Parameters dialog, uncheck the Inline parameters option.

Summary: Accessing the CompiledPortFrameData parameter of a Model block that references a function-call model causes a segmentation violation
Description: If a model contains a Model block that references a function-call model, the following causes a segmentation violation: model([],[],[],'compile');    get_param('pathToModelBlock', 'CompiledPortFrameData');

Summary: Generated MATLAB code for a Direct-Form I, Second-Order Sections fixed-point filter?designed?in?FDATool produces a hard error
Description: If a Direct-Form I, Second-Order Sections filter is designed and converted to fixed point in FDATool, the generated MATLAB file?for the filter?will?produce a hard error.

Summary: Target PC crashes if?an attempt is made?to encode an RT->RT message with Condor 1553 Encode BC Message block
Description: If you attempt to encode an RT->RT message with the  Condor 1553 Encode BC Message block, the target PC will crash.
Workaround: To work around this issue, download and install the attached files as follows. These files also correct the mask for the Condor 1553 Encode BC Message block.    Quit MATLAB.    Download the attached files, condor1553encodebcmsg.c and mcondor1553encodebcmsg.m.    Place the new files in the directory:    matlabroot\toolbox\rtw\targets\xpc\target\build\xpcblocks        Delete the previous build directory for the model.    Restart MATLAB.    Rebuild your model.

Summary: A model hierarchy may fail to build if it contains a referenced model that contains a logged or testpointed bus signal
Description: A model hierarchy may fail to build, and therefore cannot simulate,?if it contains a referenced model for which all of the following are true:    The referenced model?executes in Accelerator mode.     The referenced model contains?a bus signal that is logged or testpointed.     The bus signal contains a muxed signal as one of its elements.  When this problem occurs, error messages similar to the following appear:### Updating model reference SIM target for model: referenced    Error: File: matlabroot/rtw/c/tlc/mw/capilib.tlc Line: 500 Column: 74     The specified index (7) was out of the range 0 - 6     Error: File: matlabroot/rtw/c/tlc/mw/capilib.tlc Line: 500 Column: 82     Ambiguous reference to SignalHierarchyDescriptor - must use array index to refer to one of multiple scopes

Summary: Auto-scaling does not work reliably with Stateflow fixed-point data
Description: Logging minimum and maximum values for fixed-point data was based on an old data type property, which could get out of sync with the actual data types.?In this case,?fixed-point auto-scaling?was not always triggered for Stateflow fixed-point data.

Summary: An application that calls two mcc generated libraries, each using fopen, causes a crash.
Description: If two mcc-generated components each call fopen, and these two components are used within a single application, the latter call to fopen will crash.

Summary: Re-deploying Web Application using Java Builder component on Tomcat may fail
Description: If the server administrator re-deploys a Web application that uses the MCR and the Tomcat App Manager UI, the server may encounter a segmentation fault.? This may occur if Tomcat is running on a UNIX platform (including Mac OS X), and there is no X11 DISPLAY available.? The error message looks like: INFO: Undeploying context [/cspirs-irma]Jun 6, 2007 10:16:51 PM org.apache.catalina.startup.HostConfig deployWARINFO: Deploying web application archive cspirs-irma.war------------------------------------------------------------------------?????? Segmentation violation detected at Wed Jun? 6 22:16:51 2007------------------------------------------------------------------------Configuration:? MATLAB Version:?? 7.4.0.287 (R2007a) MATLAB License:?? unknown? Operating System: SunOS 5.10 Generic_125100-08 sun4u? Virtual Machine:? Java 1.6.0_01 with Sun Microsystems Inc. Java HotSpot(TM) 64-Bit Server VM mixed mode? Default Charset:? US-ASCIIRegister State: g0 = 0000000000000000?? o0 = 000000010109bd10? g1 = ffffffff28644600?? o1 = ffffffff2bde19c0? g2 = 00000000000006a8?? o2 = 0000000000000280? g3 = 0000000000000400?? o3 = ffffffff2bde1978? g4 = 00000000001e1da4?? o4 = 000000000013c6bc? g5 = 00000000d79bb800?? o5 = 0000000000000000? g6 = 0000000000000000?? sp = ffffffff125f91f1? g7 = ffffffff16425c00?? o7 = ffffffff26a52388 l0 = 0000000000000230?? i0 = 0000000000000012? l1 = 0000000000000330?? i1 = ffffffff125f9bc8? l2 = 0000000101584fa0?? i2 = ffffffffffffffff l3 = 000000010109bd10?? i3 = fffffffffffffff8 l4 = 0000000000000000?? i4 = 0000000000000000 l5 = 0000000001000000?? i5 = 00000001017a18b1? l6 = ffffffff7eef2004?? fp = ffffffff125f9301? l7 = ffffffff26c34108?? i7 = ffffffff26a4c1e0? pc = ffffffff28644608? ccr = 0000000000000099 Stack Trace:? [? 0] ffffffff26a4c1e0 /opt/MATLAB/MATLAB_Component_Runtime/v76/bin/sol64/libmwmcos.so+1360352?(mcosConvertArrayToExternal+042584)?[? 1] ffffffff26a5e81c /opt/MATLAB/MATLAB_Component_Runtime/v76/bin/sol64/libmwmcos.so+1435676?(__1c9uX__unnamed_gCvHsGHsvFG2MJcheck_rhs4nLmxArray_tag__6FippTA_v_+003972)??[? 2] ffffffff26a54e5c /opt/MATLAB/MATLAB_Component_Runtime/v76/bin/sol64/libmwmcos.so+1396316?(__1cQMfunction_handleNget_signature6MrnDstdGvector4nTmdFunctionSignature_n0BJallocator4n0C______v_+002172)??[? 3] ffffffff26a58abc /opt/MATLAB/MATLAB_Component_Runtime/v76/bin/sol64/libmwmcos.so+1411772?(mdFreeDefaultObjectSystem+005708)??[? 4] ffffffff28642fac /opt/MATLAB/MATLAB_Component_Runtime/v76/bin/sol64/libmwm_dispatcher.so+274348?(__1cLMdispatcherSconstructor_helper6MrnOMobject_system_pkc4pnJmdLogging_pnZmdFunctionHandleInterface_pnSmdPathNotification_p4_v_+001532)??[snip] MATLAB is exiting because of fatal error Non-signal thread entered the MATLAB signal handler.Please follow the steps in the workaround and report this problem to The MathWorks as soon as possible.The next time MATLAB is launched under typical usage, a dialog box will open to help you send the error log to The MathWorks.
Workaround: Make sure that Tomcat runs in an environment where an X11 server is available, and ensure that the DISPLAY environment variable is set.

Summary: External mode: connecting to target program with blocks that register fixed-point data types fails with memory allocation error
Description: If you configure a model for external mode simulation, and if the model contains blocks with fixed-point parameters for which fixed-point data types are registered during model startup, connecting to the target program fails with a memory allocation error.

Summary: Non-sequential inports at top level of model cause SegV upon saving the model
Description: If the inports at the top-level of a Simulink model have port numbers that are not strictly sequential (e.g., 1, 2 , 5),? when you try to save the model you see the error "Found an unexpected exception" and a segmentation violation is generated.
Workaround: Change the port numbers of top-level inports to be strictly sequential and re-save the model.

Summary: Segmentation violation sometimes caused by WindowButtonUpFcn that resizes a figure
Description: Having a WindowButtonUpFcn  that triggers a call to drawnow  which in turn resizes the figure can cause a segmentation violation in certain circumstances.?The segmentation violation might happen only if the?figure is simultaneously trying to close.

Summary: Sporadic crash when using Simulink menus
Description: Using the Simulink menus (in particular, the context menu) can crash MATLAB.? To tell if a MATLAB crash is caused by this problem, examine the the stack trace resulting from the crash. It should contain the following line:Stack Trace: ?? [0] libmwsimulink.dll:bool __cdecl get_selected(struct MWObject_tag * *)
Workaround: Download the attached file and copy it into matlabroot/toolbox/shared/dastudio, replacing the version already there.

Summary: Block? ports are sometimes offscreen
Description: As ports are added or removed from the block, the block's size and position changes. This can cause the first port of a block? at the top or left of the screen to move offscreen.
Workaround: Save and reload the model.

Summary: Periodic tasks do not run when executing generated code for TI C672x processors
Description: If the code you generate from a?Simulink model contains periodic sample rates, the periodic tasks in the generated code do not run properly when you execute the program on the processor. This situation occurs only for the TI C672x processors. Examining the generated code shows that CPU interrupt line 15?receives interrupts from the RTI module that is responsible for generating periodic interrupts. To run the periodic tasks properly, CPU interrupt line 5 should receive the RTI interrupts.
Workaround: Download the attached patch installer patch_installer.m and the ZIP archive patch_383368.zip. This patch applies only to MATLAB software release R2007a.To install the patch, follow these steps.    Start MATLAB.    Enter the following command at the prompt to install the patch:     patch_installer('patch_383368.zip', 'install')         Follow the instructions on the MATLAB command line to complete the patch installation.?

Summary: Adjusted r-square calculation differs in the stepwise and regstats functions
Description: The calculation of adjusted r-square in the stepwise  function follows a different definition than the one used in the regstats  function.?The difference is most noticeable when the number of observations is small relative to the number of estimated coefficients.?The definition used in regstats  is more widely?accepted, and the definition used in stepwise  is being treated as a bug.
Workaround: To make the calculation in stepwise consistent with the calculation in regstats, create a local copy of the Statistics Toolbox stepwise function (or edit your installed version), and remove the "+1" in the numerator of the ratio in the following lines of code:(line 661 in version 6.1 of Statistics Toolbox)adjrsq = 1 - (1-rsq)*((stats.df0+stats.dfe+1)/stats.dfe);(line 923 in version 6.1 of Statistics Toolbox) outst.adjrsq = 1 - (1-outst.rsq)*((stats.df0+stats.dfe+1)/stats.dfe);

Summary: Incorrect linearization of Rate Transition blocks that use double-buffering
Description: Suppose your model includes a Rate Transition block that     Operates using double buffers, indicated by the label Db_buf  appearing on the block icon.     Outputs a signal that is the input to a block operating at a continuous sample time.  If you linearize the model using, e.g., the dlinmod  function, Simulink returns a linear state-space model that incorrectly represents the block as a continuous-time integrator. However, the block should be represented as either a unit delay or a gain of one.

Summary: Prometheus/Athena, Diamond MM, and Diamond MM-16 Analog Input?and Prometheus/Athena Analog Output blocks indicate that the boards do not exist on the target PC when they do exist
Description: Any attempt to use the Prometheus/Athena, Diamond MM, and Diamond MM-16? Analog Input (A/D) and Prometheus/Athena Analog Output (D/A) blocks for ?these boards cause an error.
Workaround: There is an issue in a macro used by the block driver. To work around this issue:    Quit MATLAB.    Download the attached file, xpcimports.h.    Place the new file in the directory:    matlabroot\toolbox\rtw\targets\xpc\target\build\xpcblocks\include        Restart MATLAB.    Rebuild your model.

Summary: Subsystem right-click build does not support tunable parameters for mask parameters if identically named
Description: During right-click builds of subsystems, tunable parameters are ignored if both of the following conditions are true:     A tunable parameter is used by a mask parameter of the subsystem being built     The tunable parameter has the same name as the mask parameter  For example, suppose that variable K is defined in the base workspace as follows: K = Simulink.Parameter;K.Value = 3;K.RTWInfo.StorageClass = 'ExportedGlobal'; and suppose that mask parameter K in the subsystem refers to workspace variable K. If you right-click the subsystem and select Real-Time Workshop > Build Subsystem  or Real-Time Workshop > Generate S-Function , K will not appear as a tunable parameter in the generated code.
Workaround: To work around this bug, do either of the following:        Place the subsystem inside another subsystem and generate code from the new subsystem            Rename either the workspace variable or the mask parameter so that they do not have the same name

Summary: Clearing "Generate makefile"?model configuration option?fails to?override user-specified "Make command" parameter??
Description: Clearing the Generate makefile  option on the Real-Time Workshop  pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box specifies that the Real-Time Workshop build of the model should not generate a makefile for compiling the generated code. Clearing this option should cause any user-specified Make command  to be ignored. However, if Generate makefile  is cleared and the user has entered a custom make command in the Make command  parameter, the custom make command is used rather than ignored.
Workaround: To work around this bug, change the value of the Make command parameter to its default, make_rtw, before clearing the Generate makefile option and building the model.

Summary: Crash in x86CodeGenerator::GenerateBuiltinCall
Description: A rare crash sometimes occurs in x86CodeGenerator::GenerateBuiltinCall if the JIT cannot allocate memory or set the execute bit on a section of code.
Workaround: Turn off the JIT by using "feature jit off"

Summary: Color-based segmentation demo includes a confusing step that takes mean of a single value
Description: The "Color-Based Segmentation Using K-Means Clustering" demo, in Step 6, includes an operation that is unnecessary and confusing because it involves taking the mean of a single value.
Workaround: Replace the ipexhistology.m demo file in your current MATLAB installation with the updated version contained in the attached file.Exit MATLAB.Make a back-up copy of the ipexhistology.m file in your existing installation. For example, rename the file adding ".old" to the file name. The following gives the full path to the file. matlabroot represents the name of your MATLAB installation directory. matlabroo\toolbox\images\imdemos\ipexhistology.mDownload the attached file, extract ipexhistology.m, and store it in the directory mentioned in the previous step.Restart MATLAB.Run the following command at the MATLAB prompt:? rehash toolbox

Summary: Simulink may crash when logging bus signals within a referenced model
Description: If a bus signal within a referenced model is logged, executing the model may crash Simulink if the following are true:    The bus signal being logged contains at least two elements that are bus signals.     Two or more of the bus signals nested within the main bus signal are unnamed.
Workaround: Name all unnamed bus signals within a bus signal that is being logged.

Summary: Bus Creator block does not display the propagated signal name from the output port of a Two-Way Connection block
Description: When a Bus Creator block has an input signal coming from the output port of a Two-Way Connection block, the Bus Creator dialog doesn't show the propagated signal name. This happens because a propagated signal name coming out of a Two-Way Connection block is enclosed in a pair of angle brackets, and is therefore treated as a signal label, and not a signal name, by the Bus Creator block.
Workaround: You?can override the propagated name and give it a name without the angle bracket ('<'), and then it will show up correctly in the Bus Creator dialog.

Summary: Vector CANape returns "Parameter out of range" error?when xPC Target execution time?is observed
Description: Vector CANape returns "Parameter out of range" errors while monitoring the xPC Target exection time parameter, xExecutionTime .
Workaround: For the R2006a release (xPC Target 2.9) and later, to work around this issue:    Quit MATLAB.    Download the attached file, xcpforxpc.dll.    Copy the new file into the following two directories:            CANape\exec, (your CANape installation)                matlabroot\toolbox\rtw\targets\xpc\api                    Restart MATLAB.    Rebuild your model.

Summary: truesize function help contains typographical error
Description: The help for truesize  specifies the input arguments as [MROWS NCOLS] but later in the description it says [MROWS MCOLS].?It should use MCOLS throughout.

Summary: Function geotiffinfo gives incorrect length units for projections not in meters
Description: If a projection defined in a GeoTIFF file is in units other than meters, the values of the following fields in the structure returned by function geotiffinfo  will be incorrect:    UOMLength      GeoTIFFCodes.UOMLength      CornerCoords.Lat      CornerCoords.Lon   In addition, if the structure returned by geotiffinfo  is then used with functions projfwd  or projinv , the output coordinate values will be incorrect.?
Workaround: If you need to project from latitude-longitude to x and y coordinates in units other than meters, then, after calling function projfwd, convert from meters using function unitsratio.If you need to unproject x and y coordinates that are in units other than meters, convert to meters using function unitsratio prior to using function projinv, .

Summary: controlchart error for?I chart with one value per subgroup
Description: The controlchart  function can produce an error when trying to create an I chart with grouped data having exactly one data point per group.?This error also occurs when the data input is a timeseries  object, even when there is no subgrouping specified.?The problem does not?occur if there are additional charts, such as MA or MR, specified along with the I chart.
Workaround: For data in an ordinary array, omit the grouping variable when there is just one value per group:?? x = randn(10,1);   g = (1:10)';   controlchart(x,g,'chart','i')??% error?? controlchart(x,'chart','i')??? % no errorFor data in a timeseries object, supply the data vector rather than the entire object:t = timeseries(x)   controlchart(t,'chart','i')       % error   controlchart(t.data,'chart','i')  % no error

Summary: Problems reading non-integer grids with acrgridread
Description: The acrgridread  function encounters two problems when reading an Arc ASCII grid file if the Z-values in the file are not integers:    The function may stop after reading only some of the grid elements     Values read in are truncated to integers
Workaround: To work around this issue, replace the arcgridread.m function file with the version in the attachment, as follows:    Exit MATLAB.    Make a backup copy of the following file.    MATLABROOT\matlab\toolbox\map\mapdisp\arcgridread.m    MATLABROOT represents the name of your MATLAB installation directory, as returned by the matlabroot function.    For example, rename the existing file, adding "old" to the file extension: arcgridread.m.old    Copy the version of arcgridread.m from the attachment into the directory specified in the previous step.    Restart MATLAB.    Run the rehash toolbox command at the MATLAB prompt.

Summary: Using a MEX compiler other than lcc may cause Stateflow builds to generate errors on localized Windows machines
Description: When you use mex -setup  to specify a compiler, the command stores a compiler options file (mexopts.bat ) in a user profile directory (returned by the prefdir  command). On Windows, if the name of the user profile directory contains international, non-ASCII characters, Stateflow may not be able to find the compiler options file and, therefore, generate errors when trying to build the simulation target.
Workaround: ?Copy the mexopts.bat file from the user profile directory to the current working directory using this command:copyfile(fullfile(prefdir,'mexopts.bat'),pwd)

Summary: imdisplayrange returns error after being deleted when target image's cdata, cdatamapping, or axes clim is updated
Description: If you display an image, call imdisplayrange  to associate a display range tool with the image, and then delete the display range tool, you can still receive an error message from imdisplayrange  when you change the image's cdata, cdatamapping, or the axes clim properties.
Workaround: To workaround this issue, replace the version of imdisplayrange in your existing MATLAB installation with the updated version contained in the attached file. Exit MATLAB.Rename the following file, adding the extension ".old" to the file name. matlabroot is the name of your MATLAB installation directory. matlabroot\toolbox\images\imuitools\imdisplayrange.mDownload the attached file, extract imdisplayrange.m, and store it in the directory mentioned in the previous step.Restart MATLAB.Run the following command at the MATLAB prompt:? rehash toolbox.  ?

Summary: Build process might fail for referenced model that contains Lookup Table block
Description: The Real-Time Workshop build process might fail for a referenced model when all of the following apply: The referenced model contains a Lookup Table block. The data types of the inputs and output of the Lookup Table block are user-defined.

Summary: Error?using Import Data?in?Distribution Fitting Tool
Description: In?Distribution Fitting Tool, selecting Import Data ?from the File  menu may cause a java.lang.NullPointerException error.
Workaround: Do not use Import Data?from the File menu. Instead,?click on?the Data button. The same functionality is available, without the error.

Summary: Incorrect code reference to?absolute time?generated if asynchronous?subystem includes non-inlined S-function
Description: Incorrect code is generated for an asynchronous subystem if the subsystem includes an S-function block that references a non-inlined S-function. When a block in the asynchronous subsystem accesses absolute time or task time, the index of the pointer to the time vector is out of the valid range. All blocks that reside within the asynchronous subsystem and that directly or indirectly make use of task time or absolute time (using TLC function LibGetTaskTimeFromTID ) are affected by this problem.
Workaround: To work around this bug in release R14sp2, R14sp3, R2006a, R2006b, or R2007a, perform the following steps:Note: In the following instructions, matlabroot represents your MATLAB installation root, as returned by the MATLAB command matlabroot.        In your MATLAB installation tree, rename the following file, for example, by adding .old to the end of the file name.    matlabroot/rtw/c/tlc/mw/rtmdldefs.tlc            Download the rtmdldefs_release.tlc attachment with the _release suffix that corresponds to your release version (for example, for R2007a, download rtmdldefs_R2007a.tlc).            Rename the downloaded file to rtmdldefs.tlc and place the file at the location indicated below:    matlabroot/rtw/c/tlc/mw/rtmdldefs.tlc            Restart MATLAB.    Note: If you are using release R14sp2, R14sp3, R2006a, or R2006b, consider additionally downloading the fix for bug report 358425.

Summary: Spurious out of memory error when using complex scalars and empty matrices constructed from zeros or ones.
Description: Elementwise expressions involving scalar complex and matrix constructors (e.g., ones, zeros) give a spurious "out of memory" error if the requested size of the matrix is zero.For example,??? function foo??? x = 2i * zeros(0);
Workaround: Either check for zero inputs to zeros and ones or turn off the JIT with "feature accel off".

Summary: Embedded MATLAB may produce incorrect results for the inner product of two matrices
Description: Embedded MATLAB may produce incorrect simulation results and generate incorrect code for the following calculations:     Inner product of two matrices where the second matrix is the result of a matrix multiply     Inner product of two matrices where the second matrix is the result of a matrix concatenation  For example, the following code yields incorrect results because the second matrix is the result of a matrix multiply: trgSpec = [260.5704? 169.3282? 227.9564] ;invC = [0.0003?? -0.0002?? -0.0002 ; -0.0002??? 0.0017?? -0.0011;? -0.0002?? -0.0011??? 0.0011] ;trgFact2 = trgSpec * (invC * trgSpec') In this example, the following code yields incorrect results because the second matrix is the result of a matrix concatenation: a1 = [u(1); u(2)]; a2 = [u(3); u(4)]; y1 = a1' * a2; This bug may occur in:?     Embedded MATLAB Function blocks and Truth Table blocks in Simulink     Embedded MATLAB functions in Stateflow     Embedded MATLAB Coder     Embedded MATLAB MEX  ? ?
Workaround: Reshape the second matrix in the inner product calculation, as in this example:trgFact2 = trgSpec * reshape((invC * trgSpec'),3,1)

Summary: XCP component cannot provide communication with xPC Target
Description: Attempts to use the XCP compononent to communicate with xPC Target fail.
Workaround: To work around this issue:    Quit MATLAB.    Download the attached file, xpcgenlib.tlc.    Place the new file in the directory:    matlabroot\toolbox\rtw\targets\xpc\target\build\tlc        Restart MATLAB.    Rebuild your model.

Summary: Adding a block from the Simulink Library Browser automatically resized the model window; fixed in 2007b.
Description: The correct behavior is that when a block is added from the Library Browser to a model, the model window should come to the foreground and get keyboard focus, and the block should be centered in that window.? If the window was maximized, it should remain maximized; if it was minimized (iconified), it should be restored to its previous size and position (and come to the front, with focus).Reproduction steps for the bug that was fixed:1. Create a new model and maximize the window to cover the entire screen 2. In the Library Browser, right click on a block and choose "Add to Untitled" 3. The block window will revert to it's default size and location as the block shows up.

Summary: No error reported when parent model runs ERT S-function with?mismatched start time
Description: If an ERT S-function is simulated in a parent model using a start time different than the start time used to generate the ERT S-function code?from the original model, simulation results may be invalid but no error is reported.
Workaround: To work around this bug, set the start time of the parent model equal to the start time that was used to generate the ERT S-function code?from the original model.

Summary: Advantech PCL-711B A/D block dialog box specifies incorrect input ranges
Description: The Advantech PCL-711B A/D block dialog box lists +-10 , +-5 , and so forth as the available input ranges. However, the Advantech PCL-711B board supports only +-5 , +-2.5 , +-1.25 , +-0.625 , and +-0.3125  as the input ranges.
Workaround: As a workaround, add a Gain block at the output of the Advantech PCL-711B A/D block to multiply the output of the block by 0.5.The number that a block outputs is always twice the actual voltage that is at the input terminal of the board.

Summary: Using switch statements with complex (non-real) values causes assertion in Embedded MATLAB
Description: Using switch  statements with complex values causes assertions in Embedded MATLAB functions. For example, the following code causes an assertion because it uses a complex value as the case  expression: switch u  case {1+2i}    y = u;otherwise    y = 0;end

Summary: The bus selector dialog may fail to open in certain cases
Description: The bus selector dialog may fail to open if both of the following conditions exist:??? 1. The input bus signal is from a bus source that has a bus object specified.??? 2. The specified bus object?is not defined in the MATLAB base workspace.Simulink will issue an error similar to the following:??? "Error occurred when trying to create dialog ??? The error message says that the Bus Object 'MissingBusObj' is missing in the base workspace,?and as a result the bus selector dialog is unable to open.

Summary: Incorrect simulation result: C MEX S-function generated with Legacy Code Tool can produce incorrect simulation result when using Simulink.Bus objects
Description: If you use the Legacy Code Tool to integrate legacy C functions with structure arguments, using Simulink buses that define bus elements as 2D or ND arrays, the function can return incorrect simulation output.?For example, consider the following Legacy Code Tool function specification, where aStruct  is a struct  typedef  defined in a legacy code header file and implemented with a Simulink.Bus  object in the base workspace:def.OutputFcnSpec = 'void foo(aStruct u1[1], aStruct y1[1])'; e = Simulink.BusElemente.Name = 'field1';e.Dimensions = [5 10];aStruct = Simulink.Bus;aStruct.Elements = e; The generated C MEX S-function is configured?as though?the structure field? aStruct.field1  is a 2D array with 15 elements rather than 50.

Summary: Accelerator mode: incorrect results when?subsystem is enabled by signal with different sample time
Description: An enabled subsystem running in Accelerator mode may fail to become enabled and produce incorrect results under the following conditions:     All blocks of the subsystem run at one rate     The subsystem is enabled by another rate (that is, the subsystem's enable port has a different sample time than the subsystem)
Workaround: To work around this bug, insert a Rate Transition block at the enable port of the subsystem.

Summary: str2double does not accept nonnumeric input that is not a row vector
Description: Calling str2double  with an input string that contains any nonnumeric characters should return an answer of NaN. However, if you call str2double  with an input string of nonnumeric characters that is not a row vector, MATLAB throws an error.
Workaround: You can work around this problem using the command?? str2double(reshape(inputstring, 1, numel(inputstring)))

Description: If you resize a block by dragging one of its corners, then click the center of the block to initiate a move, the resize cursor continues to appear.

Summary: Referenced models in Accelerator mode may?lack protection against division by zero
Description: Code generated by Real-Time Workshop Embedded Coder?can optionally?include?protection against?against division by zero, depending on the setting of?Configuration Parameters > Optimization > Remove code that protects against division arithmetic exceptions .? This option appears only if an ERT-based target is selected on the Real-Time Workshop  pane.? When the option is enabled (the default) generated code does not protect against division by zero.? Such unprotected code may not give bit-true agreement with Simulink, but?is more efficient than protected code.Model execution within Simulink should always protect against division by zero, regardless of any parameter setting or other factor.? However, if?Remove code that protects against division arithmetic exceptions ?is enabled, code generated for a referenced model that executes in Accelerator mode will erroneously lack protection against division by zero.? This bug reflects the fact that Simulink, when generating code for an Accelerator mode?referenced model,?uses some of the same code generation tools as Real-Time Workshop.? The bug does not occur with other types of Simulink acceleration.
Workaround: If the Real-Time Workshop pane specifies a GRT-based target, Configuration Parameters > Optimization > Remove code that protects against division arithmetic exceptions does not appear and is internally disabled by default, so code generated for an Accelerator mode referenced model protects against zerodivide.? No special action is necessary in this case.If the Real-Time Workshop pane specifies an ERT-based target, Configuration Parameters > Optimization > Remove code that protects against division arithmetic exceptions?appears and is enabled by default.? To work around the bug, disable the option prior to simulation.? Code generated for an Accelerator mode referenced model then protects against zerodivide.
