Summary: iptaddcallback errors recursively when called on a figure with an active navigation tool (such as zoom or pan)
Description: When using iptaddcallback  to set an HG callback on a figure, and the figure has an active navigation tool (such as zoom or pan), iptaddcallback  will begin to error recursively until the MATLAB recursion limit is reached.

Summary: Generating code for tunable parameter expressions causes assertion
Description: Generating code for a model that has a tunable parameter expressions containing log() , log10() , or sqrt()  causes the following assertion: CGIR assertion in .\base\cg_const_function.cpp(271): 'parentScope != 0'
Workaround: Avoid the assertion by one of the following:    Make the parameter expression nontunable. For example, clear the Configuration Parameters > Optimization > Inline Parameters check box or make the parameters used in the expression nontunable.    Restructure the model to avoid the tunable parameter expression while retaining tunablity. For example, if the parameter expression of a Gain block is sqrt(P), replace the Gain block with a Product block, whose second input is fed by a constant block with value 'P' whose value is passed through a Math block configured to execute 'sqrt()' before entering the Product block.

Summary: Building a model that contains the National?Instruments?PCI-6528 Digital Output block?generates a link error
Description: Building a model that contains the National Instruments PCI-6528 Digital Output block generates a link error.
Workaround: As a workaround:    Quit MATLAB.    Download the attached file, donipci6528.c.    Place the new file in the directory:    matlabroot\toolbox\rtw\targets\xpc\target\build\xpcblocks    where matlabroot represents your MATLAB installation root, as returned by the MATLAB command matlabroot.    Restart MATLAB.    Rebuild your model.

Summary: Changing the PathDelays property of channel objects after construction can result in an erroneous output
Description: After a channel object has been created, changing the value of the PathDelays property?by reducing the number of paths?can result in an erroneous output.An example follows:fd = 1; ts = 0.1; Nsamples = 1e2; chan = rayleighchan(ts,fd); chan.PathDelays = 0.5; chan.AvgPathGaindB = 3; reset(chan,1); y = filter(chan,ones(1,Nsamples)); figure; semilogy(abs(y),'b'); hold on; chan.PathDelays = [0 0.25 0.5]; reset(chan,1); y2 = filter(chan,ones(1,Nsamples)); semilogy(abs(y2),'g'); chan.PathDelays = [0.5]; reset(chan,1); y3 = filter(chan,ones(1,Nsamples)); semilogy(abs(y3),'m'); % Output y3 should be the same as output y: it is not
Workaround: If the number of paths needs to be decreased, instead of changing the value of the PathDelays property, create a new channel object using rayleighchan or ricianchan.

Summary: SEGV when a non-trivial Configuration Parameter expression returns a Simulink object.
Description: If you enter a non-trivial expression for?a Configuration Parameter value, and this expression returns a Simulink object, then Simulink will SEGV.For example:??? Set StopTime = '[myParam ]' where myParam  is a Simulink.Parameter object in the base workspace.
Workaround: Refer to Simulink objects directly instead of referring to them as part of an expression.For example:??? Set StopTime = 'myParam'rather than:??? Set StopTime = '[myParam]'

Summary: Spectrum objects are not deployable via MATLAB Compiler
Description: If you include spectrum objects, such as spectrum.welch , spectrum.periodogram  etc. in an application deployed via MATLAB Compiler, a run-time error occurs.

Summary: If a cell array is serialialized and then deserialized the resulting cellArray cannot be altered.
Description: If a cell array is serialialized and then deserialized, the resulting cellArray contains the correct data. However, if an attempt is made to change the data contained using an indexer the application throws an exception, as in the following code. MWCellArray cell1 = new MWCellArray(new int[] { 1, 5 });? cell1[1, 2] = new MWNumericArray(1); ; cell1[1, 3] = new MWNumericArray(2);?  // Serialize cell1 Stream writeStreamCell = (Stream)File.Open("cell1.xml", FileMode.Create,?? FileAccess.Write); SoapFormatter soapFormatterCell = new SoapFormatter(); soapFormatterCell.Serialize(writeStreamCell, cell1); writeStreamCell.Close();? // Deserialize cell1 Stream readStreamCell = (Stream)File.Open("cell1.xml", FileMode.Open,?FileAccess.Read); MWCellArray newCell1 = (MWCellArray)soapFormatterCell.Deserialize?(readStreamCell); readStreamCell.Close();  Console.WriteLine(newCell1);  newCell1[1, 2] = new MWNumericArray(3); newCell1[1, 3] = (new MWNumericArray(4);  Console.WriteLine(newCell1); ?
Workaround: The temporary workaround for this bug is to clone the deserialized cell array and use the clone to modify the results as follows: MWCellArray newCell2 = (MWCellArray)newCell1.Clone();  Console.WriteLine(newCell2);  newCell2[1, 2] = new MWNumericArray(3); newCell2[1, 3] = (new MWNumericArray(4);  Console.WriteLine(newCell2);

Summary: Data Acquisition Toolbox Simulink blocks clip the data while trying to acquire from or output to a Data Acquisition device.
Description: During simulation, Data Acquisition Toolbox Simulink Analog Input and Analog Output blocks clip the data when a value other than the default is selected for 'InputRange'? or 'OutputRange' respectively. If the default range value is not changed, the data is not clipped and the block works correctly.

Summary: Initial value expressions for Stateflow data do not support? boolean type
Description: Specifying the initial value of Stateflow data as an expression that resolves to boolean (logical) type generates an error during model compilation. The error indicates that the expression does not evaluate to a numeric type.
Workaround: Cast the boolean expression to double() type ˇŞ for example, to double(true) or double(false).

Summary: Running the Database Toolbox update function returns an error when the condition of a WHERE clause is not met
Description: I am running the Database Toolbox update function to modify rows in a database table based on criteria specified in a WHERE clause. When no rows meet the WHERE clause criteria, the following error appears:>> ??? Error using ==> database.update Error:Commit/Rollback Problems Error in ==> database.update at 59 update(connect,tableName,fieldNames,data(i,:),whereClause{i})

Summary: Bus object consistency errors are not reported unless the last Bus Creator block checked?has an error
Description: Bus object consistency errors for Bus Creator blocks are reported for all or none of the Bus Creators in the Model. Simulink stores a list of Bus Creator blocks, and performs consistency checking for all blocks in the list.?If the last block in the list has a consistency error, Simulink reports all consistency errors. If the last block does not have any consistency error,?Simulink reports none of the errors.
Workaround: Do?the following to identify all bus object consistency errors in your model. Your model must be compiled successfully before running these steps:(1) Run the following command to create a bus object:????? e1 = Simulink.BusElement;????? e1.Dimensions = 3;????? e1.Name = 'a';????? e2 = Simulink.BusElement;????? e2.Dimensions = 3;????? e2.Name = 'b';????? errorBus = Simulink.Bus;????? errorBus.Elements = [e1 e2];(2) Open your model, and run the following command to copy a Bus Creator block with block name 'zz' to the topmost level in your model:???? model = bdroot;???? s = add_block('built-in/BusCreator',[model '/zz']);???? set_param(s,'Inputs','2');???? set_param(s,'UseBusObject','on');???? set_param(s,'BusObject','errorBus');(3) If there is any block with name 'zz' at the top level, add an additional 'z' to the Bus Creator name to make it unique. Run the following command and verify that the new Bus Creator block is the last block in the sorted list:??????h = find_system(model,'FollowLinks', 'on', 'BlockType','BusCreator'); sort(h)(4) Compile the model. You should get an error for the new Bus creator block. You should also get additional errors for any other Bus Creator block(s) in your model that fails the consistency checking. You should fix all errors before removing the Bus Creator block from your model.If you get only one error for the new bus creator block, the bus objects specified on the Bus Creator blocks are consistent with the bus signals. You should then delete the new Bus Creator block from your model.

Summary: The polybool function?fails due to numerical precision issues
Description: The polybool  function sometimes fails due to numerical precision issues that affect its?underlying sweepline algorithm.
Workaround: See Bug Report 523404 for pre-processing tips for challenging data sets.

Summary: The TLC function FixPt_DataTypeFxpIsSigned( block, dataTypeId ) does not correctly determine the signedness of the data type.
Description: For user-written, fixed-point sfunctions that support code generation, TLC utility functions are available to investigate the properties of a data type. The TLC function FixPt_DataTypeFxpIsSigned( block, dataTypeId ) determines whether a data type is signed or unsigned. This FixPt_DataTypeFxpIsSigned function had a bug which caused it to return the data-type word length instead of the signedness.

Summary: Map frame clipped after changing certain map axes properties
Description: When worldmap  or usamap  constructs a map axes, it calls tightmap('tight')  and set the Clipping  property of the map frame to 'off' . If you then change certain map axes properties, FEdgeColor  for example, the map frame is redrawn with Clipping  set to the default of 'on' , and as a result the frame can become partially obscured.
Workaround: If you download and install the new version of toolbox/map/mapdisp/tightmap.m provided by Bug Report 252025, that will resolve this problem as well.

Summary: Model simulation fails to start but no error dialog is shown for a model containing Embedded MATLAB-based blocks
Description: Models fail to simulate when they contain an Embedded MATLAB Function block or Truth Table block that sends a complex output to a Simulink block that requires a real input. When this type mismatch occurs, no error dialog box appears and the bug is reported incorrectly in the MATLAB command window, as follows: Operands to the || and && operators must be convertible to logical scalar values.
Workaround: Diagnose the error by placing a dummy block (a gain of 1) that accepts a complex signal on the outputs of any blocks you suspect may be the source of the problem.

Summary: SAD block might produce incorrect results
Description: The SAD block produces incorrect results when both of the following criteria are met:* The input image and the image template have the same number of rows or columns*You run the model in Accelerator mode or you generate code from the model
Workaround: You can correct this problem by replacing the svip2dsad.tlc in $matlabroot/toolbox/vipblks/vipmex/tlc_c directory with the contents of attachment_377096_20070621.zip file attached to this bug report.

Summary: Menubar missing from FFT Analysis Tool GUI
Description: The menubar containing several items such as File, Edit, View, etc, was disabled in FFT Analysis GUI in releases R2006b and R2007a.
Workaround: Copy the psbfftscope_R200XX.fig file relevant to the release (XX is either '6b' or '7a') to the folder <MATLABROOT>/toolbox/physmod/powersys/powersys/    Delete psbfftscope.fig in the source folder above    Rename psbfftscope_R200XX.fig as psbfftscope.fig?

Summary: Linux: Current Directory Browser?does not display multiple folders?that have?the same name, but use?different cases
Description: On the Linux platform, the Current Directory Browser displays only one folder if?multiple folders have the same name, but have?uppercase or lowercase letter differences between them.? For example, if one folder is named?folder1  and another is named Folder1, then the?Current Directory Browser displays folder1 , but not Folder1 .

Summary: Error estimating nonlinear grey-box model with one state from multiexperiment data
Description: Estimating the output of a nonlinear grey-box (idnlgrey) model produces an error when:1. The model has only one state, and2. You have multiexperiment data.
Workaround: There are two possible workarounds:1. Replace the file <matlabroot>\toolbox\ident\nlident\@idnlgrey\getSensitivityRefined.p with the attached file, after unzipping. You might need to restart MATLAB.2. If you cannot replace the file getSensitivityRefined.p, then do one of the following:(a) Set the models property Algorithm.GradientOptions to 'basic' and estimate the model.(b) Estimate the model using single-experiment data only.

Summary: Incorrect code generation: Generated code might not compile for embedded targets
Description: The generated code that initializes external inputs might not compile if the target is an embedded target that specifies a byte alignment, such as the TI C6000 target. An error similar to the following mightbe reported:"Model.c", line 58: error: argument of type "char" is incompatible with parameter of type "void *" The embedded target, ERT, is not affected, nor are nonembedded targets such as GRT or RSIM.
Workaround: In the Configuration Parameters > Optimization pane, select the Remove rool level I/O zero initialization check box.

Summary: anovan?produces indexing error
Description: For some non-hierarchical models, the anovan  function produces an error message.?A non-hierarchical model is one that includes a higher-order term, such as A*B,  but omits lower-order terms that are contained in it, such as A  or B .In most cases the message indicates an indexing error.
Workaround: Including the omitted lower-order terms avoids the problem.

Summary: Model build fails when Build action is set to Archive_library or Create_Processor_In_the_Loop_project and you select real-time execution profiling
Description: When you build a??model while the Profiling real-time execution ?checkbox is selected and?Build action  is set to Archive_library  or Create_Processor_In_the_Loop_project, you get an error in the MATLAB command window and the build process stops . The compiler generates following error message:"profile.c", line 16: fatal error: could not open source file "profile_vars.h"1 fatal error detected in the compilation of "profile.c" Real-time execution profiling requires? that you create an executable project. You cannot? perform real-time task execution profiling unless you set the build action to Create_project , Build , or Build_and_execute .?
Workaround: To avoid this compiler error, clear the?Profiling real-time execution?checkbox when you set Build action ?to Archive_library or Create_Processor_In_the_Loop_project.

Summary: In some cases LINPROG using Simplex algorithm terminate with exitflag=1 but without satisfying the linear equality constraints.
Description: When in the equality constrain matrix, Aeq, there are columns with a single non-zero entry (column singletons), LINPROG simplex algorithm may return an incorrect solution vector, x.
Workaround: Eliminate column singletons from the problem formulation before calling LINPROG.

Summary: Compiler options specified to "Make command" not honored by right-click S-function builds
Description: Compiler options specified to the Make command  parameter on the Real-Time Workshop  pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog are not honored for right-click S-function builds. For example, if you specify the value make_rtw OPT_OPTS=-g  for Make command , requesting that debug information be generated, then right-click a subsystem and select Real-Time Workshop > Generate S-Function , the build fails to generate debug information.
Workaround: To work around this bug, generate S-function code for the subsystem, and then go to the code generation directory subsystem_sfcn_rtw, where subsystem is the name of the subsystem source block, and edit the generated makefile to provide the desired compiler options.

Summary: Fixed-point and integer signals greater than 32 bits are silently truncated on the boundary with Embedded MATLAB Function blocks.
Description: A fixed-point or integer signal entering an Embedded MATLAB Function block or Truth Table block will be silently truncated to 32 bits if it has more than 32 bits and inherits its type from Simulink.? The results of this truncation are unpredictable and depend on the endianness of the host, among other factors.If you specify the type of signals that are wider than 32 bits ˇŞ rather than inherit the type from Simulink ˇŞ Embedded MATLAB Function blocks and Truth Table blocks throw an error and do not truncate the signal.
Workaround: Insert a data type conversion block to reduce input signals to a supported number of bits (1-32).

Summary: Code generation might fail when a heterogeneous signal is an input to a reusable subsystem
Description: Real-Time Workshop might fail to generate code or MATLAB might crash for a model with a heterogeneous signal input to a reusable subsystem, under all of the following conditions:     There is a subsystem in the model configured to generate reusable code.     One of the subsystem input signals is a heterogenous signal, a Muxed signal with sources of different data types.     The heterogenous signal is not used directly.? Instead, it is Demuxed and the separated elements are used in the subsystem.
Workaround: Insert a Signal Conversion block immediately before the input to the reusable subsystem.

Summary: Model reference ignores the value of?TLC options
Description: Code generation for a referenced model ignores configuration parameter values specified in the?TLC options  field?of the Configuration Parameters > Real-Time Workshop  page.? This failure can cause a configuration mismatch error even though TLC options  specifies values that should create a consistent configuration.
Workaround: Set parameters by using the Configuration Parameters dialog box?field specific to each parameter,?rather than by specifying the parameter values in TLC options.??For example, to?enable inline parameters, go to Configuration Parameters > Optimization and select ?Inline parameters.??Do not enter -aInlineParameters=1 in the TLC options field.

Summary: Fixed-Point Toolbox divide function generates uncompilable code in Embedded MATLAB
Description: The divide  function in Fixed-Point Toolbox generates uncompilable code for Embedded MATLAB functions used in Simulink and Stateflow, and compiled with emlc . This bug occurs under the following conditions: The exponent of the numerator minus the exponent of the denominator is less than the exponent of the output. The rounding mode is ceiling .  When this bug occurs, you get a compiler error that references an undefined symbol 'untyped'.
Workaround: Try a different rounding mode.

Summary: Editing?a figure saved with visible layout grid causes errors when using plottting tools
Description: Using plotting tools to edit a figure with a visible layout grid that was saved?in MATLAB 7.0 (R14) or later?generates the following error:? No method 'classhandle' with matching signature found for class 'scribe.scribeaxes'. Details: When calling addLsnrs ToFigure, a MATLAB error occured
Workaround: To work around this issue, turn off the layout grid prior to saving a figure.

Summary: Using 'install -t' to install only?the licence manager on UNIX systems fails to configure scripts in $MATLAB/etc
Description: When using the text based installer (install -t )?to install only the license manager on UNIX systems, sometimes the scripts in $MATLAB/etc  do not get configured.?
Workaround: To work around this issue, you can reinstall using the GUI installer, or you can perform the following steps in a?UNIX command shell:    cd to the installation directory    Run the following command:    ?    install_matlab 4

Summary: MATLAB crashes when I use Excel Link with the MATLAB debugger.
Description: I set a break point in a MATLAB-file which I use with Excel Link. The debugger reaches the break point correctly. When I press continue  MATLAB crashes and disappears. This occurs with any MATLAB-file and is reproducible.

Summary: pearsrnd enters infinite loop
Description: The pearsrnd  function?enters an infinite loop, and fails to return control to the calling workspace, for some combinations of mean, standard deviation, skewness, and kurtosis inputs. These combinations correspond to certain cases of the Pearson Type IV distribution.

Summary: fileparts doesn't handle Windows volume-relative pathnames correctly
Description: On Windows, the fileparts  function incorrectly inserts a backslash after the drive-letter and colon when given arguments of the form X:  or X:Aname  (but not of the form X:Aname\Anothername ). In both cases, the result is X:\  when it should be X:.   Returning X:\  is incorrect because the inputs to fileparts  represent volume-relative pathnames, while X:\  is an absolute pathname that represents the top-level directory of the X drive.

Summary: cd command clears current working directory information for all but destination drive
Description: On Windows, if the cd  command is used to change the current directory to one on a different drive, the current working directory information for all drives except the destination drive is reset to the root directory. This interferes with the ability to use volume-relative pathnames, such as X:Afile . For example,cd X:\Adirectorycd Y:\BdirectoryF = fopen('X:Afile', 'r'); should open the file X:\Adirectory\Afile , but because of the second line, instead will open X:\Afile  (if it exists).

Summary: "Signed integer division rounds to" model configuration parameter can be mistakenly hidden and disabled when "Emulation hardware" is enabled and "Device type" is set to "MATLAB Host Comptuer"
Description: If you enable the Emulation hardware  parameter on the Hardware Implementation pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog, and specify MATLAB Host Computer  for Device type , Simulink?     Hides the Signed integer division rounds to  parameter     Tries to determine the rounding mode used by the current host machine for signed integer division     If not able to determine the rounding mode, disables the parameter by setting it to Undefined   Note that?, if Device Type  in the Embedded hardware  section is set to a value other than Unspecified, and  you use the Model Advisor and select the Real-Time Workshop Embedded Coder check Check the hardware implementation , a failure with an advisory severity?might occur with the following notification: It is recommended to specify Signed integer division rounding of the hardware. Specify the Signed integer division rounding to ensure correct code generation for the hardware targeted by this model. However, due to the condition noted above, the Signed integer division rounds to  parameter is hidden and disabled.

Summary: Why does the GetWorkspaceData function fail to correctly return a logical array variable?
Description: The GetWorkSpaceData  function fails to correctly retrieve an array of logical values. For example,h = actxserver('matlab.application'); h.Execute('x = logical([1:10])');x1 = h.GetWorkspaceData('x','base')

Summary: dir('X:') does not display contents of X drive's current working directory
Description: On Windows platforms, a pathname of the form X:  (as opposed to X:\ ) specifies the current working directory of the X drive (where X can be any drive specifier from A to Z). The MATLAB function dir, when passed an argument of the form X: , incorrectly displays or returns the contents of the top-level directory X:\ . It should display or return the contents of the current working directory of the X drive.

Summary: Segmentation violation occurs in a model with a Demux or Bus Selector block
Description: Real-Time Workshop might not generate code for a model and a segmentation violation might occur when a model with a Demux block or a Bus Selector block satisfies all of the following conditions:     The output signal is a matrix signal.     The block is connected to a Multi-Port Switch block or a Math Function block
Workaround: Insert a Signal Conversion block between the two blocks and make sure that the option "Override optimizations and always copy signal" is checked.

Summary: Embedded MATLAB M-Lint messages shown in the documentation cannot be found.
Description: Embedded MATLAB-specific M-Lint messages are not yet available in the MATLAB Editor.? The documentation is premature.
Workaround: Use the Embedded MATLAB Editor in Simulink to get Embedded MATLAB-specific M-Lint messages.

Summary: Function-Call Subsystem block that receives a bus signal might cause "Data dependency violation" error
Description: Suppose your model contains two function-call subsystems that execute with different sample rates, where one subsystem outputs a bus signal that the other subsystem receives. If the subsystem that receives the bus signal contains a Bus Selector block that does not select every signal from the incoming bus, Simulink might incorrectly display the following error message: Data dependency violation due to use of function-call or action subsystem. The loop can be traversed in the forward direction by following the red lines and in the backward direction by following the blue lines. Green lines indicate overlapping forward and backward paths in the loop. See Subsystem Examples in the Simulink library for valid and invalid examples of function-call and action subsystems.
Workaround: To work around this issue, remove from the bus any signals that the Bus Selector block does not select.

Summary: hdfread fails to read datasets with slashes in their names.
Description: hdfread  cannot access HDF datasets with slashes in the dataset name. This results in the empty set being returned and the following warning Warning: No data set found. Make sure the filename and data set name are correct.
Workaround: Installation instructions?????? Log in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.?????? Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps.?????? Exit MATLAB.?????? Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB> folder.?????? Make a backup copy of each of the files listed in the .zip file. To see the contents of the .zip file: ????????????? ???????????????????? Windows Systems: Open the .zip file and look at the contents.???????????????????? Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh):unzip -l <MATLAB>/<.zip file>????????????? ?????? ?????? Extract the files from the .zip file. ????????????? ???????????????????? Windows Systems:?????????????????????????? ????????????????????????????????? Open the .zip file.????????????????????????????????? Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.?????????????????????????? ???????????????????? ???????????????????? Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh): ?????????????????????????? cd <MATLAB>unzip <.zip file>???????????????????? ????????????? ?????? ?????? Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.

Summary: Function demdataui fails to import SATBATH data after importing ETOPO5 data
Description: If you use demdataui  to import ETOPO5 data and then attempt to use it to import SATBATH data in the same MATLAB session, no data is imported. An error dialog is issued with the message "No external DEM data found.".
Workaround: Download the new version of demdataui available via Bug Report 364691.

Summary: Unhelpful build error for model reference hierarchy with mismatched system target files
Description: Within a model reference hierarchy, the same system target file must be selected for each model. If you attempt to build a model with incompatible system target file selections, the build process should issue an informative error message. However, if the child models are not open when you build, the build process instead issues a message similar to the folllowing: Could not find information file: /builddir /slprj/ert/sldemo_mdlref_counter/tmwinternal/binfo_mdlref.mat where builddir   is the folder from which you initiated the build.
Workaround: To work around this bug, open all models in the model reference hierarchy before building. If the build detects incompatible system target files, the error message informs you that the system target file must be the same for all models in the model reference hierarchy.

Summary: On PowerPC Macs, isvector does not always return correct answer
Description: On a PowerPC Mac, the isvector  builtin does not always return the correct answer when the input is a matrix with more than two dimensions. For example,a = ones(2,1,3); isvector(a)
Workaround: A simple workaround is to perform a dummy operation on the input to isvector. For example, either of these two operations return the correct answer:isvector(double(a))isvector(full(a))

Summary: DSP/BIOS profiler is broken due to a missing variable
Description: CCS_Obj.profile('report')  matlab command, where CCS_Obj is a handle to the CCS, fails with the following error message: ??? Reference to non-existent field 'avgTime'. Error in ==> generateHTML_TItarget>showSubsystemStatistics at 181??????????? TXT{end+1} = ['<td>' ... Error in ==> generateHTML_TItarget at 38??? TXT = showSubsystemStatistics (TXT, S, P); Error in ==> ti_profreport at 85fileName = generateHTML_TItarget(S,P,profileTime); Error in ==> ticcs.ide_profileReport at 10??? ps = ti_profreport('report',h); Error in ==> baselink.profile at 85??????? ps = ide_profileReport(h);
Workaround: Download the attached patch installer patch_installer.m and the ZIP archive patch_374586.zip. To install the patch, follow these steps:1. Start MATLAB2. Enter the following command at the prompt to install the patch:?patch_installer('patch_374586.zip', 'install');3. Follow the instructions on the MATLAB command line to complete the patch installation

Summary: LSQNONLIN and LSQCURVEFIT medium-scale algorithms might error when encountering a sparse Jacobian
Description: The objective function can return a sparse Jacobian, and in some cases during the course of the optimization, the Jacobian might become ill-conditioned. In that case, an error will occur inside the medium-scale LSQNONLIN and LSQCURVEFIT. These conditions produce the following error message:??? Error using ==> norm Sparse norm(S,2) is not available.

Summary: Corrupt data output or "??? MCC: Invalid D/A value" error when using the Measurement Computing?PCI DAS6040
Description: When I run the following code:ao = analogoutput('mcc', '0'); addchannel(ao, 0); putsample(ao,0)  I encounter the following error:??? MCC: Invalid D/A value
Workaround: This?has been confirmed as a bug in Data Acquisition Toolbox using the MCC PCI DAS6040. To work around this issue, you need to modify the mwmcc.ini file. To Modify the mwmcc.ini, do this:    At the MATLAB command prompt type    matlabroot     MATLAB returns the root installation directory.    Quit MATLAB.    Find the file    $MATLABROOT\toolbox\daq\daq\private\mwmcc.ini, where $MATLABROOT is the MATLAB root installation directory.    Make a copy of this file and rename it to mwmcc.ini.old.    Open the file mwnidaqmx.ini in a text editor, search for the line containing the text:    [PCI-DAS6040]    Below this line, find the first occurrence of the text    AOResolution = 16    Change the '16' to '12' so this line now reads    AOResolution = 12    Save the file mwmcc.ini and restart MATLAB.

Summary: Certain webcams can cause?Video and Image Processing Blockset to produce a?segmentation violation
Description: Certain video cameras, such as the Creative Webcam Live! Motion , can cause Video and Image Processing Blockset to produce a segmentation violation.The segmentation violation?occurs when you open the Video and Image Processing Blockset? Sinks?library or when you run a Video and Image Processing Blockset?demo.This problem affects your computer if Video and Image Processing Blockset produces a segmentation violation when you type the following command at the MATLAB command prompt:>> videodevinfo
Workaround: You can correct this problem by replacing the?VideoDeviceChooser.dll in $matlabroot/bin/win32 directory with the?file attached to this bug report.

Summary: Under the openGL renderer, uipanel children remain visible when the panel is set to be invisible.
Description: When?openGL is used in rendering a figure and the Visible  property of a uipanel is set to false , the children within the uipanel are still visible.
Workaround: Do not use the openGL renderer when uipanels are used if you want to hide them. Useset(figure_handle,'Renderer', 'zbuffer')orset(figure_handle,'Renderer','painters')

Summary: Property Editor scrollbars on the MAC can take up all the available space for axis labels
Description: The x ,y ,?and z  label text edit boxes in the Property Editor for Axes always display horizontal scroll bars on the MAC .?This causes the axis label text?in the boxes to be obscured even?for short labels when the vertical space available for the Property Editor is too small.?

Summary: Cannot?use a MATLAB structure to specify an index in Selector or Assignment block dialog
Description: In the Selector and Assignment block dialog, you cannot?specify a member data of a MATLAB structure, such as a.x. , as the index. Doing so?generates the following error message: "Index" for dimension 1 Error using ==> evalUndefined variable "a" or class "a.x".
Workaround: As a?workaround, do one of the following:    Use a MATLAB variable?to take the value of the MATLAB structure member data (for example, b = a.x). Then use the new variable, b in this example, to specify the index in the block dialog.    Use the set_param command to set the the index. For example:    set_param('model/Selector', 'Indices', '[1 a.x]')

Summary: Change in NCBI RID?format causes the getblast function to fail to recognize the?RID
Description: An error occurs when retrieving NCBI BLAST reports using the function getblast , as a consequence of?change made?to the Request ID (RID) format. The new format was introduced by NCBI on April 16, 2007.
Workaround: Quit MATLAB.Rename the file $MATLAB$\toolbox\bioinfo\bioinfo\blastncbi.m to $MATLAB$\toolbox\bioinfo\bioinfo\blastncbi.m.oldRename the file $MATLAB$\toolbox\bioinfo\bioinfo\getblast.m to $MATLAB$\toolbox\bioinfo\bioinfo\getblast.m.old? Rename the file $MATLAB$\toolbox\bioinfo\bioinfo\blastread.m to $MATLAB$\toolbox\bioinfo\bioinfo\blastread.m.old? where $MATLAB$ is the directory where MATLAB is installed on your machine.Download the attached MATLAB files to the directory $MATLAB$\toolbox\bioinfo\bioinfo\Restart MATLAB.Run the commandrehash toolboxcacheat the MATLAB prompt.?

Summary: uigetfile?or uiputfile may throw a warning when the file dialog is dismissed.
Description: The uigetfile ?or uiputfile  function may throw the following warning when its file dialog box is dismissed:Warning: Objects of UiFileOpenDialog class exist - not clearing this class
Workaround: Ignore the?warning.?It does not affect the functionality of uigetfile?or uiputfile.

Summary: LCC-generated C code crashes for functions with large inputs
Description: LCC does not correctly compile functions with very large inputs passed on the stack, causing the generated code to crash. For example, you can trigger this bug by passing a structure larger than 4KB by value.? This bug does not occur when you pass inputs by reference.Because LCC is the default compiler on Windows, this bug can affect any product or feature using code generation and structures, such as Real-Time Workshop, Stateflow, Embedded MATLAB Function Blocks, and Truth Table blocks.

Summary: FDATool and FVTool error when displaying hexadecimal values for fixed-point SOS filters
Description: If you select Hexadecimal  from the Analysis  >  Analysis  Parameters  menu to view filter coefficients of a fixed-point SOS filter in hexadecimal format, an error occurs.

Summary: Right-mouse clicking on the legend of a Signal Viewer results in errors
Description: You will see the following?MATLAB error if you?perform a simulation?of?a model containing a signal viewer, and then turn on and right-click the viewers' legend:?  ??? Reference to non-existent field 'origLegBD'.  Error in ==> simscopesv>i_LegendBDFcn at 1011 ?? origLegBD????? = scopeUserData.origLegBD;  ??? Error while evaluating axes ButtonDownFcn  ??? Reference to non-existent field 'origLegCM'.  Error in ==> simscopesv>i_LegendContextFcn at 1079 ?? origLegCM????? = scopeUserData.origLegCM;

Summary: The Data Object Wizard should not require a Real-Time Workshop Embedded Coder license
Description: If you run the Data Object Wizard, without Real-Time Workshop Embedded Coder license,you get an error. The Data Object Wizard is a Simulink feature and should not require aReal-Time Workshop Embedded Coder license.

Summary: Why do I receive a warning message when setting the FrameRate property of my DCAM device?
Description: When I attempt to set the FrameRate  of my DCAM device, I receive a warning message. For example:vid = videoinput('dcam', 1, 'Y8_640x480');src = getselectedsource(vid);set(vid, 'FrameRate', '15');Warning: An error occurred after the property value was updated. SET could not be undone.The DCAM device has failed to initialize. However, if I start  or preview  the device, it seems that the frame rate was actually set on the device.
Workaround: In this case the warning message is cosmetic and does not affect the configuration of the device. You can either ignore the warning, or disable it:warning('off', 'imaq:dcam:setvideoframerate');

Summary: Priority Queue block incorrectly sorts simultaneous arrivals when capacity exceeds 100
Description: The Priority Queue block in the Queues library fails to sort entities correctly when all of these conditions are met:     The capacity of the queue is greater than 100.     Multiple entities with the same value of the sorting priority attribute arrive at the Priority Queue block simultaneously.
Workaround: Change other blocks in the model to avoid simultaneous entity arrivals at a Priority Queue block.The attached model contains an example in which the Period parameter in the Time-Based Entity Generator blocks are slightly different, to avoid simultaneous entity arrivals with the same sorting priority value.

Summary: How can I pass?a SAFEARRAY of booleans from MATLAB to a COM object?
Description: I have a COM object that has a method that accepts an array of boolean values. When I call this method from MATLAB, only the first element of the boolean array is received by the COM object. I have set the COM_SafeArraySingleDim flag.
Workaround: No workaround avaialable. This issue is fixed in MATLAB 7.5.

Summary: Spectral analysis functions producing graphical outputs are not compiled properly by the MATLAB Compiler
Description: If you use spectral analysis functions such as mscohere , pwelch , etc. without any output arguments, a plot is produced. The MATLAB compiler does not support this particular syntax.
Workaround: Call the spectral analysis functions with one or more output arguments and then use the plot command to produce the graphical output.

Summary: Calling FCLOSE on a VISA object sometimes causes a java "NullPointerException"
Description: When this FCLOSE  command executes,g = visa('tek','GPIB8::1::INSTR') fopen(g)fclose(g) I receive this following error: ?  ??? Error using ==> icinterface.fclose at 53Java exception occurred:java.lang.NullPointerException??at com.mathworks.toolbox.instrument.Poller.getDelay(Poller.java:83)??at com.mathworks.toolbox.instrument.Instrument.fclose(Instrument.java:1691)
Workaround: This is a benign error.? As it is occuring at a stage where all the resources have been freed from this object, it should not cause any problems.? To work around this issue, enclose? the FCLOSE statement in a try block which has no catch.g = visa('tek','GPIB8::1::INSTR')fopen(g)try? fclose(g)end

Summary: The Discrete-Time Scatter Plot Scope block does not skip the specified number of samples before plotting
Description: The offset parameter of the Discrete-Time Scatter Plot Scope block was implemented incorrectly.

Summary: Writing GIF files with imwrite leaks a small amount of memory for each file.
Description: Writing GIF files with imwrite  leaks a small amount of memory for each file.? This memory is not reclaimed until MATLAB exits.

Summary: Control-C during execution of a MEX-function can cause MATLAB to crash
Description: Under rare circumstances pressing Control-C during the execution of a MEX-function may cause unstable MATLAB behavior, manifesting as an assertion or crash. One known example where this may occur is if the MEX-function is called from within a doubly-nested for loop .

Summary: Real-Time Windows Target kernel cannot be installed, reporting that Pentium or better processor is required.
Description: The tests for supported CPU features are sometimes too strict. They report missing CPU features that are actually not missing but just disabled in hardware. This causes the Real-Time Windows Target kernel installer to fail, reporting that a Pentium or better processor is required. The problem appears most often with mobile CPUs.
Workaround: The problem can be worked around by installing Real-Time Windows Target kernel using this sequence:olddir = cd;    cd(fullfile(toolboxdir('rtw'),'targets','rtwin','rtwin','private'));    rtwtinst('install', fullfile('..','..','kernel'));    cd(olddir);    The kernel installation can then be verified by issuing rtwho.

Summary: Incorrect code generation: Embedded MATLAB functions and Stateflow generate incorrect code when function inputs are reused
Description: Embedded MATLAB Function blocks, Stateflow charts, and Truth Table blocks may generate incorrect code if you write to function inputs in the source code.? The bug occurs when an optimization designed to convert doubles into integers applies to variables interacting with the offending input variable.The following conditions trigger this bug:     Inlining is disabled or does not occur.     Your code overwrites an input variable with a value computed in a loop.     The input variable being written to is also used in a control-flow expression.  Some functions that satisfy all of these conditions will not trigger the bug. For example, the following function meets these conditions and generates incorrect code when inlining is disabled: function M = fcn(N)M = 0;z = 0;for i =1:3    z = z+1;endN = z; % Overwrites input N with a value computed in a for loop .for j = 1:3    i = 2;    while i < N-1 % Uses input N is used in a control-flow expression .        if i > 2            i = i + 1;  % Generates incorrect code because i is compared to input N.         else            M=1;            i = i+3;        end    end    w = 0;    for i = 1:2       w = w+1;    end    N = w; % Overwrites input N with a value computed in a for loop .end This bug affects code generation via Real-Time Workshop and simulation when debugging is turned off. One symptom of this bug is that your model behaves differently with debugging on and off.
Workaround: There are two workarounds:    Do not reuse function inputs for computed values.    Disable the optimization that causes this bug.To disable the optimization, type the following MATLAB commands: >> sf('Feature','Shrink Types','off')>> sf('Feature','Shrink Types Late','off') % Only needed in R2006b and later.  You must run these commands in every session or include them in your startup.m file.

Summary: DEPLOYTOOL takes a long time to open when creating a project in certain directories.
Description: DEPLOYTOOL takes a long time to create a new project or open an existing project when at least one of the directories leading to the project directory (including the project directory) contains a large number of files and sub-directories.
Workaround: Limit the number of files and subdirectories in each of the subdirectories leading to the project directory.

Summary: The installed version of the FLEXnet Licensing End User Guide is corrupted
Description: The?PDF file enduser.pdf , installed in the matlabroot \flexlm  folder on?Windows systems and the matlabroot /etc  directory on UNIX systems,  is corrupted and cannot be read.? matlabroot   represents the name of your MATLAB installation directory, as returned by the matlabroot  function.
Workaround: Replace the corrupted version of the enduser.pdf file in your MATLAB installation with the version attached. Download the file and copy it over the existing version in the matlabroot\flexlm folder on?Windows systems and the matlabroot/etc directory on UNIX systems,?where? matlabroot represents the name of your MATLAB installation directory, as returned by the matlabroot function.

Summary: Simulink Debugger hangs when?a breakpoint is set in a referenced model
Description: Clicking either the Play/Continue? or??Stop  button causes the model to hang and the following error messages to be displayed when a breakpoint is set in the referenced model and the debugger was started from the root model:??? Undefined function or variable 'cont' ??? Undefined function or variable 'stop'

Summary: Code generation might fail for a Model block that contains a signal with a global or custom storage class
Description: Real-Time Workshop might generate an unresolved symbol error when compiling generated code if all of the following conditions are met:    The model contains a Model block.     The Model block contains a signal with a global or custom storage class.     The source of the signal connects to a root outport in the Model block.     Block reduction optimization is enabled, affecting blocks connected between the signal and its source.
Workaround: You may negate this issue by:    Insert a Signal Conversion block after the branch point of the signal source that feeds the root outport.    Select the "Override optimizations and always copy signal" option in the Signal Conversion block.

Summary: Code generation for ERT-based model using ASAP2 interface fails with "Indices may not be negative" error
Description: The build process can fail with the error "Indices may not be negative " when generating code for an ERT-based Simulink model for which all of the following conditions are true: ASAP2  is selected for the Interface  option on the Interface  pane of the Configuration Parameters dislog box. The model includes a masked subsystem that contains a Lookup Table block, and the Lookup Table parameters have been set using the Subsystem block Mask Parameters . Reusable function  is selected for the Real-Time Workshop system code  option in the Subsystem Parameters dialog box.
Workaround: To work around this bug, perform the following steps:Note: In the following instructions, matlabroot represents your MATLAB installation root, as returned by the MATLAB command matlabroot.In your MATLAB installation tree, rename the following file, for example, by adding .old to the end of the file name.matlabroot/rtw/c/tlc/mw/asamlib.tlcDownload the attached file asamlib.tlc and place it in the same directory as the file you just renamed.Restart MATLAB.

Summary: Errors might occur when specifying $M for the Local temporary variables parameter
Description: Specifying the $M ? token for the Local temporary variables  parameter in the Configuration Parameters  > Real-Time Workshop  > Symbols  pane creates a name mangling string for local temporary variables. The Real-Time Workshop build process generates an error when all name mangling strings are exhausted. The error is an assertion similar to the following:  Stateflow parsing for model "sawTooth"...Done Stateflow code generation for model "sawTooth"....failed assertion in .\rtwcg_utils\rtwcg_env.cpp at line 124: utStrlen(cg_string_toascii(newkey)) > 0 Stack Trace: [0] sf.mexw32:0x11e9b3e9(0x0fd9e78c "utStrlen(cg_string_toascii(newke..", 0x0fd9e758 ".\rtwcg_utils\rtwcg_env.cpp", 124, 0) [1] sf.mexw32:0x11db2b76(0, 0x0fd9e758 ".\rtwcg_utils\rtwcg_env.cpp", 124, 0x0fd9e78c "utStrlen(cg_string_toascii(newke..") [2] cg_ir.dll:void __cdecl client_assertion_failed(char const *,int,char const *)(0x0fd9e758 ".\rtwcg_utils\rtwcg_env.cpp", 124, 0x0fd9e78c "utStrlen(cg_string_toascii(newke..", 0x311592ef) + 27 bytes [3] rtwcg.dll:public: void __thiscall RTWCG::RTWCGEnv::put(char *,void *)(0x164e3dec "is_active_c1_sawTooth", 0x164e9968, 0x311592db, 0x164e9320) + 106 bytes [4] rtwcg.dll:public: void __thiscall RTWCG::RTWCGNameSpaceC::consistentRename(enum E_RTWCG_ENV,class CG_Scope *)(0, 0x164e9320, 1, 0x163cd4c0) + 234 bytes [5] rtwcg.dll:public: void __thiscall RTWCG::RTWCGNameSpaceC::rename(class CG_Scope *)(0x164e9320, 0x3115929f, 0x164f1a28, 0x0f9b4610) + 40 bytes
Workaround: Specify a macro string other than the $M token for the Local temporary variables parameter. The string can include the $M token with another token such as $N$M.

Summary: Simulink may fail to simulate if a bus signal is connected to an S-Function, Stateflow or Model Reference block
Description: The following conditions need to be true: The model contains a user-written S-Function, Model Reference, or Stateflow block that is configured to accept a bus A bus signal is connected to this block The 'Bus signal treated as vector' diagnostic in the Configuration Parameters > Diagnostics > Connectivity panel is set to 'error'  Simulink will produce an error message similar to the following: Stateflow Interface Error: Cannot propagate bus signal to input?SFunction (#) of? Chart (#42) because this input port expects a non-bus signal. The bus signal originates from 'Model/Chart/ SFunction'. If the input bus signal consists of only scalar, 1-D, or either row or column vectors and have the same data type, signal type, and sampling mode, consider inserting a Bus to Vector conversion block in the signal path. Otherwise, consider using a Bus Selector in the signal path. If the 'Bus signal treated as vector' diagnostic is set to 'warning', a spurious?warning message similar to the error message above will be issued. The warning is harmless and can safely be ignored.  In addition, if the 'Bus signal treated as vector' diagnostic is set to "warning" or "none", bus signals emerging from the block may not be drawn correctly. Specifically, the bus signal may be drawn as a scalar signal.
Workaround: To workaround the simulation error, change the 'Bus signal treated as vector' diagnostic setting to 'none'.To correct the line drawing style, follow these steps: Download the attached file, attachment_372429_20070508.zip, to a temp dir and unzip it.Copy FixBusLineStylesFor7a.p to a location on the MATLAB path.Start MATLAB and run the following command: FixBusLineStylesFor7a.Simulink should now draw bus signals correctly after model compilation.Please note that FixBusLineStylesFor7a is only valid for use in R2007a.

Summary: Constant block value might become non-tunable
Description: The value of a Constant block might become non-tunable if it is connected to an S-Function block, a Model Reference block, or the IC-port of an Integrator block, and any of the following is true:    The Constant block is not connected to any other blocks.     The S-Function block is not inlined     The signal is a global signal.
Workaround: Insert a Gain block between the Constant block and the subsequent block.

Summary: DEPLOYTOOL does not compile when a directory on path contains Unicode characters
Description: If any of the project directories (project directory, directory where MATLAB file resides, output directory, or MATLAB install directory) contain Unicode characters, the project does not compile.??

Summary: In Embedded MATLAB, persistent structure variables defined by extension are not persistent
Description: When you declare a variable to be persistent and then define it as a structure by extension ˇŞ that is, by initializing individual fields in the structure ˇŞ Embedded MATLAB treats the variable as temporary, not persistent.?? For example in the following code, the value of p  does not persist, causing isempty  to always return true  and the else  clause to never execute.persistent p; if isempty(p) ? p.a = 1; else ?p.a = p.a + 1; % This line is never executed. end This bug affects Embedded MATLAB Function blocks, Embedded MATLAB functions in Stateflow, Truth Table blocks, and Embedded MATLAB MEX (emlmex).?
Workaround: Use a subfunction or the function struct to initialize persistent structure variables. The following code uses a subfunction init_p to initialize a persistent structure p?:persistent p; if isempty(p) ? p = init_p; else ? p.a = p.a + 1; end function p = init_p p.a? = 1;This example uses the? struct function to initialize a persistent structure p:persistent p;if isempty(p)? p = struct("a",1);else?p.a = p.a + 1;end

Summary: The?angle function returns a complex result in Embedded MATLAB even though the imaginary part is always zero.
Description: In an Embedded MATLAB Function block, the angle  function returns a complex result with a zero imaginary part even though it should return a real result.
Workaround: Cast the return value of angle to a real value, as in this example:y = real(angle(z))

Summary: The complex function gives incorrect answer in Embedded MATLAB for fixed-point inputs
Description: If you pass fixed-point values with different numeric types to the complex  function, Embedded MATLAB incorrectly converts the imaginary part.
Workaround: Cast both complex function inputs to the same fixed-point numerictype.For example, change:y = complex(x1,x2)to:y = complex(x1,fi(x2,numerictype(x1))?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Code generation may not match simulation when model contains conditionally executed subsystems with specified Outport initialization value
Description: The simulation output signal value for a conditionally executed subsystem might be different than the generated code output signal value when all of the following conditions are met: The conditionally executed subsystem contains a Constant block connected to an Outport block with a specified Initial output  value. The outport of the conditionally executed Subsystem block is connected to an S-Function block, a Model Reference block, or the IC-port of an Integrator block.
Workaround: Insert a Gain block between the Subsystem and subsequent blocks.

Summary: Multiple models using a common library model with masked parameters and Embedded MATLAB Function blocks generate errors
Description: You may get build errors when working with multiple models that share a common library model containing multiple masked Embedded MATLAB Function blocks. The errors occur under the following conditions: You update multiple models that you have open at the same time. The common library model contains multiple masked Embedded MATLAB Function blocks that take different sets of parameters.  When this bug occurs, update diagram produces error messages about parameters that may not be used by the model. Here is an example scenario: You have a library model L  containing two Embedded MATLAB Function blocks A  and B . Block A  takes parameter p . Block B  takes parameter q . You have two models, M1  and M2 , open. Both of these models use library L  but model M1  only uses block A from L and model M2  only uses block B  from L . If you update diagram in M1 , and then (without closing M1 ) update diagram in M2 , you will see errors about missing parameter p . Note, that M2  is not using p .
Workaround: Work on one model at a time.

Summary: Block parameters dialog flickers when opening
Description: The mask?parameters?of the following blocks?flicker multiple times when you open the block dialog box:?Linear Transformer Saturable Transformer Series RLC Branch Parallel RLC Branch Three-Phase Transformer (Two Windings) Three-Phase Transformer (Three Windings) Zigzag Phase-Shifting Transformer

Summary: Use of Builder for Java components results in javax.xml.parsers.FactoryConfigurationError
Description: This error is most frequently encountered when integrating a Builder for Java component into a J2EE web application or any other Java application which makes use of JAXP.? The following exception is thrown after the first component has initialized the MCR in the application's JVM:javax.xml.parsers.FactoryConfigurationError: Provider org.apache.xerces.jaxp.DocumentBuilderFactoryImpl not found at javax.xml.parsers.DocumentBuilderFactory.newInstance(Unknown Source)
Workaround: Add the following .jar file to the class path of the application: <matlabroot>/java/jarext/xercesImpl.jar

Summary: Add Items dialog box of the OPC Write block not displayed after namespace browsing error
Description: After clicking the "Add Items" button, if an error occurs browsing the OPC server namespace, the "Add Items" dialog box is not displayed.
Workaround: Create an OPC group object in the MATLAB workspace and import the group using the "Import from Workspace" button.For example you can create the group with the following commands:daObj = opcda('localhost', 'Matrikon.OPC.Simulation.1');connect(daObj);writegrp = addgroup(daObj, 'Write Group');itm1 = additem(writegrp, 'Bucket Brigade.Int4');itm2 = additem(writegrp, 'Bucket Brigade.Real8');disconnect(daObj);and then import the group "writegrp" using the "Import from Workspace" button.If you receive the following error message "??? Undefined function or variable 'block'"?you will need to apply the patch from the bug report? Trying to import a group into the OPC Write block can generate the error "Undefined function or variable 'block'".

Summary: Incorrect code generation: Code generated from an if branch that starts with a loop in Stateflow charts and Embedded MATLAB functions produces wrong answers
Description: Stateflow and Embedded Matlab generate incorrect code for if  statements where the if  branch starts with a loop ˇŞ such as while , do-while , or for  ˇŞ and the else  branch is not empty. The generated code moves the loop statement outside the if  statement.For example, consider this code from an Embedded MATLAB function: if (c1) { ???   while (c2) { ??? ??? block1; ???   } } else { ???   block2; }   Embedded MATLAB incorrectly transforms the <tt>if</tt> statement in generated C code for simulation, as follows: while (c2) {?? block1;}if (!c1) {   block2;} The example also applies to equivalent graphical flow graphs in Stateflow.
Workaround: 1. Download the attached file sfinit.m.2. Start R2007a MATLAB.3. At the MATLAB prompt, type:??? which private/sfinit.m??? This command should display a path such as:??? C:\Programs\R2007a\matlab\toolbox\stateflow\stateflow\private\sfinit.m? % Private to stateflow4. Back up this file to a different location.5. Save the downloaded file to the path displayed above.6. Restart MATLAB.

Summary: Stateflow Coder License is Required When Generating HDL Code from Embedded MATLAB Function Block
Description: When Simulink HDL Coder code generation is initiated from a model containing an Embedded MATLAB Function block,?the following error message is displayed:?Stateflow Coder license is missing! To generate code for custom targets, you require a Stateflow Coder license. Stateflow HDL code generation failed for chart 'Subsystem/Embedded MATLAB Function' (#49).

Summary: There is inconsistent behavior when passing cell data into a Builder for Excel addin.
Description: There is a bug in the process of converting cell data from Excel into either a numeric or character matrix. Data originating from a range of cells in an Excel worksheet comes into MATLAB as a cell array. Unless otherwise instructed, the data conversion logic attempts to convert this cell data into a matrix. In the special case in which the first element is not a scalar, but all other elements are scalars, this routine converts the cell array to a matrix, and truncates the first element to a scalar. For example, in the following VBA code: Dim x(0, 1 To 2) As Variant  x(0, 1) = "ab" x(0, 2) = "cd"  the array x is passed into MATLAB as a cell array of strings as shown below: {'ab' 'cd'}  When the second string is modified so that it has just one character, the data that comes into MATLAB is incorrect, as shown below: Dim x(0, 1 To 2) As Variant  x(0, 1) = "ab" x(0, 2) = "c"  The array x  is now passed into MATLAB as a character array as shown below: 'ac'
Workaround: To work around this problem, modify the data conversion flags to always pass cell data to MATLAB without attempting to convert to a matrix. For example, in the following VBA code:myObj.MWFlags.ArrayFormatFlags.InputArrayFormat = mwArrayFormatCellSetting the flag as shown will cause all data being passed to methods of the myObj object to be marshalled as cell arrays.

Summary: Index out of bounds when training an RBF neural network with NEWRB
Description: When NEWRB is called with a maximum number of neurons MN greater than the number of samples S an error is generated.
Workaround: When calling NEWRB always pick the maximum number of neurons MN to be the save or less than the number of sample vectors Q.There is no benefit to having more neurons than vectors, and the error can be avoided this way.

Summary: Deployed applications using imabsdiff, imdivide, imfilter, or immultiply can crash
Description: When you use the MATLAB Compiler to deploy an application that uses the Image Processing Toolbox functions imabsdiff , imdivide , imfilter , or immultiply , the deployed application can unexpectedly close.? ?  ?
Workaround: Add -a iptgetpref to the mcc command line.

Summary: Reading a DICOM file errors with message "Maximum variable size allowed by the function is exceeded"
Description: Using dicominfo  to read metadata from a DICOM file may produce this message: ??? Maximum variable size allowed by the function is exceeded. To request support for 64-bit arrays in this function please send this error to 64bit@mathworks.com  This message usually occurs with radiotherapy DICOM files that have sequences of nested attributes but do not contain at least one ˇ°itemˇ± tag.
Workaround: To resolve this issue, replace the version of the dicomparse?MEX-file in your existing MATLAB installation with the updated version contained in the attached file, following this procedure:?(1)???? Quit MATLAB.(2)???? Save a copy of? the?dicomparse?MEX file?in your installation.?For example, rename the file, adding the .old extension to the filename.?To determine the name and location of the dicomparse MEX file in your installation, execute the following command at the MATLAB prompt:which -all dicomparseThe exact file name of?the dicomparse MEX file will vary, depending on your platform.?For example, on a 32-bit Windows system, you would rename the file dicomparse.mexw32, in $MATLAB\toolbox\images\medformats\private\,? where $MATLAB is the root of your MATLAB installation. (3)???? Download the attached file and extract the version of dicomparse MEX file appropriate for your system from the attached files and copy it into the?directory listed in step2.(4)???? Restart MATLAB.

Summary: Port directions for the National Instruments PCI-6527 are reversed
Description: When I create a digital i/o object for my NI PCI-6527, the directions of the ports are reversed. The board supports Ports 0:2 for input (reading) and Ports 3:5 for output (writing).>> dio = digitalio('nidaq','Dev1') Display Summary of DigitalIO (DIO) Object Using 'PCI-6527'. ???????? Port Parameters:? Port 0 is port configurable for writing. ?????????????????????????? Port 1 is port configurable for writing. ?????????????????????????? Port 2 is port configurable for writing. ?????????????????????????? Port 3 is port configurable for reading. ?????????????????????????? Port 4 is port configurable for reading. ?????????????????????????? Port 5 is port configurable for reading. ????????????????????????????????????? Engine status:? Engine not required. DIO object contains no lines.
Workaround: This is caused by an erroneous entry in the initialization file that describes the device's capability.To work around this issue, do the following:    Quit MATLAB.    Find the following file:     $MATLAB\toolbox\daq\daq\private\mwnidaqmx.ini    where $MATLAB is the MATLAB root installation directory that you can obtain by issuing the following command in MATLAB:    matlabroot    Make a copy of this file and rename it to mwnidaqmx.ini.old at the MATLAB Command Prompt.    Open the file mwnidaqmx.ini in a text editor, and go to line 1019. Remove only lines 1019 through 1025, which are as follows:????    [PCI-6527]     ???? hasAnalogInput?? = false     ???? hasAnalogOutput? = false     ???? hasDigitalIO???? = true     ???? digitalPortSize? = 8,8,8,8,8,8     ???? digitalPortCap?? = 2,2,2,1,1,1        The correct entry for this device is further down in the file. Make sure that you do not remove this correct entry.    Save the file mwnidaqmx.ini and restart MATLAB.

Summary: Duplicate include paths?generated in model build makefile if?starting directory is root of Windows mapped drive
Description: For a Real-Time Workshop model build on a Windows system, if the?starting directory is at the root of a mapped drive, duplicate include paths are generated in the makefile. The inclusion of duplicate paths potentially can cause the include path line length to exceed the maximum allowed by the compiler tool chain. For example, consider a Windows system in which the?model directory is C:\myproj\mymodel\ , which is mapped to a drive F:\ . If the build is initiated from the real location on C: , no problems occur. However, if the build is initiated from the mapped location F:\ , duplicate paths are generated and problems with the include path line length may occur.
Workaround: To work around this bug, when mapping a drive for model building on a Windows system, map?the drive?at least 1 directory above the starting directory for the build.

Summary: A "java.lang.NegativeArraySizeException" error appears when fetching data from a database
Description: Attempts to fetch?data from a database throw the Java exception "java.lang.NegativeArraySizeException".
Workaround: The attached $MATLAB/toolbox/database/database/@cursor/fetch.m fixes this problem. It calculates the number of columns based on the size of the returned vector divided by the number of rows of the resultset.????

Summary: Memory leak in getframe on Linux and Solaris
Description: There is a memory leak in getframe  on Linux and Solaris.

Summary: Segmentation fault occurs when generating code for a model containing a function-call subsystem
Description: A segmentation fault might occur when generating code for a model containing a function-call subsystem that does the following:     Has multiple callers     Calls or contains a reusable subsystem  ?
Workaround: Disable code reuse for subsystems called by or contained in function-call subsystems that have multiple callers, for example, set their implementation to Inlined or Function instead of Auto or Reusable function.?

Summary: Y-axis?magnitude and phase limits cannot be set independently on Bode plots?with IOGrouping
Description: The y-axis limits for magnitude and phase on MIMO Bode plots cannot be set independently when IOGrouping is set to All.? When you enter a value for a magnitude limit, the corresponding phase limit automatically takes on the same value as the new magnitude limit.? Also, when you enter a value for a phase limit, the corresponding magnitude limit automatically takes on the same?value as the new phase limit.
Workaround: Go to $MATLAB\toolbox\shared\controllib\@ctrluis\@axesgrid where $MATLAB is the MATLAB root directory on your machine.? To determine the location of this?root directory, type the following at the MATLAB command prompt:    matlabroot        Rename setylim.m to setylim.m.old. It is recommended that you back up the file before applying the patch in order to easily revert back from the patch.    Download the attached patch (setylim.m) to $MATLAB\toolbox\shared\controllib\@ctrluis\@axesgrid.    Execute the following command at the MATLAB command prompt:     rehash toolboxcache

Summary: A model containing a Model block may fail to compile if a bus signal in the referenced model is testpointed
Description: If a model contains a referenced model with a testpointed bus signal, the model may fail to compile or simulate if the following condition is true:1. The testpointed bus signal in the referenced model has a mux signal as a component signalThe error produced will be similar to the following:### Model reference SIM target (referenced_msf.mexa64) for model referencedis out of date because referenced_msf.mexa64 does not exist### Updating model reference SIM target for model: referencedError: File: /tmp/slprj/sim/referenced/referenced.rtw Line: 930 Column: 15Identifier NumRegions multiply defined. Second and succeeding definitions ignored### Real-Time Workshop build procedure for model: 'referenced' aborted due to an error.??? Error: Errors occurred - aborting
Workaround: In the referenced model, do not log or testpoint?any bus signal that has a component mux signal.
