Description: In release 2007a,?tsmovavg  produces incorrect Exponential Moving Average (EMA) answers with a matrix input. However, vector inputs are unaffected.Affected usage: t =  [-25861      -26229      -19469      -21315      -21387      -16575      -22923      -22124      -18467      -27810      -27647      -21087      -29508      -29559      -23703        1913       -9355       -4256        -531       -8729       -2723        -594      -11680       -3466          66      -13223        -887        -871      -11393       -5400]?  tsmovavg(t, 'e', 6, 1) Unaffected usage: t =  [-25861      -21315      -22923      -27810      -29508        1913        -531        -594          66        -871]tsmovavg(t, 'e', 6, 1)

Summary: Generating an S-function checks out a Real-Time Workshop Embedded Coder license
Description: A Real-Time Workshop Embedded Coder license is checked out to generate a subsystem based S-function, even though the Use Embedded Coder  check box is cleared in the Generate S-function dialog.

Summary: Contec?AD12 and AD16 blocks won't compile but give duplicate definition errors
Description: The Contec?AD12-16E A/D block failed to build?because of?a naming conflict.
Workaround: Quit MATLAB.    Change directory to matlabroot, where matlabroot represents your MATLAB installation root, as returned by the MATLAB command matlabroot.    Download the attached file g370065.zip.    Unzip the file in matlabroot. This will replace the existing files.    Restart MATLAB.

Summary: Overstated Stateflow limitation documented regarding target and code format support for multiple instances of a model
Description: The table "Features Supported by Real-Time Workshop Targets and Code Formats" in the section ?"Choosing a Code Format for Your Application" in the Real-Time Workshop documentation?overstates a?Stateflow limitation.?The table entry "Multiple instances of model?" is qualified with?the?parenthetical phrase "no Stateflow blocks."? This phrase should be replaced wth the following footnote, which clarifies the Stateflow limitation:You can generate code for multiple instances of a Stateflow chart or subsystem containing a Stateflow chart, except when the Stateflow chart contains exported graphical functions or the Stateflow model contains machine parented events.

Summary: Certain sparse matrices passed back from a .NET component contain erroneous data
Description: The following MATLAB-code returns two sparse arrays that demonstrate the problem: function [a, b]= testsparse() a = sparse( 2, 2 ); a(1, 1) = 1; a(2, 2) = 4; b = sparse( 4, 4 ); b(1, 2) = 1; b(2, 2) = 2; b(3, 2) = 3; b(4, 2) = 4; endThe following client code calls the generated .NET component method:testsparseclass testClass =MWArray[] results = testClass .testsparse(2);WriteMatrix(results[0]new testsparseclass(););WriteMatrix(results[1]static private  void  WriteMatrix(MWArray matrix)?{ ???? for  (int  r= 1; r <= nm.Dimensions[0]; r++)????? {??????? for  (int  c= 1; c <= nm.Dimensions[1]; c++)?????????{??????????? // THIS GIVES THE WRONG RESULTS ??????????? Console.WriteLine(((double )matrix[r, c]).ToString();????????? }????? }?}
Workaround: The?following code can be used as a workaround in R2007a:staticprivate void WriteMatrix(MWArray matrix)?{??? double[,] nativeArray= (double[,])(matrix.ToArray(MWArrayComponent.Real));???? for (int r= 1; r <= nm.Dimensions[0]; r++)????? {??????? for (int c= 1; c <= nm.Dimensions[1]; c++)?????????{??????????? // Current Workaround??????????? Console.WriteLine((nativeArray[r-1, c-1]).ToString();?? ????????? }????? }?}Note: the sparse matrix is first converted to a native array before it is indexed.

Summary: MATLAB crashes after closing a connection from Datafeed Toolbox 7.4 (R2007a) to a Bloomberg data server
Description: Referencing a closed Bloomberg connection object in Datafeed Toolbox 7.4 (R2007a) causes a segmentation violation.

Summary: Saving certain objects to an existing MAT-file with the -7.3 option sometimes errors
Description: When you save some?classes of objects to a MAT-file, or another MATLAB file that uses the MAT-file format, and specify the -v7.3  option, a later save to that same filename using the -append  option produces an error. Notes:    Types of objects that produce the error include Java objects.     The -v7.3  option can be set using the save  function or via File > Preferences > General > MAT-Files .     If you save using a GUI, the -append  option might be used although that might not be apparent.  For example, resaving an RPT-file in the Report Generator Report Explorer, when the MAT-file save preference is set to -v7.3 , produces this error:Attempt to create or open handle failedSevere:Error: An InternalException was thrown.Caught unexpected MathWorks::System::InternalExceptionUnexpected error status flag encountered. ?Resetting to proper state
Workaround: As a workaround, after loading a MAT-file and making changes,?save it to a different filename.Another workaround is to save using the save function and do not specify the -append option.

Summary: The Simulink command Simulink.Bus.createObject may save bus objects that are unrelated to the newly created bus objects
Description: The Simulink command Simulink.Bus.createObject  can be used to create a bus object and any dependent bus objects for a Bus Creator block. Optionally, the command can also save the created bus objects in a MATLAB-file. If any unrelated bus objects exist in the base workspace prior to the execution of this command, one or more of the unrelated bus objects will be included in the MATLAB-file along with the newly created bus objects.
Workaround: Edit the MATLAB-file generated by Simulink.Bus.createObject and remove the section that defines the unrelated bus object(s).

Summary: Simulink may SEGV if a model contains S-functions and/or Stateflow blocks that share a global signal that is not correctly defined
Description: When a model contains more than one S-function and/or Stateflow block that share the same global Simulink Signal, and this signal is not correctly defined in the base workspace, Simulink may SEGV.? The SEGV has?a stack trace as shown in part below:------------------------------------------------------------------------?????? Segmentation violation detected at Fri Mar 23 16:35:53 2007------------------------------------------------------------------------Configuration:? MATLAB Version:?? 7.4.0.66805 (R2007b)? MATLAB License:?? unknown? Operating System: Microsoft Windows XP? Window System:??? Version 5.1 (Build 2600: Service Pack 2)? Processor ID:???? x86 Family 15 Model 11 Stepping 2, AuthenticAMD? Virtual Machine:? Java 1.5.0_07 with Sun Microsystems Inc. Java HotSpot(TM) Client VM mixed mode? Default Charset:? windows-1252Register State:? EAX = 124c5640? EBX = 956b4b88? ECX = 13824e20? EDX = 00000000? ESI = 124c5640? EDI = 0f80aa1d? EBP = 00ce9d5c? ESP = 00ce9d5c? EIP = 7a864c7e? FLG = 00210206Stack Trace:? [0] libmwsimulink.dll:char const * __cdecl GetSFcnFullPathFileName(class slBlock const *)(0x124c5640, 0x124c5e60, 0x15553190, 0x15be1eb0) + 14 bytes? [1] libmwsimulink.dll:int __cdecl SlFwdPropRuleNS::sl_compare_sfcnmth_keys(void *,void const * *,void const * *)(0, 0x00ce9dd0, 0x15be1eb8, 0x15537d40 "4wˇŔz") + 29 bytes? [2] libut.dll:struct _ghash_bucket * __cdecl ut_ghash_find_bucket(struct _ghash_table *,void const *,struct _ghash_bucket * * *,unsigned long *)(0x00ce9dd0, 0x00ce9da8, 0x15563474, 0x124c5e60) + 75 bytes? [3] libut.dll:_ut_ghash_query(0x15553190, 0x00ce9dd0, 0x00ce9ddc, 131) + 27 bytes
Workaround: Simulink posts error messages for global signals that are not correctly defined in the base workspace. Fixing the model by addressing these error messages should prevent the SEGV.??For example,?suppose that a model has?two Stateflow charts. One chart writes to a global data store and the other reads from the same global data store. If the Simulink.Signal corresponding to the global data store is not defined in the base workspace, a Simulink?error will?occur when the model updates or simulates, and a SEGV may occur also. Declaring the needed Simulink.Signal in the base workspace will eliminate the error message and prevent the SEGV.

Summary: help and doc functions can cause MATLAB to?become nonresponsive?on Linux platforms
Description: When you run?help  or doc  on some Linux platforms, MATLAB can become unresponsive.
Workaround: For helpAs a workaround for the problem with help,?put a comment symbol, %, at the start of?the following line in help.m:url = com.mathworks.mlwidgets.help.HelpInfo.getReferencePageUrl([topic '.html']);In R2007a, this is line 390 of help.m.? The help.m file is?located at matlabroot/toolbox/matlab/helptools, where?matlabroot is the directory in which MATLAB is installed on your system, which you can determine by running the matlabroot function.???????Commenting out this line disables the links to the reference pages in the output of the help function.For docInstead of using the doc function to access a reference page,?use the Help browser Contents entry for the product?and its?Functions - Alphabetical List.If you need to access the reference page from a MATLAB-file, use the web function with the -helpbrowser option.?To determine?the url value, first view the reference page using the Functions - Alphabetical?List, and then select View > Page Location in the Help browser. The resulting dialog box provides the url to use with the web function.

Summary: Loading a saved SISO Design Tool Project generates an error.
Description: When loading a saved SISO Design Tool Project, the following load error may be encountered:Subscript indices must either be real positive or logicals.
Workaround: Copy the attached files into the following directory and overwrite the original files: (matlabroot)\toolbox\control\ctrlguis\@sisogui\@sisotool\  After updating the files, it is recommended that you execute the following line of code:  >> rehash toolboxcache

Summary: ICDF calculation for inverse Gaussian fails to converge
Description: In the Distribution Fitting Tool dfittool , fitting an inverse Gaussian distribution and evaluating or plotting the inverse cumulative distribution function (ICDF) of the fit creates warning messages, and the computed ICDF values are not accurate.  This problem can also occur when executing M ATLAB code generated by dfittool , or when calling the icdf  function with 'inversegaussian' as the specified distribution.
Workaround: Modify line 131 of $MATLABROOT/toolbox/stats/private/addinvg.m to read F = invgcdf(qNew,1,lambda0);

Summary: Database Toolbox: Update function incorrectly inserts character array data into database table
Description: Using @database/update to update a table with data from a MATLAB structure only inserts the first character in a row from a character array.

Summary: Simulation halts with error message if it reaches fixed limit on number of simultaneous events
Description: To prevent livelock, SimEvents software limits the number of simultaneous events. The limits are fixed, not user-configurable. Simulations in which one block executes 1000 simultaneous events or the whole model executes 100,000 simultaneous events halt with an error message like one of these: Error occurred in 'untitled/Event-Based Entity Generator'.  Exceededmaximum number of simultaneous events, 1000, at time 0.000000.Exceeded maximum number of simultaneous events, 100000, at time 0.000000.
Workaround: Identify the block or blocks that execute many simultaneous events, and perturb the execution times of the events by adding a small random number to the event times. For example, if the block is a server whose service time is constant, then change the Service time from parameter to Signal port t and add a random signal to the signal representing the desired service time. As a result, the events will no longer be simultaneous.

Summary: Project created with mcc can't be compiled using deploytool
Description: The project files created?using the mcc  command cannot be compiled with deploytool .
Workaround: There are two possible workarounds to this issue:1. Use the command line tool, MCC, to compile the project:?????? mcc -F foo.prj???2.?Go to the Project Settings dialog box in the Deployment Tool, and in the General section, change the "Intermediate" directory to remove the trailing file seperator.?

Summary: MATLAB crashes on incorrect expressions involving attempts to acess fields of a structure returned by a function
Description: Attempting to access fields of a structure in the following manner is invalid and causes MATLAB to crash.??In this example,?fcn  is the name?of a function that returns a structure that has a field named time :??? fcn(3).time(end) This bug has been fixed so that MATLAB?generates?an error instead of crashing.? To avoid getting this error in the future, use the syntax shown below:??? x = fcn;??? x(3).time(end); Except in rare cases, MATLAB does not allow indexing the result of a function call, and certainly does not allow the indexing to involve end .
Workaround: The best rule is never to directly index the result of a function call, and especially not by "end".

Summary: ActiveX?INVOKE commands?using incorrect case take much longer in MATLAB 7.4 (R2007a) than earlier versions
Description: When I execute the following code in MATLAB 7.4 (R2007a): f = figure('position', [300 300 600 500]);cal = actxcontrol('mscal.calendar', [0 0 600 500], f)tic;invoke(cal,'NextDay');toctic;invoke(cal,'Nextday');toc The second call takes much longer than the first one. However,?this was not the case?in previous releases.
Workaround: There is a bug in MATLAB 7.4 (R2007a) in the way that it handles an ActiveX invoke command with?an?inaccurate case parameter.?In this?example, the library specifies NextDay, but the command uses Nextday.To work around the issue, download the attachment_368993_20070507.zip from Attachments below and extract comcli.dll file. Use this file to replace the existing file in the directory?$MATLABROOT/bin/win32/, where $MATLABROOT is obtained by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB Command prompt.

Summary: Calling sfprint with a handle returned by sfgco does not work.
Description: Calling the Stateflow function?sfprint  with a handle returned by the function?sfgco ?does not print the current Stateflow object as expected.
Workaround: Call sfprint with the internal Id of the Stateflow object, as in this example:>>h = sfgco>>sfprint(h.id, 'png')If?h is a chart object, this sequence of commands?will print the chart to a .png file.

Summary: Name-based interconnection feature in feedback function causes illicit error
Description: An illicit error may occur when using the name-based interconnection feature in the feedback function.? For example, the following code causes an error:G = tf({[1 0];[0 1]},{1;1},'inputname','ip','outputname',{'op1';'op2'});H = tf([1 2],'inputname',{'op1';'op2'},'outputname','ip');feedback(G,H,'name') The error is due to an incorrect validation check in the code.
Workaround: Use the index-based syntax to specify the connection of? the two systems.

Summary: Generating code for C5000 models utilizing Inf and NaN values produces incorrect results
Description: In some cases, code generation with C5000 processors is broken because C variables in the code, such as rtInf , rtMinusInf , rtNaN , rtInfF , rtMinusInfF , and rtNaNF , use incorrect, nonfinite data types. Having the incorrect data types in the generated code can cause the results from running your generated code on your processor to be different from your simulation results.You might get wrong results in the following cases: When you use the generic real-time (GRT) target, ccslink_grt.tlc , nonfinite numbers are generated during code generation. You get incorrect answers when you pass Inf  and NaN  as parameters to any block in your model. When you use the embedded real-time (ERT) target, ccslink_ert.tlc , nonfinite numbers are generated as an option during code generation.? The nonfinite numbers can cause your generated code to produce wrong results. To check whether you are generating the optional nonfinite numbers, follow these steps:  Select Simulation  > Configuration Parameters  from the model menu bar. Select Real-Time Workshop  from the Select  tree. Check whether you selected Non-finite numbers . When you select this option, your model generates optional nonfinite numbers and you may encounter incorrect results.   To prevent the incorrect answers from occurring when you use the GRT target, download and install the patch provided in the Workaround. To prevent the incorrect answers from occurring when you use the ERT target, do one of the following tasks:  Clear the Non-finite numbers  option on the Real-Time Workshop pane in the Configuration Parameters for your model. Do this if you do not need to generate the optional nonfinite numbers. If you need the nonfinite numbers, download and install the patch provided in the Workaround .  ?
Workaround: Follow these steps to apply a software patch that fixes the code generation problem. This patch applies only to MATLAB version 7.4 (R2007a) and Link for Code Composer Studio V3.0.1. Download attachment_368842_20070831.zip.2. Extract the following patch files from attachment_368842_20070831.zip:patch_installer_368842.mpatch_368842.zip3. In MATLAB, and enter ver at the command prompt to verify that you are using MATLAB version 7.4 (R2007a).4. In MATLAB, change Current Folder to the location of the patch files.5. Enter the following command at the MATLAB prompt to install the patch:patch_installer_368842('install')? Installing the patch can take a few minutes.

Summary: Building subsystem of model that uses configuration set reference fails
Description: Although Real-Time Workshop supports building a Simulink model that uses configuration set references, building a subsystem of the model fails with the following error: Cannot set simulation parameters of temporary model match those of the block's parent model.Error using ==> set_param--> A configuration set reference does not allow writing to parameters in the source configuration set. The following subsystem build methods fail for a model that uses configuration set references:     Right-clicking the subsystem and selecting Real-Time Workshop > Build Subsystem .     Right-clicking the subsystem and selecting Real-Time Workshop > Generate S-Function .     Executing the command rtwbuild('model /subsystem ') .
Workaround: To work around this bug, do not use a configuration set reference in a model when building a subsystem of that model.

Summary: When plotting a single vector, quiverm or quiver3m makes its length zero
Description: The quiverm  and quiver3m  functions use a heuristic to automatically scale vector lengths that requires at least two distinct input locations, otherwise zero-length vectors result. These functions should suppress automatic scaling when only a single distinct location is given.
Workaround: To work around this problem, you can suppress automatic scaling by explicitly specifying a scale of zero. Scaling is controlled by the fifth argument to quiverm, S. For example, to display a single vector, the last line of the reference page example for quiverm could be changed to:quiverm(lat0(1),lon0(1),u(1),v(1),'r',0)A similar work-around can by used with quiver3m.Alternatively, you can update your installation as follows:1. Exit MATLAB.2. Make a backup copy of the following files. MATLABROOT\matlab\toolbox\map\mapdisp\quiverm.m MATLABROOT\matlab\toolbox\map\mapdisp\quiver3m.mMATLABROOT represents the name of your MATLAB installation directory, as returned by the matlabrootfunction.For example, rename the existing file, adding "old" to the file extension, quiverm.m.OLD3. Copy the versions of quiverm.m and quiver3m.m from the attachment to the directory specified in the previous step.4. Copy the new file multipleDistinctLocations.m from the attachment into the directory MATLABROOT/toolbox/map/mapdisp/private.4. Restart MATLAB.5. Run the rehash toolbox command at the MATLAB prompt.

Summary: Rate Limiter block might produce incorrect results when conditional input branch optimization is on
Description: The Rate Limiter block outputs incorrect results when all the following conditions apply: The Rate Limiter block is located on a signal path that becomes a data input for a Switch block. On the Optimization  pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box, the Conditional input branch execution  parameter is selected.
Workaround: To work around this issue, disable the Conditional input branch execution parameter on the Optimization pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box.

Summary: Incorrect code generation: casts in Embedded MATLAB may overflow or saturate incorrectly
Description: Embedded MATLAB's optimizer incorrectly optimizes casts to integral types when overflow occurs. For example:??? q = [-1 -1; -1 -1];??? b = zeros(2,2);??? b(:) = uint16(q);According to MATLAB semantics, the cast to uint16 should saturate on overflow. Hence, the correct answer is:??? b = [0 0; 0 0]Due to a bug in Embedded MATLAB's optimizer, this overflow checking is sometimes omitted. In this example the following incorrect answer is computed:??? b = [65535 65535; 65535 65535]This bug occurs in examples that use integral casts (int8, int16, int32, uint8, uint16, uint32) and casts to fixed-point types with trivial scaling, but only when overflow occurs. For example, int8(x) may produce an incorrect answer when x = 300, but not when x? = 10.? Not all instances of overflow are affected.? Triggering the bug requires a specific chain of events that is hard to predict.The bug can occur both in simulation and in Real-Time Workshop. However, it is much less likely to occur in simulation with debugging enabled for the Embedded MATLAB Function block. (Debugging is enabled by default.) It is more likely to occur in Real-Time Workshop or in simulation with debugging disabled. Therefore, the bug can usually? be identified by comparing  the simulation results with debugging enabled and disabled.For more information, refer to the technical solution .
Workaround: Change your code so that overflow checking is not required. (Do not cast numbers that are out of range.)

Summary: FMINUNC (medium-scale) has a relative stopping test at initial guess comparing the initial gradient against itself
Description: The main stopping condition in the medium-scale algorithm in FMINUNC is a relative test. It compares the magnitude of the current gradient against that of the initial gradient.However, this test is also done in particular at the initial guess x0 , comparing the initial gradient against itself. Because of the way it is implemented, this comparison does not adversely affect the great majority of the problems.

Summary: Embedded MATLAB reports logicals with values in the range 0 to 255 instead of 0 to 1
Description: When constant folding conversions of numeric values into logicals, Embedded MATLAB does not truncate values to fall in the range of 0 to 1, leading to truth values between 0 and 255. This bug only occurs when the conversion occurs at compile time (and the final values show up in the generated code).
Workaround: When converting non-logical values to logical values, use the form x~=0 to force the conversion to truncate values to fall in the range of 0 to 1.

Summary: Incorrect code generation: code that gives wrong answer can be generated when Enabled Subsystem blocks are connected to IC ports
Description: Real-Time Workshop generates incorrect code when Enabled Subystem blocks are connected to IC ports under the following conditions:    Two copies of the same Enabled Subsystem block are placed in a model.     The output of one Enabled Subsystem block is connected to an IC port (for example, the IC port of an Integrator block).     The other Enabled Subsytem block:            Is not connected to an IC port.         Does not explicitly specify an initial output.         Is connected to a Constant block.
Workaround: Always specify an initial output for Enabled Subsystem blocks.

Summary: Generating code for Bitwise Operator blocks with Boolean signals might crash MATLAB
Description: Suppose your model contains a Bitwise Operator block whose input and output signals are of type Boolean. If you use Real-Time Workshop to generate code for such a model, MATLAB crashes.
Workaround: To work around this issue, cast a Boolean signal to an integer data type before feeding the signal to a Bitwise Operator block. For example, insert a Data Type Conversion block before the Bitwise Operator block and convert its input signal to an int8 type.

Summary: Get error "Undefined function or variable 'ctfroot'" when using daqsupport
Description: When I use the command daqsupport , I get the message: ??? Undefined function or variable 'ctfroot'. Error in ==> daqsupport at 109 root = ctfroot; %#ok<NASGU> ?This has been identified as a bug in Data Acquistion Toolbox.
Workaround: To work around this issue, please download and extract the file daqsupport.m, and replace the file daqsupport.m in the directory $MATLABROOT\toolbox\daq\daq (where $MATLABROOT is the location where MATLAB was installed).

Summary: Code generated from Rotation Matrix2VR block occasionally invalid
Description: Models with the Rotation Matrix2VR block occasionally generate invalid code.  A compiler error results.A situation known to lead to invalid code is where the input signal line to the Rotation Matrix2VR block comes from a Mux block.
Workaround: Download the attached file and copy it into the following directory. Overwrite the existing file.$matlabroot/toolbox/physmod/mech/mech/tlc_c/

Summary: Relative directory names in MATLAB path produce spurious warning: "The file containing block diagram <name> is not on the MATLAB path"
Description: MATLAB may erroneously issue a warning when loading a model from a library that is referenced by a relative path name.For example, assume the model mymodel  uses the simulink library? "C:\work\libs\mylibrary.mdl".    ? Set up the relative path name and open the model:  ? >> cd C:\work>> addpath libs>> open_system mymodel    Even though the library is properly loaded, the following warning is issued:  ? The file containing block diagram 'mylibrary' is not on the MATLAB path    ?
Workaround: Use absolute directory names to prevent this warning. ?For example:>> addpath C:\work\project\libs?Or, more generally:>> addpath(fullfile(pwd,'libs'))?

Summary: Error occurs when printing a figure or exporting a figure's contents to a clipboard.
Description: An error occurs when performing the following tasks on loaded images that were created and saved in release R2007a Control System Toolbox or System Test:    Printing      Exporting image contents to a clipboard
Workaround: Copy the attached file into the following directory and overwrite the original file: (matlabroot)\toolbox\shared\controllib\@ctrluis\@axesgrid\ After updating the file, it is recommended that you execute the following line of code: >> rehash toolboxcache

Summary: Blocks from the Filter Design Toolbox library cannot be opened in subsystems
Description: Blocks from the Filtering > Filter Design Toolbox library cannot be opened when placed in subsystems.
Workaround: To work around this issue, download the attached file and place it in the following directory$MATLAB\toolbox\shared\filterdesignlib\@FilterDesignDialog\@DesignBlock\where $MATLAB is the MATLAB root directory on your machine, as returned by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt.Alternatively, you can design filters by first placing the blocks in the top-level model, and then moving them into subsystems once the filters are designed.

Summary: The Output Port block incorrectly indicates that the mixer output frequency is negative
Description: When your model contains one or more mixer blocks whose net effect is to downconvert the input signal by more than 1 GHz, the Output Port block incorrectly generates an error message. The error message says 'Mixer output frequency is negative'. It appears when you open the Output Port block after performing one of the following actions: Run a simulation Update the diagram Click the Update Diagram  button to initialize the model  In all cases, the output frequency is not actually negative.
Workaround: To work around this issue, replace the version of the getDialogSchema.p files in your current installation with the updated version attached, as follows:Get the name of the MATLAB installation directory, matlabroot, as returned by the matlabroot function.Exit MATLAB.Rename the following files:matlabroot\toolbox\rfblks\rfblks\@rfblksdialog\@OutputPort\getDialogSchema.pto matlabroot\toolbox\rfblks\rfblks\@rfblksdialog\@OutputPort\getDialogSchema.oldThis creates a backup copy of the file.Download the attached files, getDialogSchema.p to the directory specified in the previous step.Restart MATLAB.Run the rehash toolboxcache command at the MATLAB prompt.

Summary: Some Statistics Toolbox code may fail to compile
Description: The MATLAB Compiler is intended to be able to compile most code in the Statistics Toolbox, with the exception of certain graphical user interfaces. Under some conditions, however, functions that invoke code related to the Statistics Toolbox may fail to compile. Known examples include:    Code generated from the distribution fitting tool dfittool      Code calling the treefit  function  The problem occurs with code that needs to run functions in the private subdirectory of the Statistics Toolbox. In the cases listed here, the compiler is unable to determine which private functions are required. If you attempt to run the compiled code outside MATLAB, you may get an error that some functions are missing.
Workaround: It may be possible to avoid this problem by editing the dfswitchyard function in the Statistics Toolbox, and adding the following lines at the bottom:%#function statgetargs%#function mgrp2idx%#function dfgetdistributions%#function dfhistbins

Summary: Incorrect code generation: Persistent variables may produce incorrect results in Embedded MATLAB functions under rare circumstances.
Description: Persistent variables in Embedded MATLAB functions may produce incorrect results when all of the following conditions apply: You call a function containing persistent variables twice within the same function. One call site gets inlined in the generated code and the other does not.  In these circumstances, a faulty optimization may incorrectly move generated code around the call site, causing the generated code to produce incorrect answers. This is unlikely to occur because such code movement depends on a number of factors about how the code is structured that are hard to characterize.

Summary: Plot Tools can cause a java assertion to be thrown to a log window if an attempt is made to close the corresponding figure before the Plot Tools have fully initia
Description: Closing a figure window while Plot Tools are in the process of opening can cause a java assertion to be thrown to a log window. The problem is most noticeable the first time Plot Tools are opened when initialization typically requires more time due to class loading and object instantiation.
Workaround: There is currently no work around other than just waiting for Plot Tools to finish initialization.

Summary: Changes to chart library lost if not explicitly saved
Description: If you have a Stateflow chart in a library that is not opened and then modify that chart using the "Edit Library" menu item of a chart that links to the library then you will never get a dialog asking you to save the library (hence your changes may be lost).
Workaround: Either keep the library chart opened in a window while editing it (you may need to manually open it) or make sure that you open and save the library before exiting Matlab.

Summary: textscan freezes when given stdin as the file identifier input
Description: Calling the textscan  function with the file identifier argument set to zero (i.e., the value for stdin ) can cause MATLAB to hang instead of returning an error. Here is an example of this calling syntax:   textscan(0, '%s', ...)
Workaround: You can work around this bug by not passing 0 (stdin) as the first argument to textscan.

Summary: Incorrect code generation: Real-Time Workshop may ignore type conversion to fixed-point from an integer or another fixed-point data when both block reduction and expression folding optimizations are enabled.
Description: In rare cases, when you enable both block reduction and expression folding optimizations, Real-Time Workshop ignores the setting in Data Type Conversion blocks to adjust the scaling of fixed-point signals without modifying the stored integers ˇŞ that is, when you set input and output to have equal stored integer. As a result, the type conversion does not occur and generated code is incorrect.This bug does not occur during normal mode simulation. Therefore, you can detect this bug if you have adequate simulation coverage by comparing results from normal mode simulation against the results of the generated code.
Workaround: Disable either block reduction or expression folding in the Optimization pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog. To disable block redution, clear the Block reduction option; to disable expression folding, clear the Eliminate superfluous temporary variables (Expression folding) option.

Summary: (Communications Blockset Raised Cosine filter blocks) "Visualize filter with FVTool" button is missing in R2007a
Description: The Raised Cosine Transmit Filter  and Raised Cosine Receive Filter  blocks, in the "Comm Filters" library of the Communications Blockset, should have a Visualize filter with FVTool  button in the mask dialog.  This button is a feature to display the filter's frequency response in FVTool.  However, the Visualize filter with FVTool  button is missing in R2007a.
Workaround: Please follow these steps to replace two files:     Rename the file      ForRaisedCosineReceiveFilter_getDialogSchema.p      to      getDialogSchema.p     and use it to replace:      matlabroot\toolbox\commblks\commblks\@commdialog\@RaisedCosineFilterRx\getDialogSchema.p    Rename the file      ForRaisedCosineTransmitFilter_getDialogSchema.p      to      getDialogSchema.p     and use it to replace:     matlabroot\toolbox\commblks\commblks\@commdialog\@RaisedCosineFilterTx\getDialogSchema.p    Restart MATLAB

Summary: License manager error after web install of Extended Symbolic Math Toolbox
Description: After downloading and installing R2007a MATLAB and the Extended Symbolic Math Toolbox, symbolic functions error the first time they are called. The error is as follows: License checkout failed. License Manager Error -5 Cannot find a license for Symbolic_Toolbox. Make sure an INCREMENT line for Symbolic_Toolbox exists in the license file, and that the formatting of the license file is correct.

Summary: M-Lint for complicated functions might result in low memory?
Description: When you use M-Lint with?a function or script?that is?complicated, that is, has dozens of variables and complicated flow control, MATLAB might use excessive memory.
Workaround: If you?experience problems due to low memory availability and you use M-Lint for complicated MATLAB files,?use File > Preferences? > M-Lint to disable integrated M-Lint in the Editor/Debugger?Restart MATLAB to reclaim lost memory.You can open complicated files in the Editor/Debugger with integrated M-Lint enabled if you are not changing the files. M-Lint uses memory only when a file changes.

Summary: sym2poly does not allow 'k' as a polynomial variable
Description: The sym2poly  function errors when called with a symbolic polynomial in the variable 'k' . For example:syms kf=1+2*k;sym2poly(f) Returns:??? Error using ==> sym/sym2poly1+2*k is not a polynomial.
Workaround: Use a symbolic variable other than 'k'.

Summary: The struct _INTERFACE_INFO (declared in mclmcr.h) conflicts with the struct _INTERFACE_INFO declared in ws2tcpip.h
Description: When building a C++?application with?MSVC++ 2005 that includes a C/C++ shared library developed with?MATLAB Compiler, a name conflict can result if?<afxsock.h>  is included in the same source file as mclmcr.h . When this occurs, the following error message is displayed:>c:\program files\matlab\r2007a\extern\include\mclmcr.h(1486) : error C2011: '_INTERFACE_INFO' : 'struct' type redefinition > C:\Program Files\Microsoft Visual Studio 8\VC\PlatformSDK\include\ws2tcpip.h(367) : see declaration of '_INTERFACE_INFO'
Workaround: Do not include include <afxsock.h> in the same source file as MathWorks includes. This?requires breaking your application source code into separate files.

Summary: On-Chip Debugging / On-Chip PIL Not Supported on ARM Hardware
Description: For ARM processors the CrossView Pro instruction set simulator can be used for debugging and processor-in-the-loop (PIL) cosimulation, but there is currently no on-chip debugging or PIL support.
Workaround: To work around this issue:1) You can contact TASKING for the latest information on CrossView Pro on-chip debugging support for ARM hardware.?? 2) You can contact Hitex for a solution to debug a Link for TASKING generated application on ARM hardware, however this will not provide PIL support.

Summary: uigetfile: wildcards before extension in?FilterSpec option produces incorrect results
Description: When you run uigetfile  with wildcards before the file extension, for example *yourterm.mat  or yourterm*.mat , the filtered results are blank and no files are shown, unless the user chooses All Files .

Summary: Variable in nested function has incorrect value.
Description: In some cases, when an assignment to a variable occurs soon after a call to a nested function, that variable will have an incorrect value in the nested function. For example, in the following code, EHH  is empty in the call to testfnc : function isolate??? for i = 1:2??????? EHH = [0 0];??????? eval('0;');??????? testfnc??????? EHH = [0 0];??? end??? function testfnc()??????? EHH(1);??? endend
Workaround: Use the following command to disable the JIT Accelerator:feature('accel','off');

Summary: Passing a scalar NaN to deval causes an infinite loop
Description: An infinite loop occurs when a scalar NaN is entered as an input parameter to the deval function. For example: sol = ode45(@vdp1,[0 20],[2 0]);x = nan;y = deval(sol,x);
Workaround: This can be avoided by using isnan to check for a NaN value before passing the variable to the function deval. If it is not possible to determine whether you are passing a NaN value, you can download and extract the attached file to the [MATLABROOT]/toolbox/matlab/funfun/ directory.

Summary: Function dist2str errors when units is 'feet' or 'meters'
Description: Function dist2str  is supposed to support "any valid distance units string," but causes an error when given 'feet'  or 'meters' .
Workaround: To work around this issue, replace the dist2str.m function file in your installation with the version in the attachment, as follows:    Exit MATLAB.    Make a backup copy of the following files.    MATLABROOT\matlab\toolbox\map\map\dist2str.m    MATLABROOT represents the name of your MATLAB installation directory, as returned by the matlabroot function.    For example, rename the existing file, adding "old" to the file extension: dist2str.m.old        Copy the version of dist2str.m from the attachment into the directory specified in the previous step.    Restart MATLAB.    Run the rehash toolbox command at the MATLAB prompt.

Summary: Right-shifting unsigned fixed-point quantities performs arithmetic instead of logical shift in Embedded MATLAB
Description: Embedded MATLAB incorrectly performs an arithmetic right shift for bitshift(a,k) , where a  is an unsigned fixed-point value and k  is negative. A logical right shift is expected.

Summary: Simulink library forwarding table does not work for a configurable Subsystem block
Description: The Simulink library forwarding table does not work for a Configurable Subsystem block. For example,   set_param('Lib1', 'ForwardingTable', {'Lib1/A','Lib2/A'})   fails to forward the old Configurable Subsystem block Lib1/A to the new block Lib2/A.

Summary: Segmentation Violation when?I invoke CCSDSP with an unsupported CCS version
Description: I get a segmentation violation when?I invoke CCSDSP with an unsupported CCS version.
Workaround: Refer to the Documentation on what CCS version(s) is supported for the particular release of Link for CCS. If the CCS version you have is not listed, you cannot use Link for CCS product with it.

Summary: Import Wizard: error message upon starting
Description: When starting the Import Wizard for the second time, the following message might occur:??? Error using ==> uiimport at 69 Cannot open the Import Wizard on a file while the Import Wizard is open.
Workaround: Apply the attached patch by performing the following steps:    Quit MATLAB.    Download?the attached patch file?to the matlabroot/java directory.    Start MATLAB.    Change the current directory to the java directory by running    cd(fullfile(matlabroot, 'java'))    Unzip the patch file by running    unzip patch_g366626.jar    This creates files named MJFileChooserPerPlatform* in matlabroot/java/patch/com/mathworks/mwswing/    Restart MATLAB. You should no longer experience the problem.

Summary: MATLAB R2007a startup fails on Windows
Description: R2007a MATLAB fails to start (become unresponsive?during startup) on certain Windows systems. The status bar shows Initializing, but never moves to Ready.Pressing Ctrl+C  might provide some control and show an error like the following in the Command Window: Error in ==> matlabrc at 34 ???????????? matlabpath(pathdef); This problem results from MATLAB's attempt to identify the MATLAB  folder in the Windows My Documents  folder upon startup.
Workaround: As a workaround, try the following steps:    Using a text editor such as Notepad, edit the file matlabroot\toolbox\local\userpath.m, where matlabroot is the directory in which MATLAB is installed.? Alternatively you can replace the userpath.m with the attachment below.    In lines 13 and 16    [rc p] = dos('startdir $documents\MATLAB -a');    remove the -a option. Lines 13 and 16 should now be    [rc p] = dos('startdir $documents\MATLAB');    That should resolve the problem and MATLAB should start successfully.    If that does not work, try specifying an exact path in line 13 and 16 for $documents, which is the path to your Windows My Documents directory. For example:    [rc p] = dos('startdir C:\WINNT\Profiles\User.DOMAIN\My Documents\MATLAB');    To determine the path to your My Documents directory, in a Windows Explorer, right-click My Documents and select Properties. The path is the value in Target folder directory.         MATLAB should now start successfully.

Summary: Rotate block may not rotate precisely about the center of the image.
Description: When any of the input image dimensions are even, the Rotate block does not rotate the image precisely about the center of the image.

Summary: Incorrect error for nonvirtual bus fed with some continuous signals and some signals updated at the fastest discrete rate
Description: Simulink will incorrectly issue an error message when the following are all true for either a Bus Creator or Bus Assignment block:  the block outputs a nonvirtual bus all of the elements of the bus object specify an inherited (-1) sample time at least one input signal has a continuous sample time all other input signals have either a continuous sample time or the fastest discrete rate the model is using a fixed step solver The message Simulink erroneously issues is:Sample time mismatch. When a Bus Creator or Bus Assignment block outputs a nonvirtual bus, all of the signals driving its input ports must have the same sample time. This restriction applies even if the elements of the object defining the bus specify an inherited (-1) sample time. The sample time of the signal ([0.005, 0]) driving input port 1 of 'path_to_block_in_your_model' does not match the sample time of the block ([0, 0]).   Note:  The error message will include sample times appropriate for your model. The sample times shown in the above example are for illustration only. ? Tip:  Turn on Sample Time Colors  to help see the occurrences of this problem    On the Format  menu click Port/Signal Displays , and then Sample Time Colors
Workaround: The workaround depends on the version of Simulink you are using:    For releases prior to R2006b, place a rate transition block on the input signals with the fastest discrete rate. Set its Output port sample time parameter to 0    For R2006b and subsequent releases, either use a variable step solver or create separate buses for the continuous and fastest discrete rate signals

Summary: Incorrect handling of illegal rate transition when model option "Automatically handle data transfers between tasks" is selected
Description: When all of following conditions are true for a model, Simulink fails to issue an error message for an illegal rate transition and the model's generated code does not handle the illegal rate transition:         Automatic insertion of Rate Transition blocks is on; that is, Automatically handle data transfers between tasks  is selected on the Solver  pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box.              Rate transition occurs between the source and destination of a Unit Delay blocks.               The Unit Delay blocks can not handle rate transition.     A Unit Delay block can handle rate transition if all of the following are true:             The source sample time is slower than the destination sample time         A sample time equal to the source sample time is set for the Unit Delay block         Both the source and destination sample time offsets are zero         The period of the source sample time is an integer multiple of the period of the destination sample time
Workaround: To work around this bug, do either of the following:        Avoid connecting a Unit Delay block directly with another Unit Delay that has rate transition. Insert a nonvirtual block between the Unit Delay blocks.            Clear the model option Automatically handle data transfers between tasks on the Solver pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box.

Summary: Creating a shared MCR results in a compilation error
Description: When building a component using MATLAB Builder for Java with the Shared MCR option enabled (mcc -S  flag), the following error is encountered:<Path-to-generated-java-source>:32: unreported exception com.mathworks.toolbox.javabuilder.MWException; must be caught or declared to be thrown ???????? super(); ????????????? ^ <Path-to-generated-java-source>:37: unreported exception com.mathworks.toolbox.javabuilder.MWException; must be caught or declared to be thrown ???????? super(componentData); ????????????? ^
Workaround: Edit the generated .java file so that the generated methods from which the error originates throw exception type MWException.? Similar throw declarations may need to be propagated up the call stack.

Summary: The quantizer object does not recognize the DataMode property
Description: The quantizer  object does not recognize the DataMode  property. For example, the following code shown in the product documentation errors out:q = quantizer('datamode','fixed','format',[16,14],'overflowmode','saturate','roundmode','ceil')
Workaround: To work around this issue, do not use property name/property value pairs in the quantizer constructor.? For example, instead of the code above, useq = quantizer('fixed',[16,14],'saturate','ceil')

Summary: An atomic subsystem that contains a Level-2 MATLAB-File S-Function can cause Real-Time Workshop to generate wrong reusable functions
Description: Real-Time Workshop might generate wrong reusable functions if the model has the following characteristics: The model contains multiple instances of an atomic system. The atomic system contains a Level-2 MATLAB-File S-Function. The Level-2 MATLAB-File S-Function block in the different instances of the atomic system are passed dialog parameter values that differ between atomic systems. The TLC file of the Level-2 MATLAB-File S-Function generates code based on these dialog parameter values of the S-function.
Workaround: As a workaround, set the Real-Time Workshop system code option in the Atomic Subsystem block parameter dialog box to Inline or Function.

Summary: Current Directory browser: creating a new model?seems to create two files
Description: In the Current Directory browser, when you create a new model, for example by selecting File > New > Model , two new untitled.mdl  files appear. When you name one of the new files, the other untitled.mdl  entry?should be?automatically removed; if not,?delete it.
Workaround: To prevent this problem, run Simulink before creating a new model in the Current Directory Browser.

Summary: M-Lint produces an error for certain incorrect code inside try/catch blocks
Description: Certain?incorrect MATLAB expressions, when used inside try/catch  blocks, can cause M-Lint to error. In the MATLAB Editor, this?appears as an M-Lint error message?associated with?the last line in the file: An internal M-Lint error?occurred. Please report this problem to?The MathWorks, along with this MATLAB file. An example of an expression that produces this error is try [] = foo; catch end
Workaround: Fix the incorrect expression in the try/catch block.

Summary: Selecting wrongly-enabled menu item can crash MATLAB
Description: If a block has an OpenFcn , the context menu item Mask Parameters...  is enabled even if no mask exists. Selecting the menu item can cause MATLAB to terminate abnormally.
Workaround: Do not select the Mask Parameters... context menu item if no mask exists.

Summary: Code generation fails for a Simulink model containing DM642EVM Video Capture or Video Display blocks
Description: Code generation for a Simulink model containing DM642EVM Video Capture or Video Display blocks may fail under certain conditions. Failure occurs at the link stage and an error message is reported by CCS and Simulink to the following effect: undefined                       symbol                           ---------------- _rgbPl_to_rgb565 first referenced     in file----------------  F:\Test\TI\TIC6000_R2007a\dm642evmedgedetection_ccslink\obj\dm642evmedgedetection.obj >> error: symbol referencing errors - 'dm642evmedgedetection.out' not built
Workaround: Download the attached patch installer patch_installer.m and the ZIP archive patch_366121_<MATLAB version>.zip?where ?<MATLAB version>?represents the version of? MATLAB you are using.To install the patch, follow these steps.    Start MATLAB.    Enter the following command at the prompt to install the patch:    ?    patch_installer('patch_366121_<MATLAB version>.zip', 'install')     ?        Follow the instructions on the MATLAB command line to complete the patch installation.?

Summary: Specifying particular block parameters might cause Simulink to crash on 64-bit Solaris systems
Description: On 64-bit Solaris systems, Simulink will crash if you:     Use the set_param  function to specify the value of a block's boolean parameter while the block's parameter dialog box is open. Here, boolean parameters refer to block options that you specify as either on  or off , typically by selecting or deselecting a check box on the block's parameter dialog box.     Open the Bitwise Operator block dialog box and specify the value of its Operator  parameter as NOT .  When Simulink crashes, it produces a bus error that displays a stack trace similar to the following: Stack Trace:  [  0] ffffffff34e195d0 /matlab/bin/sol64/libmwsimulink.so+14783952 (__1cVBoolean_set_check_fcn6FpkvpkcpnLmxArray_tag_pi_pnNslErrMsgQdDueue__+000048)  [  1] ffffffff34cb4dc0 /matlab/bin/sol64/libmwsimulink.so+13323712 (__1cRSLObsoletePrmDescSdialogSetInMx_Impl6kMpvpknLmxArray_tag__pnNslErrMsgQdDueue__+000360)  [  2] ffffffff34cb91c4 /matlab/bin/sol64/libmwsimulink.so+13341124 (__1cJSLPrmDescMsetValueInMx6kMpvpknLmxArray_tag_bpb_pnNslErrMsgQdDueue__+000196)  [  3] ffffffff34e1ab44 /matlab/bin/sol64/libmwsimulink.so+14789444 (__1cUString_set_check_fcn6FpkvpkcpnLmxArray_tag_ppc_pnNslErrMsgQdDueue__+002924)  [  4] ffffffff34e1ae04 /matlab/bin/sol64/libmwsimulink.so+14790148 (__1cUString_set_check_fcn6FpkvpkcpnLmxArray_tag_ppc_pnNslErrMsgQdDueue__+003628)  [  5] ffffffff348d43b0 /matlab/bin/sol64/libmwsimulink.so+9257904 (__1cJException2T6M_v_+015240)  ... Note that MATLAB terminates when using the Bitwise Operator block dialog to specify NOT  as its Operator  parameter.
Workaround: When interacting with the affected block parameters on 64-bit Solaris systems, use the workarounds described below.To specify programmatically the value of boolean parameters associated with blocks:    Ensure that the block's parameter dialog box is closed.    Use the set_param function to specify either off or on as the value of the block's boolean parameters.To specify NOT as the value of the Bitwise Operator block's Operator parameter:    Ensure that the Bitwise Operator block's parameter dialog box is closed.    Use the set_param function to specify the value of the block's logicop parameter as NOT.

Summary: Able to instantiate only a limited number of COM objects
Description: On Windows, while opening a MEX file, a file handle was?opened that was not subsequently closed. This resulted in a leak which, after repeated opening and closing of MEX files, eventually led to unavailability of resources.
Workaround: To work around this issue,?re-use the same instance of the COM object instead of destroying and recreating the object. If this is not possible (such as in a web application),?use the Object Instances Share MCR option.?Object Instances Share MCR?can be enabled in?DEPLOYTOOL by selecting?the option after selecting Settings > General. Choosing this option results in faster program execution since all instances of the COM object?use the same instance of the MCR and the MCR won't?need to be recreated for each instance of the object.?If you?compile with the MCC command or with DOTNETTOOL or COMTOOL,?use the -S or "Singleton" option which is the same as Object Instances Share MCR.?Be aware that?using the Object Instances Share MCR option causes global variables (and therefore COM object properties)?to be?static across all instances of the component.

Summary: PRINTPREVIEW function does not work in deployed applications
Description: If?you use the PRINTPREVIEW function to print a GUI in a standalone program you?receive this error message: Error in deployprint at 40Error in printdlg at 13Error in graphics\private\ppreview>onPrint at 856
Workaround: To work around this issue, use the DEPLOYPRINT function to print your GUI in deployed mode:if isdeployed??? deployprintelse??? printpreview(gcf); end

Summary: Receive Error "MCC: Buffer is too small for operation" when using analog output on some Measurement Computing hardware
Description: Under certain conditions, you may receive the following error when you use an analog output subsystem on Measurement Computing hardware: ao = analogoutput('mcc', '0');addchannel(ao, 0);putdata(ao, zeros(10, 1));start(ao)??? MCC: Buffer is too small for operation  Another error you may see is: MCC: To run in a hardware-clocked output mode, this hardware requires thatmore samples be queued using PUTDATA  ?
Workaround: This is caused by complex buffer sizing rules when working with Measurement Computing hardware. To workaround this issue, queue at least 1000 samples of data to the hardware as described.In MATLAB, type:ao = analogoutput('mcc', '0');addchannel(ao, 0);putdata(ao, zeros(1000, 1));start(ao)In Simulink, you can use the buffer block from Signal Processing to send signals of 1000 samples to the Analog Output block.

Summary: Bus editor does not save multi-line descriptions
Description: The Bus Editor does not save the contents of the Description field of a bus object if the text consists of more than one line.
Workaround: To add a multi-line description to a bus object, use the command line to set the 'Description' property of the object. For example:BusObject.Description = sprintf('This is a multi-line \n description');

Summary: xPC Target build process error "Undefined identifier im"
Description: If you build an xPC Target application from a Simulink model that contains blocks with tunable parameters of multidimensional size, the  build process generates the following error: matlabroot \toolbox\rtw\targets\xpc/target/build/tlc\xPCGenLib.tlc Line: 182 Column: 42Undefined identifier im
Workaround: xPC Target does not support parameters with multidimensional size. As a workaround:    Quit MATLAB.    Rename the following file:    matlabroot\toolbox\rtw\targets\xpc\target\build\tlc\capi_xpc.tlc        to    matlabroot\toolbox\rtw\targets\xpc\xpc\target\build\tlc\capi_xpc.tlc.old    (where matlabroot represents your MATLAB installation root, as returned by the MATLAB command matlabroot)    Download the attached file capi_xpc_R2007a.tlc.    After downloading, remove the suffix (_R2007a) from the downloaded file and place the file at:    matlabroot\toolbox\rtw\targets\xpc\xpc\target\build\tlc\        Restart MATLAB.

Summary: Documentation error implies support for invalid command option?for?SAVE function
Description: There is an error in one of the?examples?in?the?MATLAB documentation for the save  function that?implies that a?command option called -compress  is supported for this function. The error is in Example 6 on the save  reference page, in the line shown below: ??? save c2 x y -compress Although this command option had not been invalid in MATLAB versions 7.0 through 7.2, it was never officially supported and was not intended to be documented.?The save  function uses data compression by default when storing data in a MAT-file. To save without using compression, use the command ??? save c2 x y -v6
Workaround: You do not need to specify any command option with the save function to save data to a MAT-file in compressed format.

Summary: Event-based <tt>timeseries<\tt> sub-referencing methods error when passed <tt>tsdata.event<\tt> objects
Description: Passing a tsdata.event  object to any of the timeseries  object methods gettsafterevent , gettsbeforeatevent , or?gettsafterevent  will result in the following error: ??? Error using ==> timeseries.utGetEventTime at 48The second argument of utGetEventTime must be a valid event name. Error in ==> timeseries.gettsafterevent at 28??? index = utGetEventTime(this,event,'after'); The following code reproduces the error: ts = timeseries(randn(10,1)); e = tsdata.event('ev1',2); ts = addevent(ts,e); ts = gettsafterevent(ts,e)
Workaround: There are two workarounds:1. Use the event name instead of the object itself. For example, in the code reproducing the error given in the bug description, use:ts = gettsafterevent(ts,e.Name)2. Delete lines 47-48 of the file utGetEventTime.m (on the MATLAB path)

Summary: Some Link for CCS demos fail during code generation when Real-Time Workshop Embedded Coder is not installed
Description: The following Link for Code Composer Studio demos fail?during the code generation process if you do not have Real-Time Workshop Embedded Coder installed:     Real-Time Execution Task Profiling (c6713profiler )     Real-Time Execution Task Profiling? (c2808profiler )     Real-Time Execution Task Profiling? (c2812profiler )     Comparing Simulation and Target Implementation with Debug Methods (debugsumdiffdemo )    ?
Workaround: Download the attached patch installer patch_installer.m and the ZIP archive patch_365565.zip. This patch applies only to MATLAB Version 7.4 (R2007a).To install the patch, follow these steps.    Start MATLAB.    Enter the following command at the MATLAB command prompt to install the patch:    ?    patch_installer('patch_365565.zip', 'install')     ?        Follow the instructions on the MATLAB command line to complete the patch installation.?

Summary: Function regionprops can enable the MATLAB divide-by-zero warning
Description: In MATLAB R2007a, the divide-by-zero warning message is disabled by default. Calling the Image Processing Toolbox function regionprops  can cause the divide-by-zero warning message to become enabled.
Workaround: Replace the version of?regionprops in your current MATLAB installation with the updated version contained in the attached file.Exit MATLAB.Make a backup copy of the regionprops.m file in the matlabroot\toolbox\images\images directory, where matlabroot represents the name of your current installation directory, as returned by the matlabroot command. For example, rename the file, appending?"old" to the file name.Download the attached file, extract regionprops.m, and store it in the directory specified in the previous step.Restart MATLAB.Run the rehash toolbox command at the MATLAB prompt.

Summary: Blocks ignore the value of the rfdata object parameter in a model that was saved in an earlier release
Description: When you load a model that was last saved in an earlier release, the following blocks ignore the value of therfdata  object parameter in the block dialog box: General Amplifier General Passive Network General Mixer  Each of these blocks disables therfdata  object parameter and instead applies the default file data for that block.
Workaround: To work around this issue, replace the versions of the rfblksgeneralamplifier1.m, rfblksgeneralpassive1.m, rfblksgeneralmixer1.m?and isnewmodel.m files in your current installation with the updated versions attached, as follows:Get the name of the MATLAB installation directory, matlabroot, as returned by the matlabroot function.Exit MATLAB.Rename the following files:matlabroot\toolbox\rfblks\rfblksmasks\rfblksgeneralamplifier1.mmatlabroot\toolbox\rfblks\rfblksmasks\rfblksgeneralpassive1.mmatlabroot\toolbox\rfblks\rfblksmasks\rfblksgeneralmixer1.mmatlabroot\toolbox\rfblks\rfblksmasks\private\isnewmodel.m to  matlabroot\toolbox\rfblks\rfblksmasks\rfblksgeneralamplifier1.oldmatlabroot\toolbox\rfblks\rfblksmasks\rfblksgeneralpassive1.oldmatlabroot\toolbox\rfblks\rfblksmasks\rfblksgeneralmixer1.oldmatlabroot\toolbox\rfblks\rfblksmasks\private\isnewmodel.oldThis creates a backup copy of each file.Download the attached files, rfblksgeneralamplifier1.m, rfblksgeneralpassive1.m, rfblksgeneralmixer1.m?and isnewmodel.m to the directory specified in the previous step.Restart MATLAB.Run the rehash toolboxcache command at the MATLAB prompt.

Summary: Embedded MATLAB assertion on assigning one-dimensional vectors using multiple dimensions.
Description: This bug occurs when Embedded MATLAB Function blocks operate on one-dimensional input signals as if they were multidimensional matrices. Consider an example of a Simulink model that feeds a constant, one-dimensional input signal ˇŞ such as [10 20]  ˇŞ to an Embedded MATLAB Function block that contains the following code:function y = fcn(x)x(1,1,1) = 10;y = x; Even though input x  is a one-dimensional signal, the code assigns a value to x  as if it were a multi-dimensional matrix. An assertion occurs when you simulate this model or compile the Embedded MATLAB code. The first eml.mex*  entry on the stack trace is:EML::Infer_Private::make_matrix_match_subscripts(class CG_Node *) ...
Workaround: Remove trailing dimensions when accessing a one-dimensional signal. For example, rewrite the code above as follows:function y = fcn(x)x(1) = 10; % Trailing dimensions removedy = x;

Summary: Embedded MATLAB Editor: Embedded MATLAB Library Reference help item does not work
Description: In the Embedded MATLAB Editor, the Help menu item Embedded MATLAB Library Reference  does not work.
Workaround: To access the Embedded MATLAB library reference information from the Help menu in the Embedded MATLAB Editor, follow these steps:    In MATLAB, enter the following command:    cd(fullfile(matlabroot,'\toolbox\stateflow\stateflow\private'));        In this directory, replace the file eml_man.m with the version in the attached zip file.

Summary: mnrnd returns NaNs incorrectly under some conditions.
Description: Under some conditions, mnrnd  returns NaN values. This occurs when roundoff error causes the sum of the multinomial probabilities to differ from 1.
Workaround: There are two ways to work around this bug:1. Modify ?the?mnrnd function:Replace line 49 in toolbox/stats/mnrnd.m?with the following:pOK = all(0 <= p & p <= 1, 2) & (abs(edges(:,end)-1) <= size(p,2)*eps); 2. Modify your code as in the following example:?% This returns NaNs:d = 14; p = ?ones(1,d)/d;?mnrnd(100,p)?% This does not return NaNs:d = 14;p = ?ones(1,d)/d;p(end) = 1-sum(p(1:end-1)); mnrnd(100,p)?

Summary: pdist segfaults with one-dimensional data for Mahalanobis or scaled Euclidean distance
Description: The pdist  function segfaults when computing either the Mahalanobis or scaled Euclidean distance for one-dimensional data.
Workaround: In one dimension, the Mahalanobis and scaled Euclidean distances are identical, and you can compute them from the Euclidean distance using the square root of the variance to do the scaling. For example,x = randn(5,1);d = pdist(x,'Euc') ./ sqrt(var(x));

Summary: Excessive trimming for polygons with vertices on quadrangle boundary
Description: Two functions, geoshow  and maptrimp , can erroneously trim away polygons that should remain at least partly visible. This happens when one or more polygon vertices fall on any of the meridians or parallels bounding a geographic quadrangle (either defined by the map frame or by the user). When geoshow  plots on a map axes having a cylindrical or conic projection and attempts to trim polygons to the limits of the map axes, polygons that should be visible get trimmed away. The polygon input can be lat-lon arrays, a geostruct with Geometry  equal to 'polygon' , or a polygon shapefile.The problem also occurs when calling maptrimp , which takes vertices of a polygon and the latitude and longitude limits of a geographic quadrangle as input arguments. maptrimp  can eliminate the polygon or reduce it to a point if any of its vertices fall on the quadrangle.A second problem arises, which the first one has been masking: a polygon that outlines a quadrangle and includes a pole can be handled incorrectly, becoming trimmed to a single point at the pole with an arbitrary longitude of zero, rather than a pair of points with the longitudes of the points that precede and follow them. Here is an example of a polygon that defines a geographic quadrangle and includes a pole:lat = [60 90 90 60 60];lon = [35 35 55 55 35];
Workaround: To correct this problem, replace the function files trimPolygonToQuadrangle.m and trimPolygonToVerticalLine.m in your installation with the versions in the attachment, as follows:    Exit MATLAB.    Make a backup copy of the following files.    MATLABROOT\matlab\toolbox\map\map\mapproj\private\trimPolygonToQuadrangle.m    MATLABROOT\matlab\toolbox\map\map\mapproj\private\trimPolygonToVerticalLine.m    MATLABROOT represents the name of your MATLAB installation directory, as returned by the matlabroot function.    For example, rename the existing files, adding "old" to the file extension: trimPolygonToQuadrangle.m.old and trimPolygonToVerticalLine.m.old        Copy the versions of trimPolygonToQuadrangle.m and trimPolygonToVerticalLine.m from the attachment into the directory specified in the previous step.    Restart MATLAB.    Run the rehash toolbox command at the MATLAB prompt.

Summary: Why am I unable to create a videoinput object using Image Acquisition Toolbox's "coreco" adaptor?
Description: I am unable to create a videoinput  object using Image Acquisition Toolbox's "coreco" adaptor. Whenever I try to access the "coreco" adaptor, I receive an error message. For example: vid = videoinput('coreco', 1);??? Error using ==> videoinput.videoinputThere are no devices installed for the specified ADAPTORNAME. See IMAQHWINFO. If I try to determine the available hardware using imaqhwinfo , as indicated by the error message, I receive the following warning: imaqhwinfo('coreco')Warning: Message from the Sapera low-level driver:Error in "CorAcqGetHandle"  - Resource in use () ??? Could not create the low-level Sapera object. Perhaps the specified configuration file is invalid.
Workaround: This problem is caused by an interaction between the toolbox's winvideo and coreco adaptors. Hardware supported by DALSA Coreco's SaperaLT driver can be used with either the winvideo or coreco adaptors. However, only one adaptor can access the hardware at a time.Since the coreco adaptor offers more features, the hardware is normally ignored by the winvideo adaptor in order to allow the coreco adaptor access to the hardware. However, a recent change to the SaperaLT libraries has broken the algorithm used to determine which adaptor the toolbox should use, causing the winvideo adaptor to access the hardware.In order to access the hardware with the coreco adaptor, it is necessary to disable the winvideo adaptor. To do this, follow these steps:Exit MATLAB.Navigate to the directory /toolbox/imaq/imaqadaptors/win32.Rename the file mwwinvideoimaq.dll to mwwinvideoimaq.bak.Restart MATLAB.The coreco adaptor should now find the hardware properly.

Summary: Subsystems: top-level reusable subsystems might not be reusable
Description: A top-level reusable subsystem might not be reusable if you arrive at a reusable state in the following sequence:    The top-level subsystem is not reusable because there is a global signal within the subsystem.     You generate code during the current MATLAB session.     You remove the top-level subsystem global signals after generating code.     At least one of the output ports of the top-level reusable subsystem is a global signal.
Workaround: Save the model that contains the reusable subsystem.    Close the model.    Open the model.When you reopen the model, the top-level subsystem is reusable.

Description: The demdataui  function, which is a GUI?that provides an overview of digital elevation grid data available on the MATLAB path and lets users select regions from it,?has several? bugs; the most recent was introduced in R2007a: demdataui  has multiple calls to dms2deg  and deg2dms , which result warnings in R2007a. demdataui  passes the wrong path to the dted  function, so that it fails to import data. There is an extra 'dted/'  on the end of the path. demdataui  calls the older etopo5  function. It should call etopo  instead. demdata->UISAVE  warns while creating a questdlg  because of case issues (Mat-File vs. Mat-file) The satbath  data file name is outdated ('topo_6.2.img'  instead of 'topo_8.2.img' )
Workaround: To work around this issue, replace the demdataui.m function file in your installation with the version in the attachment, as follows:Exit MATLAB.Make a backup copy of the following files. MATLABROOT\matlab\toolbox\map\mapformats\demdataui.mMATLABROOT represents the name of your MATLAB installation directory, as returned by the matlabroot function.For example, rename the existing file, adding "old" to the file extension: demdataui.m.oldCopy the version of demdataui.m from the attachment into the directory specified in the previous step.Restart MATLAB.Run the rehash toolbox command at the MATLAB prompt. ?Special note: Customers using Mapping Toolbox versions older than R2007a (Version 2.5) will find demdataui.m to be in the MATLABROOT\toolbox\map\mapdisp directory instead, and will also need to copy the version of dms2degrees.m (a function introduced in R2007a) into the directory MATLABROOT\toolbox\map\mapdisp\private.

Summary: uipoen error when you double-click a file in a folder with ' in the folder name
Description: When you use the Windows Explorer to start MATLAB and double-click a file of? an associated file type (such as?.m or .mdl) from a folder that contains a single quote in its name (such as C:\MATLAB\Student's Files), MATLAB will launch but fail to load the file correctly. You might see one of the following errors: ??? uiopen('H:\MATLAB\Student's Files\startup.m',1)????????????????????????????? |Error: Unexpected MATLAB expression.This occurs because of problem in MATLAB where the single quote prematurely terminates the path string identifying the file MATLAB is to act on.
Workaround: To resolve this issue, rename folder so it does not contain a single quote character:C:\MATLAB\Student's Filesshould be renamed toC:\MATLAB\Student Files

Summary: Undo action can disable GUIDE?which then generates errors for most editing activities
Description: While working in GUIDE, if you?perform an edit, such?as resizing the layout area or altering the background color of the GUI, and then try to undo that change by pressing the Undo toolbar button, an active version of the GUI may unexpectedly be displayed.?Do not close this displayed GUI. You can minimize it, but if you close this GUI, GUIDE can enter a state that generates errors for almost anything that you do afterward. Once the active version of the GUI appears and you close it, you will not be able to save your changes to the GUI and you will have to close GUIDE. If you saved the GUI before this happened, you should be able to close GUIDE, open it again, and open the saved GUI into GUIDE for editing.
Workaround: Download the attached guidefunc.m file and replace the file of the same name with it. Keep a backup copy of the old file by renamimg or moving it before you replace it. guidefunc.m is located inMATLABROOT\toolbox\matlab\guide\where MATLABROOT is the directory where MATLAB is installed. Type matlabroot at the command line to find out this path?information if you are not sure about it.

Summary: Generated code for a Divide block with Simplest rounding might lack exception protection
Description: Suppose your ERT-based Simulink model contains a Divide block that Operates on signals of integer or fixed-point data types, and Specifies Simplest  as the value of its Round integer calculations toward  parameter.  If you execute the build process, Real-Time Workshop generates code that lacks exception protection for division, even if you requested such protection by deselecting the Remove code that protects against division arithmetic exceptions  option on the Optimization  pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box. Consequently, the generated code is unable to guard against division that triggers an exception, such as Division by zero Division of a signed intmin  by -1, where intmin  is the smallest value of an integer data type  If the generated code encounters such an exception during run-time, its behavior is determined externally, e.g., by the application, hardware, or operating system. As a result, the application might compute incorrect results or a hardware exception might abort the process and terminate the application.
Workaround: You can work around this issue by forcing Real-Time Workshop to generate division exception protection code. For each of the affected Divide blocks in your model, specify the value of its Round integer calculations toward parameter as either Floor or Zero, according to the actual method that your hardware implements. Note that this value should already be specified for the Signed integer division rounds to parameter on the Hardware Implementation pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box.

Summary: Documentation for the output data type of c280x and c281x Transmit blocks is incorrect
Description: The documentation for the c281x SPI Transmit block doc does not match the block mask description. In R2006b, SPI block output data type is limited to uint16 only. The doc for the block incorrectly says:"The sampling rate and data type are inherited from the input port. If no data type is specified, the default data type is uint16."?  The?doc should read:"The c281x SPI Transmit block?supports the uint16 data type only." Which is the correct behavior of the block.??? This problem occurs in the documentation for the c280x and c281x Transmit blocks.

Description: This was due to a difference in the way the states?are reset for the TCM decoder blocks when compared to how they are reset? for the Viterbi decoder.

Summary: Using add_line to copy lines from one Simulink model to another may cause the lines to be drawn at angles other than 90 degrees
Description: For example, consider the following code, which uses add_line to copy a vector of lines, sourceLines, from an existing model into a new model:     for k=1:numel(sourceLines)        add_line(newModelName, get_param(sourceLines(k), 'Points'));    end Suppose that some of the copied lines begin at branch points in the model. Simulink draws these lines at angles other than 90 degrees.

Summary: Yahoo constructor and Yahoo/fetch requests for historical data fail
Description: The yahoo constructor fails when using the default URL for the Yahoo! Finance site.Yahoo/fetch requests for historical data fail when a single date or a date range is specified, and for the field Adj. Close*. ?
Workaround: The URL must be specified as "http://finance.quote.com".Work around the fetch issue by using the date format yyyy-mm-dd in yahoo/fetch datenum calls for requesting historical data. The attached files yahoo/fetch.m and yahoo/yhfields.mat include the fix for this issue.?

Summary: flattopwin produces inadequate sidelobe attenuation
Description: A flat top window of sufficient length (e.g., 256 samples) should have  -93dB sidelobes. The flattopwin  function returns a window with sidelobes of only approximately -70dB.
