Summary: Problems with the Saturate IQN (IQsat) block of the TI IQmath library
Description: In R2006b, the IQsat block only accepts integer values for the upper and lower saturation. In R2007a, the block will not accept values covering the full possible range of the considered Q format. You may see error messages saying that the value you entered for the upper limit is outside of the range covered by the Q format of the input signal. Also the block will also give simulation results that are not bit-true compatible with the generated code.
Workaround: Download the attached patch installer patch_installer.m and the ZIP archive patch_363930_R200xx.zip corresponding to the MATLAB version you want to patch. This patch is available for MATLAB releases R2006b and R2007a.    Start MATLAB and type ver at the prompt to verify which version of MATLAB you are using.    Enter the following command at the MATLAB prompt to install the patch:        For R2006b:        patch_installer('patch_363930_R2006b.zip', 'install')         For R2007a:        patch_installer('patch_363930_R2007a.zip', 'install')

Summary: Permission problems can prevent use of MDCEUSER on UNIX
Description: When setting MDCEUSER  to be a nonpriviledged user account on UNIX, the mdce service can fail to start due to permission problems:  # mdce start -mdcedef my_mdce_def.sh  Unable to write a test file (.mdcetestfile) in PIDBASE directory /var/run This happens even though the MDCEUSER  account has write access to the LOGBASE  and CHECKPOINTBASE  directories.
Workaround: In the mdce_def file, set PIDBASE and LOCKBASE to be directories that MDCEUSER has write access to.

Summary: Embedded MATLAB code generation produces segmentation violation for certain matrix assignments
Description: When you generate code for Embedded MATLAB Function blocks or for MATLAB code using emlmex , errors may occur if your code contains assignments with the following characteristics: Concatenates matrices on the right (using a method other than the cat  function) Use a matrix as an index on the left  For example, the following Embedded MATLAB function generates an error during code generation because of the highlighted assignment statement: function result = hcat_to_mref(in1) A = [1:1:9; 10:1:18; 19:1:27]; A(A) = [in1, in1, ones(3, 1)];  result = A; end
Workaround: Pass the matrix concatenation expression as an argument to the reshape function, as in this example:function result = hcat_to_mref(in1) A = [1:1:9; 10:1:18; 19:1:27]; A(A) = reshape([in1, in1, ones(3, 1)], 3, 9); result = A; end

Summary: Mathematical filter blocks give wrong answers for high-order filters
Description: When you model certain types of filters, such as high-Q filters (bandpass or bandstop), the mathematical filter blocks do not simulate the frequency response of the filters correctly.? This problem is caused by numerical inaccuracies that are most apparent for high-order filters.

Summary: Incorrect video compressor codecs listed in To Multimedia File Block
Description: In R2007a, the To Multimedia File Block populates the list of video codecs incorrectly, which results in audio codecs being listed in the video codecs popup.The following warning message is one indication of this erroneous behavior: Warning: The 'Microsoft ADPCM' enumerated value is invalid. If you attempt to run the model that contains the To Multimedia File block after you see this warning, MATLAB produces the following error: Unable to create video compressor filter.
Workaround: To resolve this issue, download the ZIP file attached to this bug report. Extract its contents into the following directory:$MATLABROOT/toolbox/dspblks/dspblks$MATLABROOT is the directory in which you installed MATLAB.

Summary: H5D.read returns inconsistent data dimensions
Description: The H5D.read  function works inconsistently depending on the dimensions of the dataspace. For example, in one case the size of the data is reversed (3x1x50x50 versus 50x50x1x3), while in another the output and input sizes are the same (50x50x2x3).
Workaround: H5D.read returns incorrect?data dimensions?when the dataspace contains a singleton dimension.To workaround this issue, consider using a dataspace without singleton dimensions, or use MATLAB's reshape and permute functions to correct the data's orientation.

Summary: Arabic or Hebrew languages: when typing or pasting characters, MATLAB can become unresponsive
Description: In the Command Window, when you?type or paste characters from a right-to-left language, such as Arabic or Hebrew,?MATLAB?can become unresponsive. You need to restart MATLAB.

Summary: fread incorrectly skips when the size parameter is smaller than the repetition factor.
Description: When fread  is called with 4 input arguments (fid , count , precision , and skip ), and the value of the precision  argument specifies a repetition factor that is larger than the count  argument, it incorrectly skips after reading count  elements of data. In this example, the count is 5 and repetition factor is 10:    a = fread(fid, 5, '10*double', 4) fread  should read 5 doubles and return, but instead reads 5 doubles and then skips 4 bytes, leaving the file pointer at the wrong location.
Workaround: Avoid the use of a count value that is smaller than the repetition factor.

Summary: Code generation report may omit generated code listings if system?target file (STF)?was derived from another STF and renamed
Description: Code generation reports generated for a model by Simulink Report Generator or the rtwreport  command may omit generated code listings if both of the following conditions are true:     The model's system target file (STF) is a custom STF that was derived from another STF, such as ert.tlc  provided by The MathWorks     The STF was saved with a different name, such as myert.tlc
Workaround: To work around this bug, open your custom STF and modify the BuildDirSuffix line to reflect the renaming of your STF file. For example, if you based your STF on ert.tlc and renamed?the STF?to myert.tlc, change the line??rtwgensettings.BuildDirSuffix = '_ert_rtw';to??rtwgensettings.BuildDirSuffix = '_myert_rtw';The generated code will be placed in a directory that is correctly named based on the model name and the BuildDirSuffix (for example, mymodel_myert_rtw) and will be included in the generated report.

Summary: dfilt and Digital Filter block unnecessarily require a Filter Design Toolbox license
Description: Signal Processing Toolbox?dfilt  constructors and the Digital Filter block in the Signal Processing Blockset product unnecessarily check out a Filter Design Toolbox license.

Summary: S-Function block appearance does not match original Subsystem block
Description: When you create an S-function using the rtwbuild  command, the appearance of the S-Function block does not match the original Subsystem block. The S-Function block behaves as expected, the change in appearance does not affect the S-Function block behavior.

Summary: cellfun does not error when output is a mismatched struct array.
Description: Executing the command  c = cellfun(@(s)(struct(s{:})), {{'a',3}, {'b',45,'c', 6}}); should return an error because the result of the function call on the first cell is a struct with the field 'a', while the result of the function call on the second cell is a struct with the fields 'b' and 'c'. Because MATLAB cannot concatenate such structures, cellfun  should error. Instead, the command returns an erroneous result, which is a struct array having a field 'a', where c(1).a = 3 and c(2).a = 45.
Workaround: If you use cellfun with the 'UniformOutput' flag set to false, MATLAB returns a cell array in which each cell contains the result of each of the function calls.

Summary: Startup warning: duplicate directory name on Windows
Description: When you start MATLAB on Windows platforms, a warning like this might appear: Warning: Duplicate directory name: D:\Documents\MATLAB. This warning is harmless. It appears if you have made changes to and saved the MATLAB search path in the previous session. This is due to a bug in the save path functionality.
Workaround: This workaround corrects the bug in the save path functionality so that the warning no longer appears upon startup.Exit MATLAB.Download the attached savepath.m file to replace the existing savepath.m at matlabroot\toolbox\matlab\general\savepath.mwhere matlabroot is the directory in which MATLAB is installed, which you can determine by running the matlabroot function in MATLAB.Start MATLAB from the Windows desktop shortcut or from Windows Start > Programs.Run savepath in the Command Window.The warning should no longer appear.

Summary: Collecting model coverage data after generating code for a subsystem might cause Simulink to crash
Description: If your model contains any of the following blocks: Lookup Table MinMax Multiport Switch  and you perform the following sequence of steps: You enable (and optionally use) the Model Coverage tool in Simulink Verification and Validation to generate coverage data for your model. You use Real-Time Workshop to build an executable from a subsystem in your model, e.g., by right-clicking a Subsystem block and selecting Real-Time Workshop > Build Subsystem . You use the Model Coverage tool to collect coverage data for your model.  Simulink will crash, producing a segmentation violation that displays a stack trace similar to the following: Stack Trace:  [0] cv.mexw32:0x172a2d4d(0x17160344, 1, 0x17299fc4, 4)  [1] libmwsimulink.dll:struct slErrMsgQueue * __cdecl BlockInitializeCoverage(class slBlock *,class slSimBlock_tag *)(0x171601e8, 0x17299fc4, 0x13c83e00, 0) + 116 bytes  [2] libmwsimulink.dll:_BdSystemCoverageInitialize(0x1ed2abb0, 0x1f539320, 0, 0x1f1f7aa0) + 33 bytes  [3] libmwsimulink.dll:struct slErrMsgQueue * __cdecl ModelStart(class slModel *)(0x1f1f7aa0, 0x00ceaa10, 0, 0x1f1f7aa0) + 387 bytes  [4] libmwsimulink.dll:struct slErrMsgQueue * __cdecl sleInitModelConditions(class slModel *,struct mxArray_tag const *,bool,bool)(0x011f7aa0, 0, 1, 1) + 437 bytes  [5] libmwsimulink.dll:_sleInitModelForMenuSim(0x13c83e00, 0, 0x13c83e00, 0x00ceaa80) + 133 bytes  [6] libmwsimulink.dll:struct slErrMsgQueue * __cdecl sleModelExecutionInterface(struct slBlockDiagram_tag *,enum slEngineCmdOption,enum ECStatus *)(0x13c83e00, 0, 0x00ceaa7c, 0x13c83e00) + 99 bytes  [7] libmwsimulink.dll:enum ECStatus __cdecl sluiEngBdMenuCmdInterface(struct slBlockDiagram_tag *,enum slEngineCmdOption)(0x13c83e00, 0, 0x15986ba0, 0x13c83e00) + 39 bytes  [8] libmwsimulink.dll:struct slErrMsgQueue * __cdecl StartMenuSimIfNotStarted(struct slBlockDiagram_tag *)(0, 0x13c83e00, 1, 18) + 94 bytes  [9] libmwsimulink.dll:void __cdecl Menu_Simulation_Start(struct WinRec_tag *,void *)(0x15986ba0, 0, 0x17631324, 3015974) + 119 bytes  ...
Workaround: You can use either of the following workarounds to prevent Simulink from crashing when alternating between the collection of model coverage data and the generation of code for subsystems:Before building an executable from any of your model's subsystems, ensure that you have disabled the Enable Coverage Reporting option on the Coverage Settings dialog.After building an executable from any of your model's subsystems, close and then reload the model before collecting its coverage data.

Summary: Stand-Alone fuzzy code (fismain.c) causes error when using a large number of inputs or outputs
Description: If the number of inputs or outputs of a Fuzzy Inference System are larger than the length of strings used to name the inputs or the outputs, you might get a segmentation violation when running the Stand-Alone Fuzzy Inference Engine (through the fismain.c code).
Workaround: Download the attachment and unzip its content to a temporary directory.  Replace MATLABROOT\toolbox\fuzzy\fuzzy\fis.c. Compile fismain.c as per doc, e.g., using the command cc -g -o fismain fismain.c -lm

Summary: Building a subsystem for PIL cosimulation generates an error during compilation in Code Composer Studio
Description: Building a subsystem for PIL cosimulation generates the following error during compilation in CCS: "pil_interface_lib.h", line 13: fatal error: could not open source file "pil_interface_data.h"   This happens when the MATLAB installation directory name or the current working directory name contains spaces.
Workaround: Download the attached patch installer patch_installer.m and the ZIP archive patch_362908.zip. This patch applies only to MATLAB release R2007a.    Start MATLAB and enter ver at the command prompt to verify that you are using MATLAB version 7.4 (R2007a).    Enter the following command at the MATLAB prompt to install the patch:    patch_installer('patch_362908.zip', 'install')     Installing the patch enables Link for CCS to recognize directories that have spaces in their names.

Summary: Real-Time Workshop build process fails with 'eval' error
Description: Documentation provides examples of setting the template makefile (TMF) variables BUILD_SUCCESS, BUILD_ERROR, DOWNLOAD_SUCCESS, and DOWNLOAD_ERROR. If BUILD_ERROR is used in the manner shown in the example:   BUILD_ERROR = ['Error while building ', modelName] the following error will be displayed and the Real-Time Workshop build process will terminate.   Error using ==> eval Undefined function or variable 'modelName'.
Workaround: To work around this bug, change the TMF variable assignment from this:  BUILD_ERROR   = ['Error while building ', modelName] to the following:  BUILD_ERROR   = Error while building |>MODEL_NAME<| Also, as necessary, make the corresponding change for the make variables BUILD_SUCCESS, DOWNLOAD_SUCCESS, and DOWNLOAD_ERROR.

Summary: Memory Leak in MATLAB Builder JA MWArray native code
Description: Marshalling a Java array type into MATLAB using MWArray results in leaked memory. Scalar types and marshalling from MATLAB back to Java are not affected. For example, when running: x = new MWNumericArray(new double[]{1.0, 2.0});x.dispose(); The user may observe an error like the following: Exception in thread "main" java.lang.OutOfMemoryError: requested 8000000 bytes for jdouble in C:/BUILD_AREA/jdk1.5.0_02/hotspot\src\share\vm\prims\jni.cpp. Out of swap space?
Workaround: Download the attached .zip file (R2006b_binary_patches.zip or R2007a_binary_patches.zip)Copy the shared library file *nativemcl75* or *nativemcl76* for your platform into: matlabroot/bin/arch in your MATLAB installationmcrinstallroot/runtime/arch in all MCR installationswhere matlabroot indicates the MATLAB install directory, arch indicates the platform (glnx86, glnxa64, sol2, or win32), and mcrinstallroot indicates the MCR install directoryIt may be necessary to restart MATLAB.NOTE: This workaround only applies to R2006b and R2007a.In addition, on Windows: If you have applied the patch for R2007a, and your application fails due to runtime error R6034, the machine upon which you are executing the application does not have the correct version of the Microsoft runtime environment. nativemcl76.dll requires the Microsoft Visual C++ 2005 SP1 Redistributable Package.

Summary: timeseries constructor gives inappropriate error 'Duplicate times are found.'
Description: The timeseries  constructor can produce the following error, even if there are no duplicate times in the specified time vector: ??? Error using ==> tsAnalyzeAbsTime at 49 Duplicate times are found.  Error in ==> timeseries.setabstime at 26 [thisTime,h.TimeInfo.Startdate] = ...  Error in ==> timeseries.init at 239 this = setabstime(this,time(I));  Error in ==> timeseries.timeseries>timeseries.timeseries at 257 this = init(this,varargin{:});  The error is generated if the timeseries  constructor specifies an absolute time vector where the difference between the date vector representations of two successive times sums to zero. For example, the following produces the error even though there are no duplicate times: timeseries([0.45;0.55],{'04/16/98';'05/14/99'})  If the two dates are converted to date vectors with datevec  they become: [1998 4 16 0 0 0] [1999 5 14 0 0 0] The difference between the two date vectors is: [-1 -1 2 0 0 0] This vetor sums to zero, procucing the error.
Workaround: On line 48 (in MATLAB versions 7.3 and greater) or line 47 (in MATLAB versions before 7.3) of the file tsAnalyzeAbsTime.m on the MATLAB path, replace:if min(sum(diff(dateVec),2))==0with:if min(sum(abs(diff(dateVec)),2))==0

Summary: wavread cannot import 32-bit PCM data correctly from a Microsoft WAVE file.
Description: When I plot data that I've imported using wavread  the data looks like white noise. I have verified that I've imported the data with the correct number of samples and the correct sample rate. I've also verified that I can play the WAV file with Windows Media Player and the Quicktime player, and can load it into different audio processing applications without problems.
Workaround: There is a bug in MATLAB 7.3.0.267 (R2006b) in the way that wavread handles wave files that contain 32-bit PCM data. To work around this issue, download the updated version of the file wavread.m that is attached. Find the $MATLABROOT directory where $MATLABROOT is the MATLAB root directory on your machine, as returned by typing:    matlabrootat the MATLAB Command Prompt.Quit MATLAB.Rename the following MATLAB file:    $MATLABROOT\toolbox\matlab\audiovideo\wavread.mRename the file to wavread.m.oldDownload the attached file. Place the new file in the same directory mentioned in step 3.Restart MATLABAfter restarting MATLAB, issue the following command at the MATLAB Command Prompt: rehash toolboxcache

Summary: HDL code is generated for the entire model when model and subsystem (DUT) names are identical
Description: When the names of the model and the subsystem (DUT) for which HDL code is to be generated are identical, Simulink HDL Coder incorrectly generates HDL code for the entire model, instead of the intended subsystem. This occurs even if subsystem name is passed in explicitly to makehdl  or makehdltb . For example, if a model named lut  contains a subsystem named lut , the following command generates code for the top-level model: makehdl('lut/lut')  This problem affects command line code generation commands only. It does not occur when generating code using the Simulink HDL Coder GUI.
Workaround: To work around this problem, use different names for the top-level model and the subsystem from which you intend to generate code.

Summary: Code generates runtime errors, wrong results, and global variables that are not initialized properly
Description: The default memory mapping provided by a Target Preferences block configured for LF2407a processors does not map and initialize the .bss  memory section properly. Improper mapping can cause memory values that are not initialized properly and incorrect results at execution. Link for CCS does not report an error for this incorrect memory mapping. The default memory mapping settings for the LF2407a processor maps the .bss  memory section to external RAM placed on Page 2 of the linker command file. For variables in the .bss  section to be initialized properly on TI C24x processors (and the LF2407a processor), the .bss  section must be linked to Page 1 of the linker command file.
Workaround: To fix this problem, follow these steps:Open your model.Double-click the Target Preferences block to open the block dialog box.Click the Sections tab.On the Default sections list, select .bss.On the Placement list, select SARAM_D to place the .bss memory section in SARAM_D.Click OK to close the dialog box.Save and rebuild the model.This change ensures that the .bss section goes to Page 1 of the linker command file as required.

Summary: Desktop becomes unresponsive
Description: If you click, in rapid succession, the title bars of tools docked?in the desktop,?the desktop can become unresponsive. Once?the desktop is in this state,?subsequent?clicking does not appear to have any effect. In particular, clicking a tool or its title bar doesn't appear to give focus to the tool.?In addition, characters you type in the Command Window are not displayed.
Workaround: There are two workarounds: one that revives the current MATLAB session, and one that prevents future occurrences of the problem.Revive Current Session    Undock the Command Window. You can do this by clicking?the?undock button in the Command Window title bar. (Even though the?desktop appears unresponsive, it will respond to this action).    Type the following in the undocked Command Window, and press Enter:    com.mathworks.mwswing.GlobalCursor.clear(com.mathworks.mlservices.MatlabDesktopServices.getDesktop.getMainFrame)        Dock the Command Window into the MATLAB desktop.Desktop tools should now be responsivePrevent Future Occurrences    Determine matlabroot on your system. matlabroot is the directory in which MATLAB is installed. Determine its location by?running matlabroot?in the Command Window.    Quit MATLAB.    Download the attached file, patch.zip. Unzip the contents into the matlabroot/java/patch directory.    Restart MATLAB. You should no longer experience the problem.

Summary: Keyboard focus is lost when using the up/down keys to navigate in the Model Explorer
Description: Keyboard navigation of the Model Explorer tree or list view may cause focus to pass to the embedded dialog. If the dialog has tabs, the tabs may steal keyboard focus.
Workaround: Turn off the dialog view when using keyboard navigation.

Summary: VHDL code generated from Lookup Table block with ufix(1) output gets syntax error
Description: VHDL code generated for a Lookup Table block having an output signal of data type ufix(1)  gets a syntax error when compiled.
Workaround: To work around the problem, assign data type ufix(2) to the output of the Lookup Table. Then convert the Lookup Table output signal to ufix(1) using a Data Type Conversion block. Set the Input and output to have equal parameter on the Data Type Conversion block to Stored Integer (SI).A second workaround is to generate Verilog code, which is not affected by this bug.A third workaround is to use a constant vector of Boolean data type followed by a Selector block.

Summary: IVI-COM drivers might cause format errors while loading
Description: IVI-COM instrument drivers using special characters in their object names can create problems for Instrument Control Toolbox drivers. The characters that can cause problems include { , } , ( , ) , [ , and ] . An example problem case follows.  >> makemid('AgilentInfiniium.AgilentInfiniium');>> icdevice('AgilentInfiniium','TCPIP0::144.212.211.54::inst0::INSTR');207     [obj.jobject, errflag] = localCreateWithMatlabInstrumentDriver(mdd, hwobj,optionstring);The definition for Interface_IVI_AgilentInfiniium_(Agilent_Technologies)_1_0_Type_Library_(Released)_IAgilentInfiniiumBeanAdapter0 in the JAR filehas a format error.Error in ==> icdevice.icdevice at 217    rethrow(lasterror);
Workaround: For 32-bit Microsoft Windows operating systems, install the attached files as comcli.dll and comcli.dll.pbd in matlabroot\bin\win32.

Summary: Logical indexing using short circuited operators may crash MATLAB
Description: Using the short circuiting operators && and || inside an indexing expression may cause MATLAB to crash. Two examples of this type of statement are   b = 2;   a(b >= 1 && b <= 3) = 1; or   b.a = 2;   a.a(b.a >= 1 && b.a <= 3) = 1;
Workaround: Change expressions that index in the manner shown in Example A below to the form shown in Example B:Example A -   b = 2;   a(b >= 1 && b <= 3) = 1;Example B -  b = 2;  tmp = (b >= 1 && b <= 3);  a(tmp) = 1;

Summary: Incorrect joint reaction force output from Screw joint
Description: Joint Sensors connected to Screw Joint blocks can produce incorrect joint reaction (force or torque) outputs if other Sensor outputs (position, velocity, acceleration, computed force/torque) are enabled. In addition if the screw joint happens to be the cut joint, then incorrect results are produced if both reaction torque and force are measured together from the same joint.
Workaround: Currently you cannot accurately measure both reaction Torque and Force from the joint if it is the cut joint. You can only measure one of them. Mark one of the other joints (which is not a screw joint) as the cut joint (see Advanced tab of the joint dialog) in order to measure both torque and force. To measure kinematic quantities along with reaction force or torque, use a workaround similar to the attached "workaround.mdl" model (again make sure the screw joint is not the cut joint).

Summary: State vector manager returns incorrect number of states for Spherical-Spherical Massless Connector
Description: The state vector manager command mech_stateVectorMgr  returns 16 states for each Spherical-Spherical Massless Connector in your model. However, this Joint type in fact has 12 states. These 12 states are equivalent to three angles for each spherical primitive, times two for the Spherical Joint at either end of the massless connector, times two again to count velocities as well. Of these states, only 10 are independent, because of the massless connector constraint. The other two are dependent.
Workaround: There is no workaround available.

Summary: System stack size (bytes) option in model configuration parameters resets to 8192 bytes when using TI C6000 processors
Description: The System stack size (bytes)  option in the Link for CCS  pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box resets to 8192 bytes each time you open a model that uses a TI C6000 processor. Your changes to this value are not saved when you close the model.
Workaround: Download attachment_362403_20070302.zip and extract the following patch files:patch_installer.mpatch_362403.zip This patch applies only to MATLAB Version 7.4 (R2007a).To install the patch, follow these steps.Start MATLAB.Cd to the folder where you extracted the above patch files.Enter the following command at the prompt to install the patch: patch_installer('patch_362403.zip', 'install')?Follow the instructions on the MATLAB command line to complete the patch installation.

Summary: The Reed-Solomon Decoder blocks do not accept a user-specified generator polynomial with the initial power of alpha as 0.
Description: The Reed Solomon Decoder blocks allow the user to specify a nondefault generator polynomial.? These polynomials take the form [x -a ^b ][x -a ^(b +1)] . . . [x -a ^(b +2t -1)], where a  is the primitive element of the Galois field, b  is a nonnegative integer, and t  is the error-correcting capability of the code.? In R2007a, a parameter check on b  incorrectly constrained it to be strictly positive, instead of simply nonnegative.
Workaround: Copy two of the attached files, scomberlekamp.mex*** and scomberlekamp.mex***.csf, into matlabroot/toolbox/commblks/commmex, overwriting the files of the same name that currently exist in that directory.? Use the following platform information to determine the value of '***', and thus which two files to copy.    64-bit Linux -> a64    32-bit Linux -> glx    Mac -> mac    Mac on Intel -> maci    64-bit Solaris -> s64    32-bit Windows -> w32    64-bit Windows -> w64Then, at the MATLAB command prompt, type>> bdclose allto close all open Simulink models.? Then type>> clear mexto clear all S-functions from memory.? Then type>> rehash toolboxto reload all toolbox directories.

Summary: Calling a function with multiple output arguments may be slower than calling the same function with a single output argument.
Description: In some instances, calling a function with multiple outputs may be significantly slower than calling the same function with a single output. This behavior only occurs if the input and output variable have the same name. For example: [x, y] = myfunction(x);  may be slower than: x = myfunction(x);  The interpreter has an optimization that allows it to reuse the memory from a variable when it appears as both a function argument and the left hand side of an assignment. The performance difference occurs because this optimization is not being correctly applied when there are additional output arguments.

Summary: Error when loading a Pre-R2007a FIG-File in R2007a
Description: When loading a figure that was created in a prior release of MATLAB and which was modified or annotated in plot edit mode or by using the plotting tools, the following error can occur:No appropriate method or public field SelectedObjects for class > > scribe.scribeaxes.Error using ==> open at 162Unable to find function scribe_paste_cb within D:\Applications\MATLAB\R2007aPR\toolbox\matlab\graph2d\plotedit.p
Workaround: To work around this issue, follow the following steps: Exit MATLABRename the file $MATLAB/toolbox/matlab/graph2d/plotedit.p to $MATLAB/toolbox/matlab/graph2d/plotedit.p.old, where $MATLAB is the root directory of your MATLAB installation as returned by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. Rename the file $MATLAB/toolbox/matlab/graph2d/plotedit.m to $MATLAB/toolbox/matlab/graph2d/plotedit.m.old.Download the attached file, plotedit.m, to the above directory.Start MATLAB and type rehash toolboxcache at the MATLAB command prompt.

Summary: Insert Text block dialog box has a missing tab and all parameters are tunable
Description: The Insert Text block dialog box has the following two issues:1. The Font tab is missing, but all the font parameters are located on the Main tab of the block dialog box. 2. During simulation, all parameters appear tunable. However, if you try to change the value of anything other than the Location, Color value, or Opacity parameters, the block produces an error.
Workaround: You can correct this problem by replacing the viptextngfix.mdl file in the $matlabroot/toolbox/vipblks/vipblks directory with the file attached to this bug report.

Summary: Problem with UI barrier when barrier never hits the tree.
Description: Problem when pricing an Up and In (UI) barrier when barrier never hits the tree. If I continuously increase the barrier, the price of the call option (either European or American) will converge against zero. Once the barrier is high enough to practically never been reached, the option is worthless.
Workaround: Handle edge case of barriers that never touch the tree. Once the barrier is high enough to practically never been reached, the option is worthless. We modified the function to implement this edge case.

Summary: Why does the Image Acquisition Toolbox crash when switching formats using the DCAM adaptor?
Description: When using the dcam  adaptor of the Image Acquistion Toolbox? software, MATLAB crashes if I use multiple objects with different resolutions. For example, if I create an object with the command: vid = videoinput('dcam', 1, 'F7_Y16_1024x1024');  object creation and acquisition work fine. If I try to create an object that uses a smaller resolution such as: vid2 = videoinput('dcam', 1, 'F7_Y16_512x512');  then the vid2  object is created normally and acquisition works fine. However, if I try to use the original vid  object, MATLAB crashes.

Summary: Using MCC with the -d option to compile a C-shared library with LCC results in .LIB and .EXP files?created in wrong directory
Description: Using mcc  with the -d  option to compile a C-shared library with LCC results in creating .lib  and .exp  files in the current working directory instead of in the directory specified after the -d  option.Also, when using?deploytool  to compile a C-shared library with the LCC compiler, the following error may be encountered when the LIB file generated is?copied from the "src" directory to the "distrib" directory:Could not copy file to the output directory. After this error occurs,?project packaging will also fail with a non-existent file.
Workaround: There are no workaround for this issue other than to manually copy the required files to the correct output directory.?If you are using deploytool, copy the .lib and the .exp file from the intermediate directory (default is? project_name/src under the directory where your project resides) to the output directory (default is project_name/distrib) as specified in the project settings, and then repackage your project.?

Summary: External mode to xPC target does not correctly handle fixed-point data types
Description: When running Simulink external mode with an xPC target, fixed-point data values are not sent correctly, including parameter downloads and signal uploads.
Workaround: To work around this bug in R2006b or R2007a, perform the following steps:Note: In the following instructions, matlabroot represents your MATLAB installation root, as returned by the MATLAB command matlabroot.In your MATLAB installation tree, rename the following file, for example, by adding .old to the end of the file name.matlabroot\toolbox\rtw\targets\xpc\xpc\ext_xpc.mDownload the attached file whose suffix, _R2006b or _R2007a, corresponds to your MATLAB release number.Remove the suffix from the downloaded file and place the file at the same location as the file you renamed.Restart MATLAB.

Summary: Startup directory on Windows is not My Documents\MATLAB or Documents\MATLAB as expected
Description: The MATLAB startup directory on Windows might be incorrect when you start MATLAB for the first time. The expected default startup directory is N:\ My Documents\MATLAB  (or N:\ Documents\MATLAB  on Vista), where N:\   is a mapped drive on your machine. Instead, your startup directory might be the location of the shortcut you used to start MATLAB. This can happen when the My Documents  folder (or Documents  on Vista) is on a network drive and is specified with a UNC path rather than with a mapped drive. A UNC path is one that begins with \\  rather than a mapped drive letter. To see what your system uses, right-click on a My Documents  icon (or Documents  icon on Vista) , such as on the Windows desktop, then click Properties . If the path in the Target folder location  begins with \\  (rather than a letter such as D:\ ), use the workaround described in this report.
Workaround: If the path in the Target folder location begins with \\:    Exit MATLAB.    Select Start > Documents > My Documents.     On Windows Vista, select Start > Documents. If a MATLAB folder does not exist here, create one.    Start MATLAB from the Windows desktop shortcut or from Start > Programs.    Ensure the current working directory is N:\My Documents\MATLAB (or N:\Documents\MATLAB on Vista).    Ensure N:\My Documents\MATLAB (or N:\Documents\MATLAB on Vista) is at the top of the search path by selecting File > Set Path. If it is, the problem is resolved.If after trying the above steps, the current working directory is not N:\My Documents\MATLAB (or N:\Documents\MATLAB on Vista), or if the path is not as expected, follow these steps:    Right-click the Windows desktop shortcut for MATLAB and select Properties from the context menu.    The Target field should end with -sd $documents\MATLAB. If it does not, correct it, then click OK.    In a DOS window, run    StartDir $documents\MATLAB    A UNC path should be displayed. You can ignore any messages similar to the following:    WNetUseConnection failed:31 WNetUseConnection failed:53    Restart MATLAB, and this should address the problem. The startup directory should now be N:\My Documents\MATLAB (or N:\Documents\MATLAB on Vista).    If the error messages are not the same as the ones listed above, verify that the entire UNC path exists. One way to do this is by typing the full path (\\ through MATLAB) in a Windows Explorer address field and the MATLAB folder should open. If the folder opens, contact Technical Support and report the error messages. If you cannot navigate the path to open the MATLAB folder, the problem might be with your network, such as privileges for the path; contact your system administrator for help.

Summary: Minor problems with functions rsphere and distance for certain inputs
Description: When function rsphere  is used with the 'euler'  option and the latitude-longitude coordinate inputs are non-scalar, the output is a scalar rather than a vector or an array. This problem, in turn, has a slight impact on the numerical output of the distance  function when all of the following conditions are true:You are computing a geodesic distance on an ellipsoid You are using only a single output variable (i.e, you are not requesting that distance  return both a distance and an azimuth) You are providing non-scalar coordinate input (i.e., computing the distances between multiple point-pairs in a single call) Two or more of your point pairs are separated by less than one degree  In this case, distance  returns non-scalar results, as it should, but the distances may be off by up to a few millimeters.
Workaround: To work around this issue, replace the rsphere.m function file in your current installation with the newer version from the attachment, as follows:Exit MATLAB.Make a backup copy of the following file. matlabroot/toolbox/map/map/rsphere.mmatlabroot represents the name of your MATLAB installation directory, as returned by the matlabroot function. For example, rename the existing file by adding "old" to the file extension: rsphere.m.OLD.Copy the version of rsphere.m from the attachment into the directory specified in the previous step.Restart MATLAB.Run the rehash toolbox command at the MATLAB prompt.

Summary: Build process on Windows fails to use 'prefdir' mexopts.bat for some types of builds
Description: On Windows, Real-Time Workshop builds that need to create MEX-files fail to use the mexopts.bat  file in the user preferences directory (returned by the prefdir  function). Instead the builds use the default installed mexopts.bat  file.
Workaround: To work around this bug in R2007a, perform the following steps:Note: In the following instructions, matlabroot represents your MATLAB installation root, as returned by the MATLAB command matlabroot.        In your MATLAB installation tree, rename the following files, for example by adding .old to the end of each file name:            matlabroot\toolbox\rtw\rtw\@RTW\genMakefileAndBuild.p        matlabroot\toolbox\rtw\rtw\@RTW\genMakefileAndBuild.m                Download the attached files and place them in the same directory as the files you renamed        Restart MATLAB.

Summary: Discrete Event Subsystem block warns when inside subsystem in user-created library
Description: If you create your own library block as a subsystem that contains a Discrete Event Subsystem block, then inserting your library block into a model causes the following warning: Warning: Error evaluating 'CopyFcn' callback of Discrete Event Subsystemblock (mask) 'Discrete Event Subsystem'. Error using ==> set_paramThe LinkStatus can be set only for a linked block.
Workaround: Instead of using the Discrete-Event Subsystem block in a custom library, use the Signal-Based Event to Function-Call Event block in the SimEvents Event Translation library to invoke the Function-Call Subsystem block in the Simulink Ports & Subsystems library. You must configure the Function-Call Subsystem block as follows:    Select the Function-Call Subsystem block and choose Edit > Subsystem Parameters from the model window's menu bar.    In the dialog box that opens, select Propagate execution context across subsystem boundary and click OK.The attached library file contains an example of this workaround.

Summary: S-Function Target cannot be used with LCC compiler
Description: You cannot build an S-Function Target using SimMechanics and the LCC compiler. A linker error will occur.
Workaround: Download the attached file, then copy it into this directory, overwriting the existing file.$matlabroot>/toolbox/physmod/mech/lib/win32/

Summary: Digital I/O blocks in Data Acquisition Toolbox block library fail to load dialog properties successfully
Description: The Digital Input and Output blocks in Data Acquisition Toolbox block library fail to load the dialog properties window successfully if there are no digital devices present on your system.

Summary: Using xPC Target with Simulink external mode might generate error during simulation
Description: Running Simulink external mode with a target PC might generate the following error message: undefined variable 'tstate'  or undefined symbol or variable sig_data
Workaround: As a workaround:  Quit MATLAB.Rename the following file:matlabroot\toolbox\rtw\targets\xpc\xpc\ext_xpc.mtomatlabroot\toolbox\rtw\targets\xpc\xpc\ext_xpc.m.old(where matlabroot represents your MATLAB installation root, as returned by the MATLAB command matlabroot) For the R2006b release, download the attached file ext_xpc_R2006b.m.For the R2007a release, download the attached file ext_xpc_R2007a.m. After downloading, remove the suffix (_R2006b or _R2007a) from the downloaded file and place the file at:matlabroot\toolbox\rtw\targets\xpc\xpc\ Restart MATLAB.

Summary: MCR installer fails on some Linux systems with "Bundled JRE not binary compatible with this machine" error
Description: The MCR installer can fail when run on certain Linux systems. You might see the error message:Bundled JRE not binary compatible with this machine. The MCR installer is a Java application and the MCR package includes a? Java Runtime Environment (JRE) on which the MCR installer can run. The JRE included with the MCR package does not work on certain Linux systems.
Workaround: To work around this issue, follow this procedure:    Use the JRE that you currently have on your system, or download and install a JRE that will work on your system.    Extract the MCR installer executable to a directory such as /tmp. Use the command:?    ./MCRinstaller.bin -is:extract /tmp        Run the MCR installer in the /tmp directory. Use the command:    ?    java -jar setup.jar        This will run the MCR installer using whatever JRE you have on your path; not the JRE included with the MCR package.

Summary: Reports generated from a Report Generator R12.1 report template produces many "N/A" Entries in Stateflow Summary and Property Tables
Description: Reports generated from a Report Generator R12.1 report template produces many "N/A" Entries in Stateflow Summary and Property Tables.
Workaround: Follow these steps to work around this issue:Copy the attached file v1convert_att.p tomatlab/toolbox/stateflow/stateflow/@rptgen_sf/@csf_summ_table.In the directory matlab/toolbox/matlab/iofun/@rptgen/@rpt_prop_table, back up the file v1convert_table.p.Copy the attached file v1convert_table.p to     matlab/toolbox/matlab/iofun/@rptgen/@rpt_prop_table. In the directory matlab/toolbox/matlab/iofun/@rptgen/@rpt_prop_table, back up the file setSingleValueMode.p.Copy the attached file setSingleValueMode.p to     matlab/toolbox/matlab/iofun/@rptgen/@rpt_prop_table.

Summary: FDATool generated MATLAB code errors for some Nyquist filters
Description: In FDATool, if you design a Nyquist filter by specifying bandwidth instead of rolloff (for example, a default euiripple FIR Nyquist filter) and then generate a MATLAB file, the generated MATLAB code errors when you run it.
Workaround: On line 22 of the generated MATLAB file, change R to BW. The corrected line isb = firnyquist(N, L, BW, D, DT);

Summary: plotyy creates second x-axis and modifies left-side y-axis when an xlabel is used
Description: When plotting into two Y-axes with plotyy , as in the following code,figure;subplot(3,1,1);ax = plotyy(1:10,1:10,1:10,10:-1:1); When I add a label,xlabel('test label'); a second X-axis is drawn above the original X-axis, translated by the height of the xlabel. The height of the left-side Y-axis is also reduced by the height of the xlabel. The second X-axis intersects with the left-side Y-axis. As a result the tick marks on that axis are in the wrong locations.
Workaround: Use the output arguments from plotyy (the axes vector) to call the linkprop function to link the Position property of the two axes, as follows:figure;subplot(3,1,1);ax = plotyy(1:10,1:10,1:10,10:-1:1);lp = linkprop(ax,'Position');xlabel('test label');The position properties can become unlinked if lp ever goes out of scope, so it should be placed in the UserData of one of the axes.

Summary: S-Function Target cannot be used with LCC compiler
Description: You cannot build an S-Function Target using SimDriveline and the LCC compiler.  A linker error will occur.
Workaround: Download the attached file, then copy it into this directory, overwriting the existing file.$matlabroot>/toolbox/physmod/drive/lib/win32/

Summary: heaviside function produces different results for vector input and single value input when computed using?subs function
Description: Using the subs  function with the heaviside  function can return different values?for a scalar than a vector. For example the value at x=1  is different for the two subs  statements below:syms x af = heaviside(x-1)*(x-1)*a;subs(f,x,1)subs(f,x,0:2)

Summary: before_make hook build error during xPC Target application build process
Description: The following error is returned when building an xPC Target applicationfrom a model that has multiple signals with the same signal label names. Error building Real-Time WorkShop target for block diagram '' MATLAB error message:Error using ==> callMakeHook.p>callMakeHook at 47The call to xpctarget_make_rtw_hook, during the before_make hook generated the following error.Attempt to reference field of non-structure arrayThe build process will terminate as a result.
Workaround: The issue is in one of the xPC Target helper files required for post build processing. To work around this issue:Quit MATLAB.Rename the following filematlabroot\toolbox\rtw\targets\xpc\xpc\private\xml2xpctarget.m tomatlabroot\toolbox\rtw\targets\xpc\xpc\private\xml2xpctargetOld.m Download the attached file.Place the new file in the same directory mentioned in step 2.Restart MATLAB.

Summary: Loading and saving?parameters files for electric drive blocks?does not work
Description: The Load and Save?buttons in the dialog boxes of the?blocks in the Electric Drives?library (both?AC drive and DC drive blocks)?issue the following error when you try to use them: ??? Undefined function or method 'File' for input arguments of type 'char'.

Summary: The fwrite?function does not support writing out sparse arrays.
Description: By their nature, sparse arrays are not suitable for writing out using the fwrite  function.? Currently, MATLAB generates a segmentation?violation error?when this is attempted.
Workaround: None

Summary: Zooming on Tradeoff graphs causes an error
Description: When you try to zoom in on a graph in the Tradeoff view of the CAGE tool, an error is displayed in the MATLAB Command Window and the graph does not zoom.  The error is: ??? Index exceeds matrix dimensions.Error in ==> D:\Applications\MATLAB\R2006b\toolbox\mbc\mbcview\@cgtradeoffgui\@tradeoffGraphView\tradeoffGraphView.p>i_select at 112??? Error while evaluating patch ButtonDownFcn
Workaround: A patch is available to fix this error in Versions 3.1 and 3.2. To install this fix:Rename the file $MATLAB\toolbox\mbc\mbcview\@cgtradeoffgui\@toGVDataModel\zoom.p to $MATLAB\toolbox\mbc\mbcview\@cgtradeoffgui\@toGVDataModel\zoom.p.old.Download the file zoom.m that is listed in the Attachments section and save it to $MATLAB\toolbox\mbc\mbcview\@cgtradeoffgui\@toGVDataModel\zoom.m.Restart MATLAB.

Summary: Deleting a masked subsystem while its parameter dialog box is open can crash MATLAB
Description: Programmatically deleting a masked subsystem whose parameter dialog box is open can cause MATLAB to terminate with a memory segmentation violation.
Workaround: Mask initialization or other code that creates a masked subsystem should avoid deleting blocks whose dialogs can be opened by the user while the code is executing.

Summary: The value of a global variable can change unpredictably
Description: Under certain conditions, the value of a global variable may revert back to a previous value when a MATLAB function is called.
Workaround: You can keep this from happening by disabling the MATLAB JIT Accelerator. To do this, enter the following command at the MATLAB command prompt:   feature('accel','off');

Summary: Generating code for a model containing a To File block overwrites the MAT-file associated with the block.
Description: Simulating a model with a To File block produces a MAT-file. Generating code for the same model overwrites the MAT-file.
Workaround: Back up the MAT-file before generating code.

Summary: Wrong answer bug for models with enabled subsytems and using a variable step solver with zero crossing detection enabled
Description: If a subsystem's zero crossing is enabled and its States when enabling  parameter is set to held , an enabled subsystem will output the last minor timestep when the subsystem is disabled instead of the last major timestep. This error is caused by numerical tolerance generated when searching for a zero crossing.
Workaround: Either use a fixed step solver, or turn off zero crossings when using a variable step solver.

Summary: Current Directory browser: dragging files or folders to an empty area fails
Description: When you move or copy folders from another application, such as Windows Explorer, to the Current Directory browser by dragging, the operation fails if the area you drag to is empty.
Workaround: If you want to drag to folder B, which is located in folder A, do not open folder B in the Current Directory browser. Instead, view folder A in the Current Directory browser, and drag to the icon for folder B that appears in folder A.

Summary: Demo and Info blocks in the Link for CCS block library do not work correctly
Description: The following blocks in the block library ccslinklib have broken links: Demos Info  Clicking the blocks does not open the demos or product information.
Workaround: To access the product demos, click the MATLAB Start button and select Toolboxes > Link for CCS(tm) > Demos.To get product information, click the MATLAB Start button and select Toolboxes > Link for CCS(tm) > Help.Follow the steps below to patch the product so the blocks work properly. This patch applies to MATLAB Version 7.4 (R2007a) only.1. Download attachment_360879_20070228.zip and extract the following patch files:patch_installer.mpatch_360879.zip2. Start MATLAB and enter ver at the prompt to verify that you are using MATLAB Version 7.4 (R2007a).3. Cd to the folder location of the patch files.4. Enter the following command at the MATLAB command prompt to install the patch:patch_installer('patch_360879.zip', 'install')The Demo and Info blocks should open the Help browser with more information about demos and the product.?

Summary: In FMINSEARCH for problems that contain variables with large magnitude, the user tolerance is effectively ignored in the stopping test.
Description: In the function FMINSEARCH, when the user problem contains variables that are large in magnitude, the stopping test can ignore effectively the tolerances (options.TolFun and options.TolX) provided by the user. The algorithm then can stop if some iteration or function count limit is reached.

Summary: Use of HDL reserved words as names for vector inputs or outputs can cause HDL compilation errors
Description: If a Simulink model contains a Stateflow chart or an Embedded MATLAB block that has the following properties:    The inputs or outputs have vector types.     VHDL or Verilog reserved words (such as in , out , wire , etc.) are used as names for vector inputs or outputs.  The reserved words used as names in the Stateflow chart or Embedded MATLAB block can be assigned as port names in the generated HDL code, resulting in errors when the code is compiled.
Workaround: Use input or output names that are not reserved words in the target HDL language.

Summary: Initialization behavior of a conditionally executed subsystem might differ when you replace a downstream Subsystem block with a Real-Time Workshop generated S-function and vice versa
Description: Initial output behavior of a conditionally executed subsystem might differ when replacing a downstream Subsystem block with a Real-Time Workshop generated S-function for the following conditions. The initial output parameter of the Outport of the conditionally executed subsystem is set to []. The source of the Outport block is a Constant or Initial Condition block. The output of the conditionally executed subsystem is directly driving a Subsystem block that is to be replaced by an equivalent Reak-Time Workshop generated S-function block.  The initialization behavior should be the same for the two situations.
Workaround: As a workaround, explicitly specify the initial output parameter for the Outport block that is in the conditionally executed subsystem.

Summary: Lookup Table block might generate incorrect results if its breakpoint vector contains multiple zeros
Description: Suppose your model includes a Lookup Table block that represents a mathematical function with a discontinuity at zero. That is, the block's Vector of input values  parameter specifies a vector containing multiple zeros. If the block's input signal is a vector that contains a zero, the block will generate incorrect results for any nonzero elements that follow the input signal vector's zero. For example, consider a Lookup Table block that specifies the following parameter values:     Vector of input values:  ?[-1 0 0 1 2]      Vector of output values:  [1 2 3 4 5]   If the Lookup Table block's input signal is the vector [-1 0 1 2] , the block outputs the incorrect vector [1 2.5 2.5 2.5]  instead of the correct vector [1 2.5 4 5] .
Workaround: Consider implementing one of the following workarounds:    Refrain from specifying multiple zeros in the Lookup Table block's Vector of input values parameter.    If the Lookup Table block's input signal is a vector, ensure that the vector does not contain a zero.    If the Lookup Table block's input signal is a vector that contains a zero, ensure that the zero is the last element of the vector.

Summary: Output connection swaps between 'and' and 'or' in Rule Editor
Description: Rule Editor allows the user to create rules which use a choice of and/or for the input connection. When the input connection type in the GUI is swapped between and/or, the connection between the outputs changes as well. This is incorrect and misleading. Each output is treated separately and the output connection is locked to 'and'.
Workaround: No workround is necessary because the bug only affects what the GUI displays.

Summary: Selector blocks generate unnecessary for-loop
Description: If you fail to set the default integer type for the embedded platform to 32-bit, the Selector block always generates a for-loop  to copy the selected elements from the input to the output. This happens even if the number of iterations in the for-loop  is 1 , such as:     for (uDimIdx = 0; (int32_T)uDimIdx < 1L; uDimIdx++) {       rtb_a1_select = (Selector0_ConstP.a1_Value[(int32_T)Selector0_U.In2 - 1L]);     }
Workaround: If the platform does not support 32-bit integer, there is no workaround.Otherwise, if the embedded platform supports 32-bit integer, change the hardware implementation setting to set as default the int type 32-bit, as follows:Open the model window and select Simulation > Configuration Parameters. In the left pane, click Hardware Implementation.Change the settings in the Embedded hardware (simulation and code generation) pane to make the integer type have 32 bits.

Summary: Incorrect code generation: global storage type signal labeled S causes incorrect code generation
Description: Real-Time Workshop might generate incorrect code if a global signal is labeled S  because S  is used as a variable by SimStruct .
Workaround: Do not label a global signal S.

Summary: Saving a control file may generate test bench file with incorrect file name
Description: Simulink HDL Coder assigns a base name to generated test bench files and test bench scripts. The base naming convention is system_tb , where system  is the name of the subsystem selected for code generation. The final file name is the base name followed by an extension appropriate to the HDL target language or script type. For example, a VHDL test bench file generated for a subsystem MyDesign  would be named MyDesign_tb.vhd . However, if you save a control file for a model from which HDL code has never been generated, test bench files and test bench scripts generated from the model will be given the base name filter_tb , regardless of the name of the selected subsystem.
Workaround: To work around this problem, edit the control file and remove the line containing the 'TestBenchName' property before generating code.

Summary: Why am I unable to access Simulink output variables saved to the workspace?
Description: If a Simulink model has been configured to output a variable to the MATLAB workspace, attempting to access the output variable from SystemTest leads to an error similar to: Undefined function or variable 'yout'.  Attempting to fix this in the model's Configuration Parameters dialog may lead to the variable name being overwritten with a temporary variable name.

Summary: Model configuration parameters are not initialized when you add Target Preferences block to a subsystem
Description: When you add a Target Preferences block to a subsystem in a model, you are not prompted to initialize the configuration parameters for the model to default values. As a result, some of the parameters for the model are not initialized correctly.
Workaround: After you add the Target Preferences block to the subsystem, open the Configuration Parameters dialog box (select Simulation > Configuration Parameters from the model menu bar) and perform the following steps:Select Real-Time Workshop from the Select tree.Set System target file to ccslink_ert.tlc or ccslink_grt.tlc.Select Solver from the Select tree.Set Stop time to inf.Set Type to Fixed-step.Set Solver to discrete (no continuous states).Select Data Import/Export from the Select tree.Clear the Time, States, Output, and Final states check boxes.Click OK to close the Configuration Paramters dialog box, or click Apply to apply your changes.Save your model.

Summary: Generating a MATLAB file from Report Generator throws a FIND error
Description: Generating a MATLAB file from figloop-tutorial.rpt produces the following error:Error using ==> findSecond argument must be a positive scalar integer
Workaround: Follow these steps to work around this issue:Rename the file$MATLAB/toolbox/matlab/codetools/@codegen/@codeblock/addProperty.mto$MATLAB/toolbox/matlab/codetools/@codegen/@codeblock/addProperty.m.oldWhere $MATLAB is the root directory of your MATLAB installation, as returned by running the command MATLABROOT from the MATLAB command line.Download the attached file addProperty.m to the $MATLAB/toolbox/matlab/codetools/@codegen/@codeblock directory.Start MATLAB.Run the command rehash toolboxcache

Summary: Segmentation violation when attempting to access logged signal data
Description: In some cases, Simulink can crash with a segmentation violation when accessing the data logging object (typically logsout ) in the MATLAB command window after simulation completes. The crash can occur when all of the following are true:     Signal Logging is enabled in the Data Import/Export pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog.     At least one Signal Viewer scope is attached a signal within a non-virtual subsystem.     Save to Workspace is disabled in the Signal Viewer scope.     No other signal within the same non-virtual subsystem is marked for data logging.
Workaround: There are two possible workarounds:    Open the Properties dialog of the Signal Viewer scope and enable Save To Workspace. To limit memory usage, set Limit Data Points to a low number, such as 1.    Turn on data logging for any other signal in the same subsystem.

Summary: Matrix dimension error when you try to open the Machine Load Flow and Initialization tool of Powergui block
Description: The following error can potentially?occur?when you try to??open the Machine Load Flow and Initialization tool of Powergui block:Error using ==> set_param Error unsing ==> eq Matrix dimension must agree The error usually happens when?the Load flow frequency (hz) ?parameter is incorrectly set to something other than a scalar value, for example, to zeros(0,1).
Workaround: Right-click on the Powergui block in your model, then select Mask Parameters.In the dialog that opens, set the Load flow frequency (hz) parameter to 60.

Summary: [clipboard/window meta file] Images get truncated when copied to word document
Description: [clipboard/window meta file] Images get truncated when copied to word document after copying to clipboard

Summary: Code generation for a Simulink model containing C6000 TCP/IP Receive block fails at compilation stage
Description: The TCP/IP Receive block has an input port that accepts the number of bytes requested for the receive operation. If a constant block with sample time Inf  is connected to this port, code generation may fail at the compile stage.
Workaround: When connecting a constant block to the Req input port of the C6000 TCP/IP block, make sure that the sample time of the constant block is not set to Inf. In a function call subsystem, use a sample time of -1 for the constant block.

Summary: Creating a UIPanel specifying another UIPanel as parent in the first argument as causes an error
Description: If a UIPanel is created with a parent handle as the first argument and that handle is another UIPanel, creating the new UIPanel fails with the following error:??? Error using ==> uipanelInvalid parent handle.
Workaround: A workaround is to specify the parent handle as a?Parent parameter/value?pair instead of using the convenience argument. For example,h = uipanel('Position', [.2 .2 .6 .6]);h1 = uipanel('Parent', h, 'Position', [.2 .2 .6 .6]);

Summary: Every other time compile or link error when compiling a model for xPC Target
Description: Every other time you try to build a target application for xPC Target, a link error is generated for symbols such as _rt_InitInfAndNaN .
Workaround: As a workaround:    Quit MATLAB.    Download the attached template makefiles and copy them into the directory:    MATLABROOT\toolbox\rtw\targets\xpc\xpc        Delete any existing modelname_xpc_rtw or modelname_xpcert_rtw directories once.    Rebuild your models.

Summary: Side effects from math operations on Simulink.Timeseries produced by data logging when the data contain fixed point (@fi) valued data
Description: If fixed-point data is logged from a Simulink model to a Simulink.Timeseries object and math operations such as plus, minus, etc. are performed on that object, the resulting operation can change the data in the Simulink.Timeseries object. For example, if tsobj  and tsobj2  are two identical Simulink.Timeseries objects containing embedded.fi objects, and the first data point in both objects are 1.0 (tsobj.Data(1)  is 1.0, tsobj2.Data(1)  is 1.0), performing the operation tsobj-tsobj2 will modify the data of the first Simulink.Timeseries tsobj  to all zeros so that tsobj.Data(1)  is now 0.
Workaround: Add the following MATLAB code:c.Handle = 'off';to the file:(matlabroot)/toolbox/simulink/simulink/@Simulink/@TimeseriesDataConstructor/schema.mYou can add this line of code anywhere in the file from line 11 downward.

Summary: Calling close_system in a custom context menu callback can lead to crashes
Description: Calling close_system  in the callback invoked as a result of selecting a custom item on a context menu is not supported and may lead to a crash.

Summary: On a Japanese Windows 2000 PC, running daqhwinfo('winsound') when a USB class audio device is installed crashes MATLAB.
Description: If I install a USB class audio device device on my Japanese language Windows 2000 machine running MATLAB R2006b and executedaqhwinfo('winsound')   MATLAB crashes with this error message:Microsoft Visual C++ Runtime LibraryRuntime Error!Program: C:\MATLAB\R2006b\bin\win32\MATLAB.exeThis application has requested the Runtime to terminate it in an unusualway.Please contact the application's support team for more information.   In release R2007a MATLAB does not crash but emits this error message:??? Error using ==> daqhwinfo at 113Maximum variable size allowed by the function is exceeded.To request support for 64-bit arrays in this functionplease send this error to 64bit@mathworks.com  This did not happen in R2006a.
Workaround: This issue has been identified as an error in the Windows 2000 driver used by these devices.Upgrade the operating system of the PC to Japanese Windows XP, as this problem does not occur in that configuration.

Summary: Some Membership Function types are missing in the FIS and Anfis Editors
Description: The Membership Funtion types list is different at different locations:1. Launch the FIS Editor and open Membership Function Editor from the Edit  menu. There are 11 choices available in the Type combo box.2. Select 'Add MF...'  from the Edit  menu in the Membership Function Editor. There are only 8 choices available in the MF Type combo box.3. Launch the Anfis Editor and click the 'Generate FIS...'  button. There are only 8 choices available in the MF Type list box.There should be 11 choices in all the above cases.
Workaround: Download the attachment and unzip its content to a temporary directory.  Replace MATLABROOT\toolbox\fuzzy\fuzzy\gfmfdlg.m and MATLABROOT\toolbox\fuzzy\fuzzy\mfdlg.m. ?

Summary: Bloomberg REALTIME callbacks are run multiple times for a single data event.
Description: Bloomberg REALTIME callbacks are run multiple times for all securities associated with a given connection handle.

Summary: When?you start a?model that contains one of the composite serial communication Receive blocks, the?Receive?FIFO?might overflow if it receives characters?too quickly
Description: When you start a model that contains one of the composite serial communication Receive blocks, the Receive FIFO might overflow if sn external device sends characters too quickly to the Receive FIFO.
Workaround: As a workaround, increase the size of the software?FIFO?to a size that is?large enough to hold all the characters that can be received during the startup processing delay.

Summary: Bus Assignment blocks may error out during the first Update Diagram
Description: If an S-function block, Stateflow block, or Embedded MATLAB block that generates a bus is connected to a Bus Assignment block, an error can occur in the Bus Assignment block when the model is compiled for the first time. The error message will be similar to the following: Selected signal 'a' in 'model/Bus Assignment' is not    part of the bus entering the Bus Assignment block.     This error can occur only on the first compilation.  It will not recur on any subsequent compilation.
Workaround: Two workarounds are available:    If the error occurs during the first Update Diagram, try updating the diagram once again.    If the error occurs in an S-function block, add this code to the model's InitFcn callback:  blk = 'model/sfunction';  % The full path to the upstream S-function block    name = get_param(blk, 'FunctionName');    set_param(blk, 'FunctionName', name); Note that the second technique will not work for a Stateflow or Embedded MATLAB block.

Summary: Models with requirement links might generate errors on computers with multiple MATLAB installations
Description: Suppose your computer has multiple installations of MATLAB (e.g., R2006a, R2006b, R2007a) and the version you are running currently is not the one that you installed last. In this case, if you load a model that contains requirement links, Simulink Verification and Validation produces error messages or displays a minimized MATLAB window. You might also encounter DMI errors when attempting to navigate from, e.g., DOORS or Microsoft Word to Simulink. This issue occurs because, during installation, MATLAB registers a COM server in the Windows registry. Regardless of which MATLAB version you choose to run, MATLAB uses the most recently registered (and hence, installed) version as the COM server.
Workaround: If you use a version of MATLAB other than the version that you last installed, you must register it as an automation server by enteringregmatlabserverin its MATLAB Command Window. Be sure to execute this command before you use any of the requirement management features in Simulink Verification and Validation. Also note that you need invoke this command only once after switching to that version of MATLAB.If you routinely switch between several versions of MATLAB, you can patch the file named com_matlab_app.m such that it automatically registers the automation server with the MATLAB executable that is currently active. To do so, follow these steps:Quit MATLAB.Rename the following file:  MATLAB\toolbox\slvnv\reqmgt\private\com_matlab_app.m ??(in R2006a and later) MATLAB\toolbox\simulink\reqmgt\private\com_matlab_app.m ??(in R14SP3 and earlier)  where MATLAB is the MATLAB root directory on your computer. To locate your MATLAB root directory, type matlabroot at the MATLAB prompt. Rename the file as com_matlab_app.m.old.Download the version of the attached file that corresponds to your MATLAB release. Place the new file in the same directory mentioned in Step 2, and rename the file as com_matlab_app.m.Repeat this process for each of your computer's MATLAB installations that you routinely use.

Summary: The Database Toolbox fastinsert function produces an error: "No method 'setDate' with matching signature found for class 'com.mysql.jdbc.ServerPreparedStatement'"
Description: Run the following code to insert date/time data into a?MySQL database, using the Database Toolbox fastinsert method: cols = {'aDate', 'aTime', 'anotherCol'}'; data1 = {'01/29/2003', '10:40:27', 'null'; ...   '01/29/2003', '10:53:39', 'Some text'; ...   '01/29/2003', '10:59:48', 'null'}; fastinsert(dbc, 'example', cols, data1); This produces the error:  ??? No method 'setDate' with matching signature found for class 'com.mysql.jdbc.ServerPreparedStatement'. Error in ==> database.fastinsert at 168??????????  StatementObject.setDate(j,java.util.Date(dt(1)-1900,dt(2)- 1,dt(3)))?? DATE
Workaround: Until a fix for this issue is available, use the attached MATLAB-file to perform the database insert.

Attempting to read GIF files while running MATLAB in headless mode on a machine that is not configured for X11 may produce the following misleading message: ???
