'Fontsize', fsize,... 'Fontname', fname, ...'HorizontalAlignment','left',... 'String', string2,... 'Position', [160 10 40 400]);

Summary: Editor automatic correction feature fails with long identifier names
Description: When a very long identifier name is used in MATLAB, MATLAB truncates it to 63 characters. The MATLAB Editor's automatic correction feature can produce incorrect fixes when names are longer than 63 characters.
Workaround: Undo the automatic correction in the Editor and add the fix manually, or manually repair the fix supplied by the Editor.

Summary: End-Of-File marker not recognized when using fscanf
Description: When using fscanf  to read a text file that ends with one or more blank lines, the feof  function is unable to detect the end of the file. As a result, code such as the following loops endlessly: fid_test = fopen('myfile.txt');while(~feof(fid_test))data = fscanf(fid_test,'%f ', [1 5])% do something with data end If the blank lines at the end of file are removed, this code runs successfully.
Workaround: A possible workaround to this problem is to use the textscan function instead. For this particular example, you could use textscan as follows:fid_test = fopen('myfile.txt');while(~feof(fid_test))celldata = textscan(fid_test,'%f ', 5)data = cell2mat(celldata);% If you have reached a blank lineif(isempty(data))breakend% Otherwise, do something with the data.end

Summary: MATLAB Component Runtime (MCR) throws "Invalid data type" when running Report Generator as a compiled application
Description: An application compiles and runs without error within MATLAB. When it is run in deployed mode with the MATLAB Component Runtime, however, the following errors are displayed:Warning: an error occurred while parsing class RptgenML.FileConverter:Invalid data type.> In T:\1-37ZZDE\StandAlone_mcr\toolbox\rptgen\rptgen\rptconvert.p>rptconvert at 150In StandAlone>reportbtn_Callback at 90In gui_mainfcn at 75In StandAlone at 42??? Undefined function or variable 'RptgenML.FileConverter'.Error in ==> T:\1- 37ZZDE\StandAlone_mcr\toolbox\rptgen\rptgen\rptconvert.p>rptconvert at 150Error in ==> StandAlone>reportbtn_Callback at 90Error in ==> gui_mainfcn at 75Error in ==> StandAlone at 42??? Error using ==> StandAlone('reportbtn_Callback',gcbo,[],guidata(gcbo))Undefined function or variable 'RptgenML.FileConverter'.??? Error while evaluating uicontrol CallbackWarning: Class 'rptgen.prop' in use at MCR termination. ?
Workaround: ?    Copy "$matlab\java\jar\toolbox\rptgen.jar" file to the extracted mcr "java\jar\toolbox" directory.    Copy "$matlab\sys\namespace\docbook" directory to the installed mcr directory.?

Summary: Web command has trouble displaying files which contain non-ASCII characters
Description: If you try to open a file which contains a non-ASCII character in its name, the web command may be unable to display it.

Summary: Generated code for Random Number blocks might corrupt memory or produce incorrect results on non-32-bit targets
Description: When Real-Time Workshop generates code for a model that contains any of the following Simulink blocks:     Band-Limited White Noise     Random Number     Uniform Random Number  it interprets an address of type int  as (uint32_T*) . Consequently, the generated code might corrupt memory or produce incorrect results when you execute it on non-32-bit targets.

Summary: Workspace browser?Std (standard deviation) column does not update when values change
Description: When the value of a numeric variable changes, the Workspace browser's Std  (standard deviation) column does not update to reflect the change.
Workaround: Use the std function at the MATLAB command line, as in:std(x)to see the standard deviation.

Summary: Simulink.Bus.createObject does not work with user defined datatypes
Description: If a bus contains any signal with a custom (user-defined) datatype, such as a Simulink.AliasType, Simulink.Bus.createObject will fail to create a bus object for the bus. The command will report an error similar to the following: ??? Error using ==> simulink\private\slbus_gen_object>create_dtype_object_if_needed_l Block 'model/block' output port 1 defines a signal of data type 'MY_CUSTOM_DATA_TYPE' which is not supported for bus objects.  Bus objects do support custom data types, so the error message is inaccurate, and the failure to create a bus object is a bug.
Workaround: Install a patch by doing the following:Backup the following file:(matlabroot)/toolbox/Simulink/Simulink/private/slbus_gen_object.pCopy the attached file, attachment_348150_20070514.zip, to a temp dir and unzip it.Copy tslbus_gen_object.m to: (matlabroot)/toolbox/Simulink/Simulink/private/ Start MATLAB and execute: 'rehash toolboxreset'The patch is now installed and ready for use.

Summary: Simulink may SEGV or exhibit other undesirable behavior if a user callback causes the undo stack to clear
Description: If a Simulink user callback function is called during an undo or redo operation and causes the undo stack to be cleared, Simulink can SEGV or exhibit other undesirable behavior. Actions that clear the undo stack typically include actions that alter the structure of the model, e.g. adding or removing blocks or lines, renaming blocks, changing the connectivity of signal lines.

Summary: The readme file ships with R2007ad contains invalid path names
Description: The file readme.txt  that ships with the MATLAB Compiler in release R2007a (4.6) contains invalid path names.
Workaround: Copy the attached readme.txt file to the /matlab/toolbox/compiler directory, replacing the existing file.

Summary: TouCAN module C not operational due to missing initialization code
Description: This problem occurs with CAN blocks from the mpc555toucan library when attempting to use TouCAN module C. Module C is only available on certain variants of the MPC5xx family. If you select the TouCAN module C, the blocks do not operate as expected: no CAN messages are transmitted or received. This is because generated code is missing some required initialization code for the TouCAN module C. This extra initialization code is required because the TouCAN module C output pins are multiplexed with pins 13 and 14 of the MIOS14 peripheral.
Workaround: It is possible to workaround the problem with TouCAN module C by adding the missing initialization code, using a System Initialize block at the top level of the model.    Add a System Initialize block (under Real-Time Workship > Custom Code)    Double-click the System Initialize block to launch the System Initialize Function Custom Code window.    Insert the following code into the System Initialize Function Declaration Code text box at the top of the window:    MIOS14.MIOS14TPCR.B.VMUX2 = 1;

Summary: Trying to import a group into the OPC Write block can generate the error "Undefined function or variable 'block'"
Description: Trying to import a group into an OPC Write block from the MATLAB workspace with this procedure:  Click Import from workspace . Choose the group to import. Click OK .  can generate the following error:  ??? Undefined function or variable 'block'.
Workaround: To work around this problem, copy the attached file into toolbox\opc\opcblks\opcmasks as opcslwritedlg.p using the following instructions: ?Save the attached file into C:\TEMP.In MATLAB, enter the commands >> cd(matlabroot);>> unzip('C:\TEMP\attachment_346533_20070611.zip', 'C:\TEMP');>> copyfile('C:\TEMP\opcslwritedlg.p', 'toolbox\opc\opcblks\opcmasks\opcslwritedlg.p');>> rehash toolboxcache

Summary: Error with configurable subsystem usage in a very limited case
Description: Consider a masked subsystem (PM) that contains a child masked subsystem (CM). This child subsystem (CM) is self modifiable such that it reconstructs itself as well has variables that depend on the parent masked subsystem (PM). A configurable subsystem using such a masked subsystem (PM) can result in a memory segmentation or other error under certain circumstances. One such situation is when the library containing the configurable subsystem is edited and the model using the configurable subsystem is then updated. This causes the configurable subsystem to configure itself in the middle of configuring itself.
Workaround: Update the library before opening a model using such a configurable subsystem

Summary: FFT Analysis tool in Powergui has trouble handling an AC signal with a large DC component
Description: When you analyze an AC signal with a very high DC component, the FFT Tool of the Powergui block is computing an incorrect THD value.
Workaround: There are no known workarounds.

Summary: Segmentation violation at startup in libmwmpath
Description: MATLAB sometimes generates a segmentation violation upon startup that contains the following line in the stack trace:   [0] libmwmpath.so:mpGetCWDName... This appears to be an issue with the timing of the MATLAB startup versus the progress of the JVM startup. Restart MATLAB after getting this message.

Summary: In MIDEDIT, the Constraints field in the Property Values tab does not update if you set the constraints for a property to bounded
Description: If you add a property in MIDEDIT, then select that property in the treeview and go to the Property Values  tab and change the Constraint  to Bounded in the drop-down list, the middle Constraints  pane does not indicate this change.
Workaround: You can force this pane to update by clicking in the Data Type field (without changing any values).

Summary: Real Time Workshop generates an error?when it used to generate a warning
Description: In previous releases Real Time Workshop would generate a warning when?the model specified a nonzero start time and the selected target did not support a nonzero start time.? If the warning was overlooked or ignored, the following consequences were possible   The generated code could fail to compile and link.  The generated code could have a different runtime behavior from the simulation.
Workaround: Set the start time to zero when generating code for targets that don't support nonzero start times, such as the GRT and ERT targets.? See the Real Time Workshop documentation for a list of targets supporting nonzero start times.

Summary: Names of subsystems intended for HDL code generation must not contain newlines
Description: When the name of a subsystem contains one or more newline characters, HDL code generation from that subsystem is not possible, and code generation terminates with with the error message: Cannot find starting subsystem for HDL code generation.HDL Code generation not supported for individual blocks, please select a subsystem.
Workaround: To work around this problem, replace newlines with spaces in the names of any root level subsystems that are intended for HDL code generation in your model.

Summary: MATLAB-file generated only for last designed filter in FDATool (launched from SPTool)
Description: If you launch FDATool from SPTool, design two or more filters, and generate an MATLAB-file, that MATLAB-file contains the last designed filter regardless of which filter is currently loaded in FDATool.

Summary: Array Editor does not show values for matrices of type INT64 with more than two dimensions
Description: The Array Editor does not show the values for matrices of type INT64  that have more than two dimensions. It instead displays this error message: Cannot display 64-bit integers.
Workaround: As a workaround, type the variable name in the Command Window and press Enter. In this case, the result displayed in the Command Window is the same as the result that would have been displayed in the Array Editor.

Summary: xPC Target does not recognize new versions of Quatech?serial boards
Description: xPC Target does not recognize the device IDs of currently shipping Quatech boards.
Workaround: As a workaround, do one of the following:    For xPC Target 3.1 (R2006b) or earlier, contact MathWorks Technical Support.    For xPC Target 3.2 (R2007a), download and incorporate the attached file, as follows:            Quit MATLAB.        Download the attached file, quatech7a_A4.zip, and unzip it to matlabroot, where matlabroot represents your MATLAB installation root, as returned by the MATLAB command matlabroot.        Restart MATLAB.        Rebuild your models.        This attachment was updated on 6/20/2007 to the A4 version of the zip file. The A3 zip file version is missing a file.

Summary: Cannot paste text into the Property Editor axes label fields
Description: The axes panel of the Property Editor plot tool contains text fields into which you can enter strings for XLabel , YLabel , and ZLabel . A bug in MATLAB prevents pasting text from the clipboard into any of these text fields.

Summary: DEPLOYPRINT shifts the printout when printing GUIs created with GUIDE in landscape mode
Description: When I print a GUI created with GUIDE in landscape mode, the image is shifted slightly and is partially cut off. The image is shifted down and to the right.
Workaround: When printing certain figures in landscape mode, a bug in MATLAB Compiler 4.4 (R2006b) causes the image to appear to be misaligned.To work around this issue, modify the PaperPosition property (as shown in the following example), to align the printout up and to the left.The following example sets the PaperPosition property to be shifted by 0.15 inches left and 0.20 inches up, placing the figure in the same position on the page as when printed from MATLAB. You may need to shift the PaperPosition by different values based upon your requirements.orient landscapeif isdeployed    pp=get(handles.figure1,'PaperPosition')    x=pp+[-0.15 0.20 0 0]    set(handles.figure1,'PaperPosition',x)       deployprint -v    set(handles.figure1,'PaperPosition',pp)  else    print -vend

Summary: Array Editor: copied data sometimes loses precision when pasted
Description: When you copy data from the Array Editor and then paste it elsewhere in MATLAB or another application, the copied data does not always have the full precision of the original data. For example, when you copy 0.1234567  and paste it into an MATLAB-file, the value in the MATLAB-file is 0.1235 .

Summary: Problems using the avhrrlambert function
Description: The avhrrlambert  function has multiple problems: The globa l region is permitted in the documentation, but causes errors when specified. When attempting to read an entire dataset, some parts of the dataset are missing. The following syntaxes are documented, but cause an error with valid inputs:  ? [latgrat, longrat, z] = avhrrlambert(region)?[latgrat, longrat, z] = avhrrlambert(region, filename)?[latgrat, longrat, z] = avhrrlambert(region, filename, scalefactor)
Workaround: To work around this issue, replace the avhrrlambert.m function file in your current installation with the version in the attachment, as follows:Exit MATLAB.Make a backup copy of the following file.matlabroot/toolbox/map/mapformats/avhrrlambert.mmatlabroot represents the name of your MATLAB installation directory, as returned by the matlabroot function.For example, rename the existing file, adding "old" to the file extension, avhrrlambert.m.OLD.Copy the version of avhrrlambert.m from the attachment into the directory specified in the previous step.Restart MATLAB.Run the rehash toolbox command at the MATLAB prompt.

Summary: MEX and MBUILD interact badly with an Oracle product's installation of Perl
Description: Running MEX or MBUILD -setup can fail if you have an Oracle product installed that defines the Perl environment variable?PERL5LIB.? MEX?and MBUILD use the version of Perl shipped with MATLAB, and?there should be no dependency on PERL5LIB or any other Perl-defined environment variable.  A potential symptom of this bug is: Can't modify subroutine entry in scalar assignment atE:\oracle\product\10.2.0\db_1\perl\5.8.3\lib/File/DosGlob.pm line 12, near"'1.00';" BEGIN not safe after errors--compilation aborted at E:\oracle\product\10.2.0\db_1\perl\5.8.3\lib/File/DosGlob.pm line 13.

Summary: Multiple Web applications accessing a .NET Builder component cannot run side by side in IIS 5
Description: When two independent Web applications, accessing?one or more?.NET Builder components, are running and a page from the second application is accessed, an error message is returned stating "Object reference not set to an instance of an object."
Workaround: Patches?containing?the fix for?this bug for the V2006b and V2007a releases are attached to this report.? You should select the appropriate file, rename it to MWArray.dll.? Then complete the following steps from? a Visual Studio 2003 DOS window for the VS7 files:??? Delete the old version of the MWArray assembly from the GAC (gacutil /u MWArray)???? Copy the new version of the assembly to the directory containing the old version overwriting it.??? Add the new version of the assembly to the GAC (gacutil /i MWArray.dll)It should not be necessary to rebuild and .NET components or applications but you should restart IIS using the "Internet Information Services" tool.If you do not wish to use these patches then you?must restart IIS (as above), or configure the two Web applications so that they are running in different processes.

Summary: Why does MATLAB disappear when attempting to debug a test?
Description: At this time, SystemTest does not support any commands that interrupt the execution of a test, such as the DBSTOP or KEYBOARD functions. Attempting to use these commands may cause SystemTest and/or MATLAB to crash or disappear.

Summary: FZERO never returns when no root exists
Description: In some cases, when the equation being solved has no roots, FZERO may enter an infinite loop in the bracketing phase of the algorithm and never return. A trivial example of this occurs if you attempt to find the root of the constant function f(x) = 1:x = fzero(@(x) 1, 0, optimset('display','iter'))

Summary: DECIMATE?function causes a segmentation violation in a compiled application
Description: Compiled applications cannot call the Signal Processing Toolbox decimate  function on any version of Microsoft Windows. This problem is caused by interaction between the Signal Processing Toolbox and Simulink-based functionality, which is not deployable. Applications that call decimate  will fail to compile (the?mcc  command will terminate unexpectedly). This problem does not occur on UNIX platforms.

Summary: S-Function Builder block with nonscalar complex parameter returns wrong values
Description: Suppose your model contains an S-Function Builder block that specifies a nonscalar, complex parameter, such as [1-1i, 2-2i] . If your C-code that comprises the S-Function Builder block accesses that parameter, the S-function returns the wrong values. Consequently, the S-Function Builder block might generate incorrect simulation results.

Summary: isequal appears to give incorrect results with anonymous function handles
Description: The isequal  function appears to give incorrect results with anonymous function handles saved to different MAT-files and then loaded back into MATLAB. Create two equivalent anonymous function handles:    h1 = @(x) sin(x);   h2 = h1;   isequal(h1, h2)   ans =        1 Save each to a different MAT-file:    save fname1 h1;   save fname2 h2; Clear the workspace and then load the function handles back into the MATLAB workspace:    clear all   load fname1   load fname2 The function handles are no longer equal:    isequal(h1, h2)   ans =        0 This is not a bug, but rather insufficiently documented behavior of isequal  on anonoymous function handles. Saving the function handle in effect loses track of the original circumstances under which the function handle was created, and reloading it results in a function handle that compares as being unequal to the original function handle. Note however that anonymous function handles saved to the same MAT-file are equal when loaded back into MATLAB:    h1 = @(x) sin(x);   h2 = h1;   isequal(h1, h2)   ans =        1   save fname h1 h2;   clear all   load fname   isequal(h1, h2)   ans =        1
Workaround: There is no workaround.

Summary: Pro/ENGINEER-to-SimMechanics Translator Sometimes Exports Invalid Coordinate System Rotation Matrix
Description: The Pro/ENGINEER-to-SimMechanics translator exports an invalid component rotation matrix if the assembly unit does not match the component's model unit. This is because Pro/ENGINEER handles the unit mismatch between an assembly and its component model by miscombining the scaling portion and the rotation portion of the component's transformation matrix.No other cases of invalid 3-by-3 rotation matrices embedded in component transformation matrices from Pro/ENGINEER are known at this time.
Workaround: Use the same model unit in the assembly and all its components (parts, subassemblies, and constraints) in Pro/ENGINEER before exporting to Physical Modeling XML. The model unit can be changed in Pro/ENGINEER by selecting Edit -> Setup -> Units from the menu bar.

Summary: TMTOOL does not find instrument drivers when you right-click the 'Instrument Object' node to create instrument objects.
Description: TMTOOL  can respond incorrectly when locating driver files while trying to create a device Object.?? When you right-click the 'Instrument Object' node to create a new instrument object, and select the driver from the dropdown list, you will see this error message:Driver cannot be found on MATLAB path
Workaround: The following steps will enable TMTOOL? to find the instrument driver.    Open tmtool    Right Click the 'Device Object' node under 'Instrument Objects' node    Select 'Create New Device Object'    Click 'Browse' next to the 'Instrument Driver' edit box and select a driver from outside your MATLAB path    Select or create any interface object    Click OK

Summary: Error message "Unable to Locate DLL" (MFC80U.DLL) at startup in R2006b/R2007a
Description: You may encounter the following error messages when trying to start MATLAB R2006b or R2007a. MATLAB R2006b appears to run correctly, in spite of these messages. MATLAB R2007a will not run. The problem occurs with MATLAB installations on 32-bit Windows (Windows 2000 SP3 or SP4, Windows XP SP1, Windows Server 2003). At startup MATLAB tries to verify that Microsoft Visual Studio 2005 run-time libraries have been installed. If these libraries have not been installed, you get the following message: VCRT_check.exe -- Unable to Locate DLL Additional message text refers to MFC80U.DLL . MATLAB then tries to install these libraries by running vcredist_x86.exe .If you are running older versions of Windows, you may not be using Microsoft Windows Installer 3.x. If so, the Visual Studio 2005 run-time library installation fails: Error 1723, There is a problem with this Windows Installer package. A DLL required for this install to complete could not be run. Contact your support personnel or package vendor.
Workaround: MATLAB R2007a requires the Visual Studio 2005 run-time libraries. You MUST perform Procedure B below.MATLAB R2006b (32-bit) does not require Visual Studio 2005 run-time libraries and runs correctly without them. These run-time libraries are only required by customers who want to run MEX-files that were built on a separate computer using Visual Studio 2005.There are two ways to handle this problem. If you do not need to run such MEX-files, follow Procedure A. If you do need to run MEX-files built with Visual Studio 2005, follow Procedure B.Procedure A If you do not need to run MEX-files built with Visual Studio 2005, you can circumvent Visual Studio 2005 run-time checking and installation by doing the following:?    Locate your MATLAB.bat file by clicking Start -> Programs -> MATLAB -> R2006b, then right-clicking on MATLAB R2006b. This should display a menu with Properties at the bottom.    Click on Properties. The field labeled Target should show the location of your MATLAB.bat.    Use the Windows Explorer to navigate to the folder identified above.    Rename MATLAB.bat to MATLAB.bat.old.    Click the attachment link below and download matlab.R2006b_330449.patch.    Rename this file MATLAB.bat and place it in the same directory as the original MATLAB.bat file.Procedure B If you need to run MEX-files built with Visual Studio 2005, you can obtain a current version of the Windows Installer from Microsoft. (Your PC must be connected to the Internet and be able to access Microsoft's Web site.)Windows Installer 3.1 (v2) is required for the Visual Studio 2005 run-time library installation to complete successfully and is available from Microsoft's Web site at:http://www.microsoft.com/downloads/details.aspx?FamilyID=889482FC-5F56-4A38-B838-DE776FD4138C&displaylang=enAlternatively, you can use Windows Update by running Internet Explorer and selecting Windows Update from the Tools menu. If you follow the instructions, the application will walk you through whatever you need to update your PC (and the Windows Installer will be made up-to-date).You can find more information about the Microsoft Windows Installer at:http://support.microsoft.com/?kbid=893803

Summary: Array Editor does not correctly display short e and?short g formats
Description: The Array Editor does not correctly display short e and short g formats.?For example, selecting View > Numeric Array Format > short e  does not display variables in short e format.
Workaround: Set the format and view the variable's value in the Command Window. For example, run this statementformat short e; myvariableto view the value of myvariable in the short e format.

Summary: Bar plots draw incorrectly with YScale=log
Description: By default, the bar  command creates a bar plot that starts at 0. If the YScale  axes property is set to log , then 0 is not a valid Y value, and the plot is not drawn correctly. p=bar(rand(10,1))set(gca,'YScale','log')
Workaround: Simply set the plot object's BaseValue property to a small positive value like this: p=bar(rand(10,1),'basevalue',1e-24)

Summary: Debugging: after continuing from a breakpoint in the Editor/Debugger,?MATLAB sometimes terminates
Description: If MATLAB is stopped in a MATLAB file in the Editor/Debugger, when you continue running, MATLAB sometimes terminates.MATLAB does not generate an error log.
Workaround: For situations where you encounter the problem, use MATLAB debugging functions (for example, dbstop and dbcont) instead of the Editor/Debugger debugging features. Before you begin?debugging with the Editor/Debugger,?save any MATLAB file changes you made?so you do not lose your work in the event MATLAB encounters the problem.

Summary: Wrong library may be used after changing the MATLAB path or current directory
Description: If you change the MATLAB path or current directory, and a Simulink library in memory becomes "shadowed" by another library of the same name higher on the MATLAB path, the loaded library is used.For example, consider a Simulink model C:\work\mymodel.mdl , which uses C:\work\mylibrary.mdl .? Directory C:\work  is not on the MATLAB path.? You enter: cd C:\workopen_system mymodelsim mymodel At this point, library C:\work\mylibrary.mdl  is loaded into memory but is not visible.? Even if you close "mymodel ", the library remains in memory.You now change to another directory which contains files with the same names: C:\old_work\mymodel.mdl  and C:\old_work\mylibrary.mdl . cd C:\old_workopen_system mymodelsim mymodel Because a library called "mylibrary " is already in memory, Simulink uses it, rather than loading the one in C:\old_work .? This means that the wrong library is used in the call to "sim ".
Workaround: The avoid this scenario, close all block diagrams (models and libraries) explicitly after changing the MATLAB path or current directory by entering the following:bdclose allBefore doing this, make sure that you save any changes to open models or libraries.For more information about this type of problem and how to solve or avoid it, see:Shadowed Files in the Simulink documentation.

Summary: For some Power Electronics circuits, system does not converge and simulation stops
Description: In some circuits containing Power Electronics devices (Thyristors, Diodes, IGBTs, etc.) it is possible that the simulation stops prematurely with the following error message:"ERROR! - Reached 20 Iterations without result!!"  Typically, the simulation stops during a change in status (closing, opening) of a diode or a thyristor block. ?
Workaround: Setting Vf voltage to 0 and refining the snubber parameters of the Power Electronics devices in your circuit can help to eliminate this error.In SimPowerSystems Version 4.4 (R2007a), this error has been fixed for discrete simulation modes.

Summary: Entity and signal name conflicts are not resolved as described
Description: The Entity conflict postfix  and Reserved word postfix  strings, which are used to resolve symbol generation conflicts caused by names of subsystems, signals, and other components in a Simulink model, are not currently used as described in the documentation. Simulink HDL Coder resolves entity and signal name conflicts to unique and legal names in generated code. No compilation errors result from generated code. However, the naming conventions described in the documentation are not followed.
Workaround: To work around this problem, avoid using VHDL and Verilog reserved words for entity and signal names in Simulink models, or for clock, reset, and clock enable port names that you define in Simulink HDL Coder. Complete lists of reserved words can be found in the IEEE specifications for VHDL (IEEE Std 1076 and IEEE Std 1164) and Verilog (IEEE Std 1364).

Summary: Segmentation violation while compiling with mcc
Description: In rare cases, mcc  may crash with a segmentation violation during compilation. When this happens, mcc  will stop processing and a message will be displayed in the MATLAB command window that begins "Segmentation violation detected...". After this message, a stack trace is printed. The top 10 frames are printed below: [0] libut.dll:_ut_ghash_compare_string_keys[1] libut.dll:struct _ghash_bucket * __cdecl ut_ghash_find_bucket [2] libut.dll:_ut_ghash_insert_element[3] libut.dll:_ut_ghash_insert_key[4] libut.dll:_ut_namehash_insert_string[5] common_context.dll:_initialize_common_M[6] m_ir.dll:_mps_initialize_common0[7] m_ir.dll:_mps_initialize_common [8] common_context.dll:_initialize_common_context_M [9] compiler.dll:void __cdecl initialize_compiler_context_1  ?
Workaround: There are no workarounds.

Summary: Multiport Selector block with unordered vectors is not supported for HDL code generation
Description: The Multiport Selector block does not generate correct HDL code when used with unordered vectors ([N]).
Workaround: To work around this problem, use ordered one-dimensional vectors (1xN, Nx1) with the Multiport Selector block.

Summary: Effect of volume on stochastic rate constants must be factored in manually.
Description: If you perform stochastic simulation of a deterministic model, you must convert any reaction rates that would get affected by model volume and stoichiometry.  In addition, whenever species amount is specified in the units of amount per volume, SimBiology assumes that volume is unity during stochastic simulations.
Workaround: If you have reactions with stoichiometric coefficients greater than or equal to 2, you must manually convert the deterministic rate constants to stochastic rate constants while performing stochastic simulations.For example, 2 R -> P has a reaction rate of v=k*[R]^2. If R is in moles/liter, the deterministic rate constant k has units of liter/mole-second. The stochastic solvers require the amount of species to be in molecules, therefore the corresponding stochastic rate constant c has units of 1/molecule-second. For this reaction, the relation between c and k is given by the equation c = (2*k)/(N*V), where N is Avogadro's Number, 6.022e23 molecules/mole, and V is the volume of the model in liters. While performing stochastic simulation on this reaction, convert the deterministic rate constant k to stochastic rate constant c manually.On the other hand, if you specify the species units in molecules per unit volume or moles per unit volume and perform stochastic simulation, SimBiology assumes volume to be unity and converts species amounts to molecules for simulation. The results however, are plotted in the user specified units for the species.

Summary: Error when simulating radial-basis neural network using NEWRB and SIM with specified network target
Description: The function?sim  returns an error when called with a network created by? newrb  and the optional target argument.
Workaround: Do not use?sim with the optional argument T, as shown in the following example:[Y,Pf,Af,E,perf] = sim(net,P,[],[],T);Use the following syntax instead:Y = sim(net,P);E = calcerr(T,Y);perf = feval(net.performFcn,E,Y,getx(net),net.performParam);

Summary: UserData field of Neural Network object does not load correctly
Description: The neural network UserData field?saved in an older version of Neural Network Toolbox does not load correctly in the new version.
Workaround: Add this line to the end of UPDATENET.M:? net.userdata = s.userdata;

Summary: Video colors appear incorrectly when using aviread on files compressed with MJPEG codec.
Description: When reading AVI files into MATLAB using aviread , the color of the imported video frames can appear incorrectly when reading some files that are compressed with MJPEG compression. This is only an issue on Windows systems. aviread  does not support compression on non-Windows platforms.
Workaround: The problem appears to be an incorrect ordering of the RGB color planes that are read in.The following provides a work-around:  mov = aviread(filename);  for framenum = 1:length(mov)    mov(framenum).cdata = mov(framenum).cdata(:,:,[2 3 1]);  endNote: In order to read MJPEG-compressed video with MATLAB using aviread, a codec compatible with Video For Windows (VFW) must be installed.

Summary: The keypress function no longer responds after converting an?edit box from single line to multiline
Description: When you change?a single line edit box to a multiline edit box the key press function stops responding.?The same problem occurs when converting a?multiline edit box to a single line edit box.When setting up uicontrol editable text fields, i.e. the Style  property is set to 'edit' , if? (Max - Min) > 1 , then multiple lines are allowed. For multi-line edit boxes, a vertical scroll bar enables you to scroll the text. You can also use the arrow keys to scroll.

Summary: Real-Time Workshop does not honor stack size limit for target
Description: Stateflow and Embedded MATLAB may generate code that exceeds the stack size limit setting in RTW Configuration options. This affects Embedded MATLAB Function blocks, Stateflow, and TruthTable blocks that define large temporary vector variables that may not fit within the stack size limits of the target system. ?

Summary: High zoom levels on large Simulink models may result in missing or malfunctioning scroll bars
Description: With some very large Simulink models, it is possible to position content in a model beyond the Simulink model editor's 3276x3276 viewable rectangle limit: This causes scroll bars to vanish or fail to scroll to the full extent of a model at zoom levels greater than 100%.
Workaround: Return the zoom factor to 100% .

Summary: Segmentation violation in polybool function due to Improper polygon topology
Description: Passing polygon data with corrupt topology to function polybool  (or to bufferm  in R2006b or later) can result in a segmentation violation in private function gpcmex .

Summary: Windows? XP platforms: printing from Command Window or Editor/Debugger ends prematurely or terminates MATLAB program
Description: On Microsoft? Windows XP platforms, when printing a multipage document with certain fonts from the Editor/Debugger or Command Window, not all pages print, or MATLAB terminates unexpectedly. The problems seem to occur most often with PostScript? printers.
Workaround: Change the font used for the component from which you are printing to a physical font instead of a generic font. The problem occurs when a generic name, such as "Monospaced" or "Sans Serif", is used instead of a physical font, such as "Courier New" or "Arial".To change the font, do one of the following:To change the font only for printing and not for what you see in the desktop tool, use File > Page Setup > Fonts from the Command Window or Editor/Debugger.To change the font you see for the desktop tool as well as for printing, use File > Preferences > Fonts. For detailed instructions, click Help in the dialog box.

Summary: External mode: connection between 32-bit and 64-bit machines does not work
Description: When you use external mode to connect to a running target executable, the?instance of Simulink which built the target executable must have the same bit architecture (32-bit or 64-bit) as the instance of Simulink connecting to the target executable.? For example, if Simulink on a 32-bit architecture?is used to build the target executable, Simulink on a 32-bit architecture must be used when connecting to the running target executable.
Workaround: Make sure the instance of Simulink which built the target executable is the same architecture (32-bit or 64-bit) as the instance of Simulink connecting to the running target executable.

Summary: (Windows only) MATLAB Compiler does not correctly compile many Filter Design and Fixed Point toolbox functions
Description: On computers running Microsoft Windows, installing the Fixed Point Toolbox might cause compiling and deployment problems when compiling functions from the Filter Design and Signal Processing Toolboxes. Although the compilation of these functions might appear to complete successfully, the application you deploy with the components created by the compilation process will not run properly. These problems do not occur on UNIX systems. The compiling problems occur if either of the following are true:     The installation contains the Filter Design Toolbox, and either Simulink or the Fixed Point Toolbox.     The installation contains the Signal Processing Toolbox and the Fixed Point Toolbox.  Either combination of toolboxes causes the following functions to become uncompilable:     DFILT     MFILT     FDESIGN Filter Design functions     DFILT Signal Processing Toolbox functions  NOTE: The Signal Processing Toolbox DFILT function does compile  if the Fixed Point Toolbox is not installed. The problem occurs because many functions in the Filter Design and Fixed Point toolboxes rely on features of Simulink and DA Studio, which are not deployable.
Workaround: If your application does not make use of Fixed Point Toolbox functions (directly or indirectly) you could avoid this problem by removing the Fixed Point Toolbox (and all its subdirectories) from the MATLAB path before you compile your application.If you use this workaround, be sure to restore the Fixed Point Toolbox directory to the path if you want MATLAB to access Fixed Point toolbox functions.See the RMPATH and ADDPATH commands for details on how to manipulate the MATLAB path.There is no other workaround or fix at this time.

Summary: Function-call subsystems: invalid code generated when function-call port is connected to Ground and Outport is connected to Merge
Description: Real Time Workstop can generate code that does not compile if all of the following conditions exist for the model:     Parameters are inlined     A function-call subsystem has its trigger port connected to a Ground block (or unconnected)     The function-call subsystem has its Output port connected to a Merge block     The function-call subsystem is not inlined
Workaround: Replace the Ground block with a Stateflow or S-Function block that defines a function-call output, but never issues a function call.

Summary: Printers with hyphens (-) in their names?always indicate "Not accepting Jobs"
Description: On UNIX, the Print to file  printing GUI option fails if the corresponding printer has the status "Not accepting Jobs". This indication is always present if the printer's name contains a hyphen ("-").Printing from the command line? with print  does work, however.
Workaround: Use the print command and specify a driver and a filename.

Summary: Some returned data is being repeated using National Instruments devices.
Description: Under certain conditions using National Instruments devices, it appears that acquired data is repeated in the matrix returned by getdata . This appears to be intermittant in nature.Example: ai = analoginput('nidaq', 'Dev2'); set(ai, 'SampleRate', 50000);addchannel(ai, 0);%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%% let the adaptor automatically set the bufferingconfig% the block size should be 4096 for nidaq boardsset(ai, 'SamplesPerTrigger', 6000);start(ai)wait(ai, 10)[cdata, tbase] = getdata(ai);figureplot(cdata);hold on;% draw a line at the block size to make it more evident to seeblockSize = ai.BufferingConfig;blockSize = blockSize(1);l1 = line([blockSize, blockSize], [min(cdata),max(cdata)]);set(l1, 'LineStyle', '--', 'color', 'r')grid on;% now manually double the buffersize, you will see the discontinuous area moved accordinglyai.BufferingConfig = [blockSize*2, 30];set(ai, 'SamplesPerTrigger', 10000);start(ai)wait(ai, 10)figure[cdata, tbase] = getdata(ai);plot(cdata);hold on;blockSize = ai.BufferingConfig;blockSize = blockSize(1);l2 = line([blockSize, blockSize], [min(cdata),max(cdata)]);set(l2, 'LineStyle', '--', 'color', 'r')grid on;% % cleanupstop(ai);delete(ai);
Workaround: This is a bug in version 7.3 and later of the National Instruments Traditional NIDAQ driver. It also appeared in version 7.3 of the National Instruments NIDAQmx drivers, but has been fixed in version 7.5 and later.Updating to National Instruments NIDAQmx version 7.5 or later fixes this corruption. These drivers are available at the National Instruments website.In addition to using the latest versions of the drivers it is important to ensure that if you are using an external clock source it is set correctly. In the one case in which this issue was reported they were using:set(AI,'clocksource','ExternalSampleCtrl'); set(AI,'ExternalSampleClockSource', 'PFI0');when what was needed wasset(AI,'clocksource','ExternalScanCtrl'); set(AI,'ExternalScanClockSource', 'PFI0');?

Summary: Simulink HDL Coder evaluates block parameters in the MATLAB  base workspace
Description: When creating or using masked subsystems, Simulink HDL Coder evaluates all mask parameters in the base workspace and does not follow the Simulink rules for parameter evaluation.  This can cause problems using masked subsystems.
Workaround: To work around this limitation for masked subsystems that you create, avoid using variables for lower-level parameters in your masked subsystem. Instead, include a callback that uses set_param to set actual values in the mask helper function .

Summary: Subsystems: rate transition error can occur during right-click subsystem builds
Description: If a subsystem in your model has disconnected or grounded input signals and you use the right-click subsystem build feature to build the subsystem, you might encounter a rate transition error. The error can occur even if you were able to build the parent model without receiving a warning.
Workaround: Do not specify a sample time for the Inport block of a subsystem for which you plan to use the right-click build mechanism. Leave the Inport block sample time set to -1, which causes the input port to inherit its sample time from the destination block, parent subsystem or model.

Summary: Simulink fails to initialize properly the default values of particular block parameters
Description: Simulink fails to initialize properly the default values of boolean parameters associated with particular blocks. Here, boolean parameters refer to block options that you specify as either on  or off , typically by selecting or deselecting a check box on a block's parameter dialog box. This issue applies only to the boolean parameters of the following Simulink blocks:     Assertion     Clock     Constant     Data Store Memory     Discrete-Time Integrator     Display     Gain     Inport     Integrator     Logical Operator     Lookup Table     Lookup Table (2-D)     Model     Multiport Switch     Outport     Product     Relational Operator     Relay     Scope     Subsystem     Sum  This issue impacts the checksum that Simulink computes for models or subsystems to which you added any of the preceding blocks. For example, consider the following script: % Create and open a new modelmdl = 'test_model';new_system(mdl), open_system(mdl)% Add an Outport block to the modeladd_block('built-in/Outport', [mdl, '/Out1'])% Compute the checksum of the modelc1 = Simulink.BlockDiagram.getChecksum(mdl)% Ensure that one of the Outport block's boolean parameters,% 'BusOutputAsStruct', specifies its default valueblk = [mdl, '/Out1'];set_param(blk, 'BusOutputAsStruct', get_param(blk, 'BusOutputAsStruct'));% Recompute the checksum of the modelc2 = Simulink.BlockDiagram.getChecksum(mdl)% Compare the previously computed checksumsisequal(c1, c2) The script creates a new model, adds an Outport block to the model, and computes the model's checksum. Then the script executes the set_param  function to reset the default value for one of the Outport block's boolean parameters. This command effectively alters the model's contents because Simulink failed to initialize properly the default value of that parameter. The script demonstrates this issue by recomputing the model's checksum and comparing it with the previous checksum, for which the comparison returns 0  (false). Because this issue impacts the checksums that Simulink computes, note that code generation tools that use checksums, such as Simulink Accelerator, might behave incorrectly.
Workaround: After adding any of the affected blocks to a model for which you intend to compute a checksum, perform the following steps:    Save your model.    Close your model.    Reopen your model.When reloading your model, Simulink properly initializes the default values of the boolean parameters associated with those blocks.

Summary: Receive "??? NIDAQmx error -50103: The specified resource is reserved" when attempting to use E-series or S-series National Instruments Device
Description: If you have both the NI-DAQmx and Traditional NI-DAQ interfaces installed, and you use daqhwinfo and then attempt to start a device that can be used through either interface, such as an E-series or S-series device, you may receive this error message: ai=analoginput('nidaq','Dev4');addchannel(ai,0);start(ai)x=daqhwinfo('nidaq');   ??? NIDAQmx error -50103: The specified resource is reserved. The operation could not be completed as specified.  You cannot simultaneously access a board cannot be simultaneously accessed from both the NI-DAQmx interface and the Traditional NI-DAQ interface. Once you access a device from the Traditional NI-DAQ interface, it is reserved by Traditional NI-DAQ, and cannot be used by the NI-DAQmx interface. The call to daqhwinfo accesses the board via both interfaces, causing the device to be reserved by the Traditional NI-DAQ interface. When the NI-DAQmx interface attempts to start the device, it is unable to reserve it, causing the error.Note that other tools that query the devices, such as SoftScope will also be unable to access the device via NI-DAQmx after they have been accessed under Traditional NI-DAQ.
Workaround: This is caused by a limitation associated with the interaction of the Traditional NI-DAQ and NI-DAQmx interfaces. You can work around this issue in any of the following ways:If you do not need to access devices via the Traditional NI-DAQ interface (i.e., numeric device IDs), uninstall the Traditional NI-DAQ driver.Avoid the use of daqhwinfo('nidaq').Use National Instrument's Measurement and Automation Explorer to reset the Traditional NI-DAQ device which will make it available under NI-DAQmx.Use the Traditional NI-DAQ interface (numeric device IDs) to access the device in question.

Summary: Startup might fail with Windows XP custom desktop theme
Description: MATLAB might terminate unexpectedly upon startup on Windows systems using a custom Windows XP theme, such as the Microsoft Plus! themes. You might see the following error in the Command Window:  java.lang.NullPointerExceptionat javax.swing.border.EmptyBorder.(Unknown Source) at com.sun.java.swing.plaf.windows.WindowsTableHeaderUI$XPDefaultRenderer   .getTableCellRendererComponent(Unknown Source) at com.mathworks.mwswing.table.SortedTableHeaderRenderer   .getTableCellRendererComponent(SortedTableHeaderRenderer.java:131)at javax.swing.plaf.basic.BasicTableHeaderUI   .getHeaderRenderer(Unknown Source)at javax.swing.plaf.basic.BasicTableHeaderUI.getHeaderHeight   (Unknown Source)
Workaround: To work around the problem, use a standard Windows XP theme. To do so, in the Windows desktop, select Start > Settings > Control Panel > Display. Change the Theme to Windows XP or Windows Classic. You can customize these standard themes (for example, select large fonts or change colors).Alternatively, change the properties of the MATLAB executable to run in Windows 2000 mode. To do soIn the Windows desktop, select Start.Navigate to the MATLAB application.Right-click MATLAB and select Properties.Select the Compatibility tab, select the Run this program in compatibility mode for: check box, and then select Windows 2000 .?

Summary: Array Editor: format doesn't exactly match the equivalent format in the Command Window
Description: In the Array Editor, the format does not always exactly match the equivalent format in the Command Window. For example, run format short ex = 1e-5 MATLAB displaysx =   1.0000e-05 However, when the Array Editor preference for format is short e  and you view x  in the Array Editor, the value appears as 0 .

Summary: RTDX target emulation protocol error message when reloading a DM642 EVM project
Description: When a project generated for the DM642 EVM target already exists in the current working directory, reloading the .out  file for the project by clicking File>Reload Program  in Code Composer Studio can fail with the following error message in the CCS IDE:RTDX target does not match emulation protocol!Loaded program was created with an RTDX library which does not match the target device.  ?
Workaround: This error message is caused by a bug in the OnPreFileLoaded() function in the DM642 EVM gel file (for both CCS 2.2 and CCS 3.0). The OnPreFileLoaded() function looks like this: OnPreFileLoaded() { /*    GEL_Reset();      -- Commented out for CCS 2.20 */     FlushCache();     IER = 0;     IFR = 0;     init_emif(); }If you encounter this RTDX error message, modify the OnPreFileLoaded() function as shown here by removing the comment indicator on the GEL_Reset() line to enable the GEL reset.?OnPreFileLoaded() {     GEL_Reset();   /* Un-comment this call */          FlushCache();     IER = 0;     IFR = 0;     init_emif(); }

Summary: All elements of initial guess are modified when some elements violate bounds
Description: When some elements of the user-provided initial guess x0  violate the bounds lb  and ub , all  elements of x0  are modified internally to provide an improved initial guess within the bounds. This applies to the large-scale algorithm in Optimization Toolbox functions lsqlin , quadprog , fmincon , lsqnonlin , and lsqcurvefit .
Workaround: To prevent x0 from being modified, set all elements of x0 to values inside of the bounds prior to calling the optimization function.

Summary: This code now returns a value of 'None': f = figure; get(f,'Renderer')
Description: The following code f = figure;    get(f,'Renderer')  returns 'None'  as the figure renderer. In R14sp2, the same code returned the value of the Renderer property as 'painters' .
Workaround: The problem is caused by the fact that in the following code,f = figure;    get(f,'Renderer')the figure is not visible and has not been painted at the time when the Renderer property is queried. You cannot rely on the value returned by the Renderer property unless the figure is visible and has been painted. Note that the value returned by the Renderer property in the above code might change in future Matlab releases.The following code shows a reliable way to query the current renderer in a newly created figure: Note: the above code sample returns 'None' as the current renderer in both R14sp2 and R14sp3, which is the expected behavior because the figure is empty.f = figure;    drawnow;    get(f,'Renderer')

Summary: Multiple warnings issued for invalid TeX strings in a textbox annotation
Description: When entering malformed TeX strings such as the following into a textbox,test = {'$\hat{q} = 4$'; '$\hat{q} = 4$';};annotation('textbox','String',test,'Interpreter', 'latex','Fontsize',10); multiple errors of the following form are displayed:Warning: Unable to interpret TeX string "\hat{q} = 4$".> In scribe.textbox.methods>strcellsizes at 1342  In scribe.textbox.methods>resizetext at 1114  In scribe.textbox.methods at 20  In scribe.textbox.textbox>changedString at 306  In scribe.textbox.textbox at 160  In annotation at 142  In question at 2 Clicking the Textbox, changing font attributes or editing the text can raise similar errors.
Workaround: You can suppress the warnings by entering the following command at the MATLAB prompt:warning offNote that the warnings are correct; either the string is invalid or the wrong interpreter is being used.

Summary: Printing maps to PostScript yields bitmaps rather than vector graphics
Description: Function axesm  and a few other Mapping Toolbox functions automatically set the figure's Renderer  property to 'zbuffer' . This in turn causes print  to create bitmaps even for maps that contain only vector graphics. Excessively large graphic files and less than optimal resolution can result when printing to PostScript devices.
Workaround: You can manually set the figure's Renderer to 'painters' in order to print vector graphics. For example,set(gcf,'Renderer','painters')before issuing a print or saveas command. Be aware, however, that any objects that have transparency will be rendered as opaque with the painters renderer (only OpenGL can render transparency).?Alternatively, you can set the figure's RendererMode to 'auto':set(gcf,'RendererMode','auto')

Summary: UNIX figure windows do not display special characters correctly
Description: On UNIX platforms, accented characters and other symbols sometimes appear in figure windows as an empty box or other incorrect symbol. These characters and symbols display correctly in the Command Window.
Workaround: This problem happens?when the UNIX environment variable LANG is unset or set to a variable, such as C, whose character set does not include special characters. To work around the problem, set the LANG environment variable in the shell from which you start MATLAB by typingsetenv LANG en_US.ISO8859-1Or set it to a similar value appropriate for your operating system.

Summary: A model containing bus signals and bus capable blocks may?not simulate despite error correction
Description: If a model contains a bus capable block, and the input connections are modified between two simulations, the second simulation may fail with a spurious?error message. The following conditions?must exist for this bug to occur:    The model contains at least one bus capable block (Switch, Multi-Port Switch, Unit Delay, Merge, Memory, Zero Order Hold, Rate Transition or bus-capable S-function).     Configuration Parameters > Diagnostics > Connectivity > Bus Signal treated as Vector?is set to error.     The bus capable block is connected to bus signals or regular signals.     The first simulation errors out with a valid error message regarding invalid bus connections to the bus capable block.  After the model is fixed, the next simulation may nonetheless fail, posting a spurious error message regarding invalid bus connections.
Workaround: If you believe that an error message produced under the described conditions is invalid, try simulating the model again without making any changes. If the simulation succeeds, the previous error message can be ignored.

Summary: Linux: Cannot Select Items in Start Button when Using "Focus Strictly Follows Mouse"
Description: When using the "Focus Strictly Follows Mouse" window focus policy on Linux, you cannot select the Start  button menu items. When you move the mouse over a submenu item, the menu closes. This is a known problem with the Sun Java Virtual Machine (JVM) that MATLAB uses.
Workaround: Use a different window focus policy such as "Click to Focus", "Focus Follows Mouse", or "Focus Under Mouse".

Summary: uigetfile sometimes fails to a open large number of files.
Description: If you attempt to open a large number of files using uigetfile  with MultiSelect  set to on , uigetfile  may return 0 without opening the files.
Workaround: Try executing the following command before calling uigetfile:setappdata(0,'UseNativeSystemDialogs',false)If this does not work, consider whether or not you can structure your request to open fewer files at one time.

Summary: Mouse clicks in Stateflow can behave like shift-click when selecting multiple items
Description: When using a Mac with Java Figures, open a Stateflow model and make the window almost as big as the screen.  Click around in the open window, then click on any of the items in the diagram.  This correctly selects the single item last clicked.    If you then click outside any of the states (such as in the bottom, empty part of the diagram) multiple objects are selected, as if you were holding down the shift key and clicking.
Workaround: This is fixed in OSX 10.4 with the updated Java VM (Virtual Machine).

Summary: Function tightmap can restrict map axes too much
Description: After calling function tightmap , either with no input or with the 'tight'  argument, the axes limits may become so tight that they partially obscure the map frame.
Workaround: To work around this issue, replace the tightmap.m function file in your installation with the version from the attachment, as follows:    Exit MATLAB.    Make a backup copy of the following files.    MATLABROOT\matlab\toolbox\map\mapdisp\tightmap.m    MATLABROOT represents the name of your MATLAB installation directory, as returned by the matlabroot function.    For example, rename the existing file, adding "old" to the file extension: tightmap.m.old        Copy the version of tightmap.m from the attachment into the directory specified in the previous step.    Restart MATLAB.    Run the rehash toolbox command at the MATLAB prompt.Note that the new version of tightmap redefines tightmap('tight') to be 80% (rather than 100%) tighter than tightmap('loose'). This minor change leaves enough space to keep the map frame visible without other noticeable changes in appearance.

Summary: XVID codec is not compatible with aviread
Description: If you attempt to use aviread  on an AVI file that had been compressed using the XVID custom codec, you will see an error such as: ??? Unable to locate decompressor to decompress video stream  The custom compression codec for XVID is not supported by the Video For Windows (VFW) interface, which is used by aviread  on Windows platforms. This is only an issue on Windows systems; aviread  does not support compression on non-Windows platforms.
Workaround: There are no known workarounds with AVI files compressed using the XVID custom codec.If possible, try to resave or convert the original AVI movie without using compression, or resave it using a different compression type that is installed on your system.

Summary: Ctrl+C on ActiveX control in deployed figure window causes hang
Description: Clicking Ctrl+C  to copy text from an ActiveX control in a deployed figure window causes MATLAB to hang.
Workaround: To copy text from an ActiveX control in a deployed figure window, use Ctrl+Insert. For paste, use Shift+Insert.

Summary: A < or?> character in a uicontrol TooltipString property does not display.
Description: If the TooltipString  property of a user interface control (uicontrol) component is set to a string containing the <  (less than)? or > (greater than) character, then the character does not show when the tooltip is displayed.
Workaround: Replace the < character with &lt; > character with &gt. For example, instead ofset(uic_handle,'TooltipString','x < 5 or x > 10')useset(uic_handle,'TooltipString','x &lt 5 or x &gt 10')Note. When this problem is fixed, the use of &lt or &gt?might not continue to work as expected.

Summary: Specifying transparency has no effect for objects in a map axes
Description: Instead of controlling transparency, setting the AlphaData  of a graphic object displayed in a map axes has no effect. This limitation affects all objects that supports transparency, but is most frequently noticed with objects created with patchm , patchesm , surfm , meshm , surfacem , and geoshow . The limitation comes from the fact that axesm  and a few other Mapping Toolbox functions automatically set the figure's Renderer  property to 'zbuffer' , which does not support transparency (only the opengl renderer enables Handle Graphics objects to be transparent).
Workaround: You can either set the figure's Renderer to 'opengl' or its RendererMode to 'auto'. For example,set(gcf,'Renderer','opengl')orset(gcf,'RendererMode','auto')The workarounds sometimes have undesirable side effects. In particular, figures that include patches created with functions patchm or patchesm may exhibit graphic defects arising from limitations in the current implementation of those functions.

Summary: interpm needs to handle longitude wrapping in the 'gc' and 'rh' cases
Description: When using either the 'gc'  or 'rh'  method, interpm  always inserts points with longitudes in the interval [-180 180].  This leads to erratic longitude wrapping when the input line includes longitudes that exceed 180 degrees. This bug can also cause problems in bufferm  because bufferm  calls interpm .
Workaround: To work around this issue, replace the interpm.m function file in your installation with the version in the attachment, as follows:    Exit MATLAB.    Make a backup copy of the following files.    MATLABROOT\matlab\toolbox\map\map\interpm.m    MATLABROOT represents the name of your MATLAB installation directory, as returned by the matlabroot function.    For example, rename the existing file, adding "old" to the file extension: interpm.m.old        Copy the version of interpm.m from the attachment into the directory specified in the previous step.    Restart MATLAB.    Run the rehash toolbox command at the MATLAB prompt.

Summary: Printing to HP LaserJet 1300 or HP color LaserJet 5500?causes exception
Description: When printing to an HP LaserJet 1300 or HP color LaserJet 5500?for the first time during a MATLAB session from various desktop tools (for instance, the Editor/Debugger or Command Window), printing fails and an error message like the following appears in the Command Window: java.lang.ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException: -1at sun.print.Win32PrintService.getMediaTrays(Unknown Source)at sun.print.Win32PrintService.getSupportedAttributeValues(Unknown Source)at sun.print.ServiceDialog$MediaPanel.updateInfo(Unknown Source)at sun.print.ServiceDialog$PageSetupPanel.updateInfo(Unknown Source)at sun.print.ServiceDialog.updatePanels(Unknown Source) This is due to a bug in the Sun JVM that MATLAB uses.
Workaround: Print again because the problem only occurs the first time you print to the LaserJet during the session.Alternatively, select a different printer.

Summary: The Visible property of uicontextmenu may not work correctly.
Description: If you set the Visible  property of a context menu (uicontextmenu ) to on  programmatically, the context menu may not display. Also, if you get the value of the Visible  property of the context menu, the returned value may be off  even if the context menu is visible on the screen.
Workaround: Introduce a drawnow command after the creation of the context menu and before setting its Visible property to on.

Summary: Simulink Scope Block Not Refreshing Properly
Description: Minimizing the Scope window before each sample run sometimes causes the scope to display multiple traces rather than clearing.?? ?
Workaround: To workaround this problem,?disable the BackingStore property.?shh = get(0,'ShowHiddenHandles');set(0,'ShowHiddenHandles','On')hscope = findobj('Type','Figure','Tag','SIMULINK_SIMSCOPE_FIGURE');set(hscope,'BackingStore','Off');set(0,'ShowHiddenHandles',shh);

Summary: The simplex algorithm in LINPROG errors even though an optimal solution was found.
Description: LINPROG errors out in the postsolve stage after effectively finding the optimal solution in Phase 2 of the simplex algorithm. Iterative display shows that a solution was found but postsolve reports the error message "improper assignment with rectangular empty matrix".??

Summary: VER command not available in compiled applications
Description: In compiled applications prior to R2007b, MATLAB's VER command fails. Instead of version information, it returns an empty structure matrix. For example:>> verans = 0x0 struct array with fields: ???? Name ???? Version ???? Release ???? Date? This bug affects the ability of certain functions in the Wavelet Toolbox (WAVEDEC) and?Report Generator to be compiled successfully?. Applications that use these functions will fail at runtime, most likely with an error similar to this one:??? Index exceeds matrix dimensions.

Summary: How do I keep the DOS window from appearing when launching a MATLAB stand-alone program?
Description: The standalone applications created using MATLAB Compiler always bring up a DOS window. Is there a way to suppress this DOS window?
Workaround: Currently, you can only create console applications using MATLAB Compiler.?Windows applications are under consideration?for a future release.?The following workaround?suppresses the DOS window from appearing for a console application:1. Create a batchfile (for example, start.bat) containing one line that launches your program (for example, myprog.exe)2. Create a shortcut to start.bat3. Set Run: Minimized in the Properties\Shortcut pane of the shortcut.?Alternately, add the following set statement to your options file before you compile, based on the compiler you are using.?Without the addition of this line, the system defaults to building a console application.?Note: Edit your options file by typing the evaluating the following sequence of MATLAB commands:cd(prefdir)edit compopts.batIf you have chosen the LCC compiler at 'mbuild -setup':?set LINKFLAGS=%LINKFLAGS% -subsystem windowsIf you have chosen the Microsoft Visual C/C++ compiler at 'mbuild -setup':?set LINKFLAGS=%LINKFLAGS% /SUBSYSTEM:WINDOWS /ENTRY:mainCRTStartupIf you have chosen the Borland compiler at 'mbuild -setup':?set LINKFLAGS=%LINKFLAGS% -aa??Note: The above steps may cause undesirable or application-fatal behavior if your application generates any output that would otherwise be displayed in the console window.??This solution has not been fully tested by the MathWorks and is not officially supported.

Summary: Code generation warning and error messages routed to FDATool status bar
Description: The Filter Design HDL Coder GUI currently displays warning and error  messages on the FDATool status bar. This can make it difficult to monitor  code generation progress, particularly if another window is blocking the  FDATool window.
Workaround: If you suspect a problem with your code generation, you  can work around this display issue by exporting your filter as an object  to the MATLAB workspace and then using the Filter Design HDL Coder  command line  interface to generate the VHDL code.?To export a filter as an object to your workspace,        In FDATool, click File->Export. An Export dialog appears.            Set Export To to Workspace.            Set Export As to Objects.            Specify a name for the quantized filter object in the Quantized Filter text box. The default is Hq.            Click Apply and OK.    ?You can then specify the variable with the generatehdl and  generatetb functions.

Summary: Serial hardware flow control requires attached instrument
Description: Using hardware flow control for the serial port object can cause write operations such as fprintf  and fwrite  to freeze MATLAB if the serial port is not connected to a device with hardware flow control enabled. The following code demonstrates the problem: ? s=serial('com1');%% Remove instrument from COM1fopen(s)s.flowcontrol = 'hardware';fprintf(s,'hello')  % Never returns. ?
Workaround: To prevent MATLAB from freezing, set FlowControl to 'hardware' only while a serial device is attached to an instrument with hardware flow control enabled.

Summary: imhist does not work properly in a GUI created by GUIDE
Description: When imhist  is called repeatedly from a callback function within a GUI created by GUIDE, the contents of the GUI are deleted and a new figure window is created.
Workaround: To workaround this issue, replace the version of the imhist function in your current MATLAB installation with the updated version attached.    Exit MATLAB.    Make a backup copy of the following file.    matlabroot\toolbox\images\images\imhist.m    matlabroot represents the name of your MATLAB installation directory, as returned by the matlabroot function. For example, rename the existing file, adding "old" to the file extension, imhist.m.OLD.    Download the attached file and store it in the directory specified in the previous step.    Restart MATLAB.    Run the rehash toolbox command at the MATLAB prompt.

Summary: Windows XP and Vista ClearType Fonts Mode Not Supported
Description: On Windows XP and Vista platforms, MATLAB does not support ClearType fonts mode. This is a limitation of the JVM version MATLAB uses.
Workaround: In R14SP3 through R2007a, you can set the desktop code and text fonts to be antialiased using Preferences -> Fonts. While this does not render the text with ClearType, it smooths the text, providing a similar effect.

Summary: Linux non-US keyboard: cannot type characters such as caret (^) and tilde (~)
Description: If you use a non-US keyboard on Linux platforms, MATLAB is affected by a known Java VM problem that prevents the use of any dead key + space bar combination. In Release 13, this key sequence causes MATLAB to hang. In Release 14, MATLAB does not hang but no character appears. For many non-US keyboards, you use a dead key + space bar combination to create the caret (^) and the tilde (~) characters.
Workaround: Following are some workarounds you can use:Use another applicationType the character in another application, and then cut and paste it into MATLAB.Remap your keysUse a different key combination to enter the desired character. This remaps the key for all applications for the entire session, not just for MATLAB. The change only works for the current session, so you have to remap the key again after you log off and log back on.Follow these steps to remap a key using the command xmodmap:    Determine the key you want to use as a substitute for the dead key. For example, you could remap the F11 key to type a caret (^).    Use xmodmap to see what your current keymap looks like:    xmodmap -pke | more    This displays a table of values and a description of the behavior for each key. Note the line that contains the key you want to remap. As an example, for a Finnish Keyboard, the line might be:    95    0xffc8  (F11)        Find the decimal representation of the ASCII key that you want to map. Most Linux systems display a map when you run this command:    man ascii    The output includes the following:    The Ascii value of the caret (^) character is decimal 94.  The Ascii value of the tilde (~) character is decimal 126.        Remap the key using xmodmap. For example, to set the F11 key to input a caret (^) character, type:    xmodmap -e "keycode 95 = 94"    As another example, if you want the Shift + F11 key combination to input the tilde (~) character, type the following:    xmodmap -e "keycode 95 = 0xffc8 94"        Test the new key binding.Run without JavaIn R14, the issue is related to the use of Java, so starting MATLAB using the -nodesktop option allows you to use dead keys in the Commmand Window. However, you will lose MATLAB desktop functionality. To start MATLAB this way, at the system prompt, type matlab -nodesktop. You can still use the MATLAB Editor/Debugger, but it will still have the problem with the dead keys.Use functions insteadReplace a character with its functional equivalent, if possible. For example, use the power function instead of the caret (^) key, and use the not function instead of the tilde (~) key.Use a different editorFor editing files, use an editor other than the one provided with MATLAB. Select File -> Preferences -> Editor/Debugger and specify a different text editor.Disable dead keysYou can disable dead key sequences. This prevents you from using the characters that correspond to these sequences. It does not solve the problem, but prevents MATLAB from hanging in Release 13. There are two ways to disable the dead keys: using setxkbmap or dpkg-reconfigure.Use setxkbmap    Before starting MATLAB, from the shell prompt type    setxkbmap -variant nodeadkeys        After exiting MATLAB, from the shell prompt type    setxkbmap -variant basic    Use dpkg-reconfigure    Type    dpkg-reconfigure xserver-xfree86        When the system prompts you to select the keyboard variant, choose nodeadkeys.
